books.title: BOOK THREE: AGENTS OF THE FRAYING DARK

chapters.title: Chapter 16: Class Adaptation Guide

sections.title: The Envoy (Adapted from Bard)
sections.content:
The Envoy is a master of diplomacy, espionage, and manipulation. They are the face of the crew, navigating the treacherous political waters of Nexus 9.
*   **Domains:** Grace & Codex
*   **Evasion:** 10 | **HP:** 5
*   **Hope Feature (Silver Tongue):** Spend 1 Hope to gain Advantage on any Presence roll during a negotiation.
*   **Class Feature (Web of Contacts):** Once per session, you can declare that you know an NPC in your current location who owes you a favor.
*   **Subclasses:**
    *   *Ambassador:* Focuses on de-escalation and building alliances.
    *   *Infiltrator:* Focuses on deception, disguise, and extracting secrets.
*   **Starship Role:** Communications Officer (Jamming, Intercepting Transmissions).
*   **Diplomacy/Investigation Role:** Primary negotiator; reading motives and uncovering political leverage.

sections.title: The Operative (Adapted from Rogue)
sections.content:
The Operative is a specialist in stealth, sabotage, and targeted elimination. They operate in the shadows, dealing with problems before they become public.
*   **Domains:** Midnight & Grace
*   **Evasion:** 12 | **HP:** 6
*   **Hope Feature (Vanish):** Spend 1 Hope to immediately enter stealth, even if you are being observed.
*   **Class Feature (Sneak Attack):** If you attack an enemy who is unaware of your presence, you deal an additional 1d8 damage.
*   **Subclasses:**
    *   *Phantom:* Focuses on absolute stealth and bypassing security systems.
    *   *Saboteur:* Focuses on explosives, traps, and disabling enemy technology.
*   **Starship Role:** Electronic Warfare (Hacking enemy systems, spoofing sensors).
*   **Diplomacy/Investigation Role:** Breaking and entering; acquiring physical evidence; intimidation.

sections.title: The Pilot (Adapted from Ranger)
sections.content:
The Pilot is an ace flyer and navigator, most comfortable behind the stick of a starship or a support vehicle.
*   **Domains:** Bone & Sage
*   **Evasion:** 12 | **HP:** 6
*   **Hope Feature (Evasive Action):** Spend 1 Hope to force an enemy to reroll a successful attack against you or your vehicle.
*   **Class Feature (Ace in the Hole):** You gain Advantage on all Agility rolls related to piloting or driving.
*   **Subclasses:**
    *   *Ace:* Focuses on dogfighting and pushing vehicles beyond their limits.
    *   *Navigator:* Focuses on hyperspace calculation and surviving environmental hazards.
*   **Starship Role:** Helm (Evasive Maneuvers, Pursuit).
*   **Diplomacy/Investigation Role:** Extraction planning; analyzing flight logs and navigation data.

sections.title: The Marine (Adapted from Warrior)
sections.content:
The Marine is a shock trooper, trained for zero-G boarding actions and heavy firefights. They are the crew's primary physical deterrent.
*   **Domains:** Blade & Bone
*   **Evasion:** 11 | **HP:** 6
*   **Hope Feature (Adrenaline Surge):** Spend 1 Hope to immediately clear 2 Stress or heal 1d4 HP.
*   **Class Feature (Combat Veteran):** You ignore the "Pinned Down" condition from suppressive fire.
*   **Subclasses:**
    *   *Shock Trooper:* Focuses on close-quarters combat and breaching tactics.
    *   *Heavy Gunner:* Focuses on suppressive fire and area-of-effect weapons.
*   **Starship Role:** Tactical Officer (Firing weapons, Target Lock).
*   **Diplomacy/Investigation Role:** Physical intimidation; analyzing combat scenes and ballistics.

sections.title: The Engineer (Adapted from Guardian)
sections.content:
The Engineer is a technical savant, capable of keeping a crippled ship flying or turning scavenged parts into a deadly weapon.
*   **Domains:** Valor & Blade
*   **Evasion:** 9 | **HP:** 7
*   **Hope Feature (Jury-Rig):** Spend 1 Hope to temporarily repair a broken piece of equipment or bypass a locked door.
*   **Class Feature (Shield-Bearer):** You can use your action to grant an ally in cover an additional +2 to their Evasion.
*   **Subclasses:**
    *   *Tech-Savant:* Focuses on hacking, drones, and modifying weapons.
    *   *Shield-Bearer:* Focuses on personal armor and protecting allies.
*   **Starship Role:** Chief Engineer (Rerouting power, Emergency Repairs).
*   **Diplomacy/Investigation Role:** Analyzing technology; recovering corrupted data; bypassing security.

sections.title: The Mystic (Adapted from Sorcerer)
sections.content:
The Mystic is a telepath or a spiritual adept, capable of manipulating the minds of others or sensing the metaphysical currents of the universe.
*   **Domains:** Arcana & Midnight
*   **Evasion:** 10 | **HP:** 6
*   **Hope Feature (Mind Trick):** Spend 1 Hope to plant a brief, harmless suggestion in an NPC's mind.
*   **Class Feature (Psychic Resonance):** You can communicate telepathically with any willing ally within line of sight.
*   **Subclasses:**
    *   *Seer:* Focuses on precognition, sensing danger, and reading emotional residue.
    *   *Channeler:* Focuses on telekinetic force and offensive psychic attacks.
*   **Starship Role:** Sensor Operator (Sensor Sweeps, detecting cloaked ships).
*   **Diplomacy/Investigation Role:** Reading surface thoughts; detecting lies; sensing emotional imprints.

sections.title: The Broker (Adapted from Druid)
sections.content:
The Broker is a fixer, a smuggler, and a master of logistics. They know how to acquire rare goods and move them without attracting attention.
*   **Domains:** Sage & Arcana
*   **Evasion:** 10 | **HP:** 6
*   **Hope Feature (Hidden Cache):** Spend 1 Hope to produce a common item (e.g., a med-kit, a tool, a bribe) that you "happened" to have on you.
*   **Class Feature (Black Market Ties):** You can always find a buyer or a seller for illegal goods, regardless of your location.
*   **Subclasses:**
    *   *Fixer:* Focuses on acquiring information, forging documents, and bribery.
    *   *Smuggler:* Focuses on hiding contraband and bypassing customs inspections.
*   **Starship Role:** Quartermaster (Managing cargo, optimizing fuel consumption).
*   **Diplomacy/Investigation Role:** Following the money; identifying black-market origins; bribery.

sections.title: The Medic (Adapted from Seraph)
sections.content:
The Medic is a trauma surgeon and xenobiologist, essential for keeping the crew alive in a hostile galaxy.
*   **Domains:** Splendor & Valor
*   **Evasion:** 9 | **HP:** 7
*   **Hope Feature (Combat Triage):** Spend 1 Hope to heal an ally for 1d8 HP as a free action.
*   **Class Feature (Xenobiology):** You gain Advantage on rolls to identify alien toxins, diseases, or biological weaknesses.
*   **Subclasses:**
    *   *Trauma Surgeon:* Focuses on rapid healing and stabilizing critically injured allies.
    *   *Xenobiologist:* Focuses on analyzing alien lifeforms and synthesizing antidotes.
*   **Starship Role:** Chief Medical Officer (Treating injuries during combat, managing life support).
*   **Diplomacy/Investigation Role:** Autopsies; identifying toxins; analyzing biological evidence.

---

chapters.content: *Nexus 9* features eight distinct class archetypes, adapted from the Daggerheart core rules to fit the cosmic opera setting. Each class has a specific role in combat, diplomacy, and starship operations.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The Advancement System governs how characters grow from Level 1 to Level 10 across four tiers of play. This chapter covers the leveling structure, the level-up process (tier achievements, advancements, damage thresholds, and domain cards), class progression overviews, subclass features, multiclassing rules, and the experience and proficiency systems.

| Tier | Levels | Scale of Play |
| :--- | :---: | :--- |
| **Tier 1: Local** | 1–2 | Station-level problems, personal stakes, crew bonding |
| **Tier 2: Regional** | 3–4 | Sector-wide intrigue, faction entanglements, first ship upgrades |
| **Tier 3: Galactic** | 5–7 | System-wide crises, ancient mysteries, major faction conflicts |
| **Tier 4: Cosmic** | 8–10 | Existential threats, final confrontations, legacy-defining choices |

At each level, characters gain new domain cards, improve their damage thresholds, and unlock subclass features at key milestones (Foundation at Level 1, Specialization at Level 3, and Mastery at Level 7).

chapters.title: 1. The Leveling Structure
chapters.content:
In *Nexus 9*, characters advance from Level 1 to Level 10. These levels are grouped into four **Tiers** of play, representing the scale of the threats the crew faces and the resources at their disposal.

*   **Tier 1 (Level 1):** Local operators, station security, freelance couriers.
*   **Tier 2 (Levels 2-4):** Recognized professionals, sector-wide reputation, access to military-grade gear.
*   **Tier 3 (Levels 5-7):** Elite operatives, faction leaders, commanders of capital ships.
*   **Tier 4 (Levels 8-10):** Galactic legends, capable of altering the balance of power across the Nexus.

The GM decides when the party levels up based on narrative milestones (typically every 3-4 sessions). All party members level up at the same time.

---

chapters.title: 2. The Level-Up Process

sections.title: Step 1: Tier Achievements
sections.content:
If you are transitioning to a new Tier (reaching Level 2, Level 5, or Level 8), you gain specific automatic bonuses:
*   **Level 2:** Gain a new Experience at +2, and permanently increase your Proficiency by 1.
*   **Level 5:** Gain a new Experience at +2, permanently increase your Proficiency by 1, and clear any marked traits.
*   **Level 8:** Gain a new Experience at +2, permanently increase your Proficiency by 1, and clear any marked traits.

sections.title: Step 2: Advancements
sections.content:
Choose **two** advancements from the list below. You can only choose advancements from your current Tier or below. You must mark an open slot next to the advancement on your character sheet.

**Available Advancements:**
*   **Increase Traits:** Choose two unmarked character traits (Agility, Strength, Finesse, Instinct, Presence, Knowledge) and gain a permanent +1 bonus to them. Mark them; you cannot increase them again until the next Tier clears the marks.
*   **Add Hit Point Slot:** Permanently add 1 Hit Point slot (maximum 12).
*   **Add Stress Slot:** Permanently add 1 Stress slot (maximum 12).
*   **Increase Experience:** Choose two of your Experiences and gain a permanent +1 bonus to both.
*   **New Domain Card:** Acquire an additional Domain Card at or below your current level from one of your class's domains.
*   **Increase Evasion:** Gain a permanent +1 bonus to your Evasion.
*   **Upgraded Subclass Feature:** Unlock the next feature for your subclass (Foundation → Specialization → Mastery).
*   **Increase Proficiency (Costs 2 Advancements):** Increase your Proficiency by 1, allowing you to roll an additional damage die on attacks.
*   **Multiclass (Costs 2 Advancements, Level 5+ only):** Choose an additional class. Gain its core class feature and access to one of its domains. Take a Foundation card from one of its subclasses.

sections.title: Step 3: Damage Thresholds
sections.content: Increase your Minor, Major, and Severe damage thresholds by 1. (This reflects your growing resilience and is already calculated into the Tier tables in the Combat Math document).

sections.title: Step 4: Domain Cards
sections.content:
Regardless of the advancements chosen in Step 2, you automatically acquire **one new Domain Card** at your level or lower from one of your class's domains. You may also exchange one previously acquired Domain Card for a different one of the same level or lower.

---

chapters.content: When the GM announces a level-up, every player completes the following four steps:

chapters.title: 3. Class Progression Paths

sections.title: Starting Stats (Level 1)
sections.content:
*   **Proficiency:** 1 (Roll 1 damage die)
*   **Hope:** 2
*   **Stress Slots:** 6
*   **Domain Cards:** 2 (One from each of your class's domains)
*   **Loadout Maximum:** 5 cards
*   **HP and Evasion:** See the Class Damage Thresholds table in the Combat Math document.

chapters.content: Because *Nexus 9* uses a unified advancement system, all 8 classes follow the exact same progression cadence. The differences between classes are defined by their starting stats, their unique Class Features, their Subclass Features, and the Domain Cards they select.

chapters.title: 4. Subclass Features

sections.title: The Marine (Warrior)
sections.content:
*The frontline combatant, specializing in heavy weapons and breaching tactics.*

**Subclass 1: Shock Trooper**
*   **Foundation (Breach & Clear):** When you move into Melee range of an enemy, your first attack against them deals an additional 1d6 kinetic damage.
*   **Specialization (Unstoppable Momentum):** When you reduce an enemy to 0 HP, you can immediately move up to Close range without triggering reactions.
*   **Mastery (Juggernaut):** Once per Long Rest, you can spend 3 Hope to ignore all damage from a single attack, regardless of its severity.

**Subclass 2: Heavy Gunner**
*   **Foundation (Suppressive Fire):** When you attack with a two-handed ranged weapon, you can mark 1 Stress to force the target and all enemies within Very Close range of them to roll their next attack with Disadvantage.
*   **Specialization (Overcharge):** When you roll maximum damage on any damage die with a ranged weapon, you can reroll that die and add the new result to the total.
*   **Mastery (Walking Artillery):** You can wield two-handed heavy weapons with one hand, allowing you to carry a shield or a secondary weapon without penalty.

sections.title: The Engineer (Guardian)
sections.content:
*The protector and technical savant, keeping the crew and the ship in one piece.*

**Subclass 1: Void Warden**
*   **Foundation (Defensive Grid):** You can project a localized energy shield. Allies within Melee range of you gain +1 to their Armor Score.
*   **Specialization (Reactive Shielding):** When an ally within Close range takes damage, you can mark 1 Stress to reduce that damage by your Armor Score.
*   **Mastery (Aegis Protocol):** Once per Long Rest, you can spend 4 Hope to make yourself and all allies within Close range immune to all damage for 1 round.

**Subclass 2: Combat Technician**
*   **Foundation (Jury-Rig):** During a Short Rest, you can repair one broken piece of equipment or restore 1 Armor Slot to an ally for free.
*   **Specialization (Drone Swarm):** You deploy a permanent companion drone. It can take the Help action once per round without consuming your action economy.
*   **Mastery (System Overload):** You can spend 2 Hope to force any technological device, weapon, or vehicle within Far range to immediately shut down or malfunction for 1d4 rounds.

sections.title: The Medic (Seraph)
sections.content:
*The trauma surgeon and xenobiologist, essential for survival in deep space.*

**Subclass 1: Combat Surgeon**
*   **Foundation (Triage):** When you heal an ally who is at 0 HP, they clear an additional 1d6 HP.
*   **Specialization (Stim-Pack):** When you heal an ally, you can mark 1 Stress to also grant them Advantage on their next action roll.
*   **Mastery (Miracle Worker):** Once per Long Rest, you can spend 5 Hope to completely restore an ally to full HP and clear all their Stress, even if they have just died (within the last minute).

**Subclass 2: Xenobiologist**
*   **Foundation (Anatomical Insight):** You gain Advantage on all Knowledge rolls related to alien biology, toxins, and diseases.
*   **Specialization (Targeted Strike):** When you attack an organic enemy, you can mark 1 Stress to ignore their Armor Score by striking a vital nerve cluster or organ.
*   **Mastery (Bio-Manipulation):** You can spend 3 Hope to alter the biology of a willing ally for the scene, granting them immunity to a specific damage type (e.g., acid, radiation, poison).

sections.title: The Operative (Rogue)
sections.content:
*The stealth specialist, assassin, and infiltrator.*

**Subclass 1: Ghost**
*   **Foundation (Active Camouflage):** When you are in dim light or darkness, you can spend 1 Hope to become completely invisible until you attack or take damage.
*   **Specialization (Silent Takedown):** When you attack an unaware target from Melee range, you deal an additional 2d6 damage.
*   **Mastery (Phase Walk):** You can teleport between areas of shadow or darkness up to Far range as a Free Action once per turn.

**Subclass 2: Saboteur**
*   **Foundation (Data Spike):** You gain Advantage on all Finesse rolls to hack terminals, bypass security doors, or disable alarms.
*   **Specialization (Rigged to Blow):** You can turn any small electronic device into an explosive. It deals 3d6 energy damage to anyone within Very Close range when triggered.
*   **Mastery (Systemic Collapse):** Once per Long Rest, you can spend 4 Hope to completely disable the security grid, lighting, and automated defenses of an entire facility or ship for the duration of the scene.

sections.title: The Pilot (Ranger)
sections.content:
*The ace flyer, vehicle specialist, and skirmisher.*

**Subclass 1: Ace**
*   **Foundation (Evasive Maneuvers):** When you are piloting a vehicle or ship, its Evasion increases by +2.
*   **Specialization (Target Lock):** When you attack with a vehicle weapon, you can mark 1 Stress to gain Advantage on the roll.
*   **Mastery (The Needle Threader):** You can navigate your ship or vehicle through impossible hazards (asteroid fields, closing blast doors) without needing to make a roll.

**Subclass 2: Frontier Scout**
*   **Foundation (Survivalist):** You gain Advantage on all Instinct rolls to track targets, find safe paths, or survive in hostile environments.
*   **Specialization (Ambush Predator):** In the first round of combat, your movement speed is doubled, and your first attack deals an additional 1d8 damage.
*   **Mastery (Unseen Observer):** You can spend 2 Hope to perfectly blend into any natural or urban environment, becoming undetectable by sight, sound, or thermal sensors as long as you remain motionless.

sections.title: The Broker (Druid)
sections.content:
*The fixer, smuggler, and master of logistics and black-market connections.*

**Subclass 1: Smuggler**
*   **Foundation (Hidden Compartments):** You can conceal up to 3 small items on your person or in your ship that cannot be found by standard security scans or physical searches.
*   **Specialization (The Right Price):** You can spend 1 Hope to automatically succeed on a Persuasion roll to bribe a guard, official, or informant.
*   **Mastery (Untraceable):** Your ship and your personal comms cannot be tracked, intercepted, or recorded by any faction, regardless of their technological superiority.

**Subclass 2: Information Broker**
*   **Foundation (A Guy Who Knows A Guy):** Once per session, you can declare that you have a contact in your current location who owes you a favor.
*   **Specialization (Blackmail):** When you interact with a named NPC, you can mark 2 Stress to reveal a piece of damaging information you hold over them, forcing them to comply with a reasonable request.
*   **Mastery (The Spider's Web):** You can spend 3 Hope to instantly know the location, current status, and immediate plans of any single individual in the galaxy, provided you have met them before.

sections.title: The Mystic (Sorcerer)
sections.content:
*The telepath, technomancer, and manipulator of metaphysical forces.*

**Subclass 1: Telepath**
*   **Foundation (Mind Reading):** You can spend 1 Hope to read the surface thoughts of a target within Close range for one minute.
*   **Specialization (Psychic Assault):** When you deal energy damage with a Domain Card, you can mark 1 Stress to change the damage type to psychic and force the target to mark 1 Stress.
*   **Mastery (Mind Control):** Once per Long Rest, you can spend 5 Hope to take complete control of an NPC's actions for the duration of the scene.

**Subclass 2: Technomancer**
*   **Foundation (Machine Whisperer):** You can interface with computers and droids using your mind, requiring no physical connection or terminal.
*   **Specialization (Construct Override):** You can spend 2 Hope to take control of an enemy drone, turret, or synthetic for 1d4 rounds.
*   **Mastery (Ghost in the Machine):** You can permanently transfer your consciousness into a ship's mainframe or a secure network, allowing you to operate its systems as if they were your own body.

sections.title: The Envoy (Bard)
sections.content:
*The diplomat, politician, and face of the crew.*

**Subclass 1: Ambassador**
*   **Foundation (Silver Tongue):** You gain Advantage on all Presence rolls made to negotiate, de-escalate conflict, or forge alliances.
*   **Specialization (Diplomatic Weight):** When you or an ally within Close range fails a social roll, you can mark 1 Stress to reroll the dice.
*   **Mastery (Galactic Treaty):** Once per Long Rest, you can spend 4 Hope to force two warring factions or hostile groups in your presence to immediately cease hostilities and agree to a temporary truce.

**Subclass 2: Provocateur**
*   **Foundation (Sow Discord):** You can spend 1 Hope to plant a rumor or lie that will be believed by the general public within a specific sector or station.
*   **Specialization (Incite Rebellion):** You can mark 2 Stress to turn a crowd of civilians or low-level guards against their superiors for the duration of the scene.
*   **Mastery (Character Assassination):** You can spend 3 Hope to completely destroy the reputation of a named NPC, stripping them of their rank, resources, and allies within their faction.

chapters.content: Every class in *Nexus 9* has two distinct subclasses. When a character is created at Level 1, they choose one subclass and gain its **Foundation Feature**. As they level up and spend Advancements, they can unlock the **Specialization Feature** and eventually the **Mastery Feature**.

chapters.title: 5. Multiclassing in Nexus 9

sections.title: How to Multiclass
sections.content:
To multiclass, you must spend **two** of your available advancements during a level-up. When you do, you gain the following benefits:

1.  **Choose a Second Class:** Select any of the other 7 classes in *Nexus 9*.
2.  **Gain the Core Class Feature:** You immediately gain the primary, defining feature of that class (e.g., the Marine's Combat Superiority, the Operative's Sneak Attack).
3.  **Access a New Domain:** Choose **one** of the two Domains associated with your new class. You can now acquire Domain Cards from this new Domain when you level up.
4.  **Gain a Foundation Card:** Choose one of the two subclasses from your new class. You immediately gain its Foundation Feature card and add it to your active Loadout (this does not count against your Loadout limit).

sections.title: Restrictions
sections.content:
*   **Domain Card Levels:** When you acquire a Domain Card from your multiclass Domain, you can only choose cards at or below **half your current level** (rounded up). For example, a Level 5 character who multiclasses can only choose Level 1, 2, or 3 cards from their new Domain.
*   **Subclass Lockout:** Once you multiclass, you can no longer take the "Upgraded Subclass Feature" advancement for your original class. You are locked out of your original class's Specialization and Mastery features.
*   **One Multiclass Only:** You can only multiclass once. You cannot take this advancement a second time to gain a third class.

---

chapters.content: Starting at Level 5 (Tier 3), characters have the option to broaden their skill set by taking the **Multiclass** advancement. This represents a character learning new disciplines from their crewmates or adapting to the harsh realities of the galaxy.

sections.title: Experience
sections.content:
In *Nexus 9*, "Experiences" are short, descriptive phrases that represent your character's background, training, or past traumas (e.g., "Veteran of the Rim War," "Former Syndicate Smuggler," "Trained Xenobiologist").

When you make an action roll where your Experience applies, you can spend 1 Hope to add the Experience's numerical value to your roll.

*   At Level 1, you start with two Experiences at +2 and one at +1.
*   At Levels 2, 5, and 8, you gain a new Experience at +2.
*   You can use the "Increase Experience" advancement to permanently add +1 to two of your existing Experiences.

sections.title: Proficiency
sections.content:
Proficiency represents your raw combat effectiveness and familiarity with weapons. It dictates **how many damage dice you roll** when you successfully hit an enemy.

*   At Level 1, your Proficiency is 1 (you roll 1 damage die).
*   At Levels 2, 5, and 8, your Proficiency automatically increases by 1.
*   You can use the "Increase Proficiency" advancement (which costs two advancement slots) to increase it further.
*   Proficiency only multiplies the *dice* rolled, not the flat modifiers. For example, if your weapon deals d8+2 damage and your Proficiency is 3, you roll 3d8+2 damage.

---

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
The Domain Library is the complete reference for all domain cards available in *Nexus 9*. The 9 domains — Arcana, Blade, Bone, Codex, Grace, Midnight, Sage, Splendor, and Valor — each contain 21 cards spanning Levels 1 through 10. Each class draws from two domains, and players build their Loadout by selecting cards from their class's domain pools during character creation and at each level-up.

| Domain | Theme | Shared By |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Arcana** | Telepathy & Technomancy | Mystic, Broker |
| **Blade** | Close Quarters Combat | Marine, Engineer |
| **Bone** | Tactics & Evasion | Pilot, Marine |
| **Codex** | Law, Lore & Bureaucracy | Envoy, Mystic |
| **Grace** | Diplomacy & Deception | Envoy, Operative |
| **Midnight** | Stealth & Sabotage | Operative, Mystic |
| **Sage** | Survival & Logistics | Pilot, Broker |
| **Splendor** | Medicine & Morale | Medic, Envoy |
| **Valor** | Protection & Heavy Weapons | Engineer, Medic |

The chapters that follow detail each class's domain card pools, the card rules framework (Vault, Loadout, card types, and costs), and the cross-class domain reference.

chapters.title: 1. The Domain System

sections.title: The 9 Domains of Nexus 9
sections.content:
1. **Arcana (Telepathy & Technomancy):** Mind manipulation, psychic force, and direct neural interfacing.
2. **Blade (Close Quarters Combat):** Melee superiority, breaching tactics, and physical resilience.
3. **Bone (Tactics & Evasion):** Battlefield positioning, avoiding damage, and exploiting enemy weaknesses.
4. **Codex (Law, Lore & Bureaucracy):** Exploiting rules, recalling vital information, and manipulating systems.
5. **Grace (Diplomacy & Deception):** Persuasion, misdirection, and social engineering.
6. **Midnight (Stealth & Sabotage):** Infiltration, assassination, and electronic warfare.
7. **Sage (Survival & Logistics):** Environmental adaptation, scavenging, and black-market connections.
8. **Splendor (Medicine & Morale):** Healing, stabilizing allies, and inspiring leadership.
9. **Valor (Protection & Heavy Weapons):** Absorbing damage, suppressive fire, and holding the line.

---

chapters.content:
In *Nexus 9*, a character's special abilities, tactical maneuvers, and unique tech are represented by **Domain Cards**. Each of the 8 classes draws from two specific Domains.

As characters level up, they add new cards to their **Vault**. During a Long Rest, a character can prepare a specific number of cards from their Vault into their active **Loadout**. Only cards in the active Loadout can be used during a session.

chapters.title: The Envoy
chapters.content:
*“A war is just a failure of imagination. Give me ten minutes in a room with their general, and I’ll give you a peace treaty. Give me twenty, and I’ll give you their fleet.”*

The Envoy is a master of diplomacy, espionage, and manipulation. They are the face of the crew, navigating the treacherous political waters of Nexus 9 and the wider galaxy. In a universe where a single misunderstood gesture can ignite a sector-wide conflict, the Envoy is the line between fragile peace and total annihilation. They do not win battles by outgunning the enemy; they win by ensuring the battle never happens, or by making sure the enemy is fighting themselves by the time the shooting starts.

Envoys come from all walks of life. Some are formally trained diplomats from the Aurelian Empire or the Terran Coalition, carrying the weight of billions of lives in their briefcases. Others are corporate negotiators, independent mediators, or even high-end grifters who have realized that governments pay better than marks. What unites them is an unparalleled understanding of people—what they want, what they fear, and what they will do to get it.

sections.title: Playing an Envoy
sections.content:
**Your Role:** Face / Social Specialist
**Domains:** Codex, Grace
**Base HP:** 5
**Base Evasion:** 10

**Hope Feature: Silver Tongue**
Spend 1 Hope to gain Advantage on any Presence roll during a negotiation, interrogation, or high-stakes social encounter.

**Class Feature: Web of Contacts**
Once per session, you can declare that you know an NPC in your current location who owes you a favor. This contact will provide information, minor resources, or temporary shelter, but will not risk their life or career without significant persuasion or payment.

### The Envoy in the Galaxy

On Nexus 9, Envoys are the most dangerous people in the room. A Marine can kill a squad; an Envoy can start a war that kills millions. They are constantly playing three-dimensional chess, balancing the demands of their home faction, the needs of their crew, and the overarching stability of the station.

**In Starship Combat:** Communications Officer. You are responsible for jamming enemy sensors, intercepting transmissions, and broadcasting demoralizing propaganda or false surrender codes.
**In Diplomacy:** The Primary Negotiator. You read motives, uncover political leverage, and dictate the terms of engagement.

sections.title: Envoy Progression Table
sections.content:
| Level | Tier | HP | Evasion | Proficiency | Features Gained |
|-------|------|----|---------|-------------|-----------------|
| 1 | 1 | 5 | 10 | 1 | Choose Subclass Foundation, 1 Domain Card |
| 2 | 1 | 6 | 10 | 1 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 3 | 1 | 7 | 11 | 2 | 1 Domain Card |
| 4 | 2 | 8 | 11 | 2 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 5 | 2 | 9 | 12 | 3 | Choose Subclass Specialization, 1 Domain Card |
| 6 | 2 | 10 | 12 | 3 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 7 | 3 | 11 | 13 | 4 | 1 Domain Card |
| 8 | 3 | 12 | 13 | 4 | Choose Subclass Mastery, 1 Domain Card |
| 9 | 4 | 13 | 14 | 5 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 10 | 4 | 14 | 14 | 5 | Epic Boon, 1 Domain Card |

sections.title: Subclasses
sections.content:
### The Ambassador
The Ambassador focuses on de-escalation, building alliances, and finding common ground. They are the stabilizing force in a chaotic galaxy, using their authority and empathy to prevent bloodshed.

**Foundation: Silver Tongue**
You gain Advantage on all Presence rolls made to negotiate, de-escalate conflict, or forge alliances. When you succeed with Hope on a negotiation roll, you also learn one of the target's hidden motives or fears.

**Specialization: Diplomatic Weight**
When you or an ally within Close range fails a social roll, you can mark 1 Stress to reroll the dice. You must accept the new result.

**Mastery: Galactic Treaty**
Once per Long Rest, you can spend 4 Hope to force two warring factions or hostile groups in your presence to immediately cease hostilities and agree to a temporary truce. This truce lasts for the duration of the scene, provided neither you nor your allies take hostile action.

### The Provocateur
The Provocateur focuses on deception, disguise, and extracting secrets. They are agitators and spies, using misinformation and psychological warfare to dismantle their enemies from the inside.

**Foundation: Sow Discord**
You can spend 1 Hope to plant a rumor or lie that will be believed by the general public within a specific sector or station. The rumor must be plausible, though it does not need to be true.

**Specialization: Incite Rebellion**
You can mark 2 Stress to turn a crowd of civilians or low-level guards against their superiors for the duration of the scene. They will riot, protest, or create a massive distraction, allowing you and your allies to slip by unnoticed.

**Mastery: Character Assassination**
You can spend 3 Hope to completely destroy the reputation of a named NPC, stripping them of their rank, resources, and allies within their faction. This requires at least one scene of preparation (forging documents, leaking data, etc.).

---

chapters.title: The Operative
chapters.content:
*“You don’t need an army to take down an empire. You just need one person in the right place, at the right time, with the right knife.”*

The Operative is a specialist in stealth, sabotage, and targeted elimination. They operate in the shadows, dealing with problems before they become public. While the Envoy negotiates in the light, the Operative ensures the negotiations go smoothly by removing obstacles in the dark. They are ghosts, assassins, and infiltrators, trained to bypass the most advanced security systems in the galaxy.

Operatives are often former intelligence agents, black-ops soldiers, or corporate spies who have gone rogue or freelance. Some work for the Kaelen Syndicate as high-priced cleaners; others are deep-cover agents for the Terran Coalition. Regardless of their origin, they share a pragmatic, ruthless approach to problem-solving.

sections.title: Playing an Operative
sections.content:
**Your Role:** Striker / Infiltrator
**Domains:** Midnight, Grace
**Base HP:** 6
**Base Evasion:** 12

**Hope Feature: Vanish**
Spend 1 Hope to immediately enter stealth, even if you are being observed. You remain hidden until you attack, take damage, or interact with a complex object (like a computer terminal).

**Class Feature: Sneak Attack**
If you attack an enemy who is unaware of your presence, or who is currently engaged in melee with an ally, you deal an additional 1d8 damage.

### The Operative in the Galaxy

On Nexus 9, Operatives are the unseen hand moving the pieces on the board. They are the ones who steal the datapad before the summit, who plant the listening device in the ambassador's quarters, and who ensure the pirate captain never wakes up.

**In Starship Combat:** Electronic Warfare. You hack enemy systems, spoof sensors, and disable targeting locks.
**In Diplomacy:** Breaking and entering. While the Envoy talks at the front door, you are slipping through the ventilation shafts to acquire physical evidence or plant blackmail material.

sections.title: Operative Progression Table
sections.content:
| Level | Tier | HP | Evasion | Proficiency | Features Gained |
|-------|------|----|---------|-------------|-----------------|
| 1 | 1 | 6 | 12 | 1 | Choose Subclass (Phantom or Infiltrator), 1 Domain Card |
| 2 | 1 | 7 | 12 | 1 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 3 | 1 | 8 | 13 | 2 | Subclass Feature: Covert Technique, 1 Domain Card |
| 4 | 2 | 9 | 13 | 2 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 5 | 2 | 10 | 14 | 3 | Subclass Specialization, 1 Domain Card |
| 6 | 2 | 11 | 14 | 3 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 7 | 3 | 12 | 15 | 4 | Subclass Feature: Advanced Tradecraft, 1 Domain Card |
| 8 | 3 | 13 | 15 | 4 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 9 | 4 | 14 | 16 | 5 | Subclass Mastery, 1 Domain Card |
| 10 | 4 | 15 | 16 | 5 | +1 to two Traits, Capstone: Ghost Protocol |

sections.title: Subclasses
sections.content:
### The Ghost
The Ghost focuses on absolute stealth, bypassing security systems, and eliminating targets without leaving a trace. They are the ultimate assassins.

**Foundation: Active Camouflage**
When you are in dim light or darkness, you can spend 1 Hope to become completely invisible until you attack or take damage. While invisible, you gain Advantage on all Finesse rolls to move silently.

**Specialization: Silent Takedown**
When you attack an unaware target from Melee range, you deal an additional 2d6 damage. If this attack reduces a Minion or Standard adversary to 0 HP, they die silently without alerting others.

**Mastery: Phase Walk**
You can teleport between areas of shadow or darkness up to Far range as a Free Action once per turn. You do not need line of sight to your destination, provided you know a shadow exists there.

### The Saboteur
The Saboteur focuses on explosives, traps, and disabling enemy technology. They are chaotic infiltrators who specialize in turning an enemy's fortress into a deathtrap.

**Foundation: Data Spike**
You gain Advantage on all Finesse rolls to hack terminals, bypass security doors, or disable alarms. When you succeed with Hope, you also download a piece of valuable encrypted data.

**Specialization: Rigged to Blow**
You can turn any small electronic device (a datapad, a commlink, a drone) into an explosive. It deals 3d6 energy damage to anyone within Very Close range when triggered remotely or by proximity.

**Mastery: Systemic Collapse**
Once per Long Rest, you can spend 4 Hope to completely disable the security grid, lighting, and automated defenses of an entire facility or ship for the duration of the scene.

chapters.title: The Pilot
chapters.content:
*“The void doesn’t care who you are or what god you pray to. It only cares if you know how to fly.”*

The Pilot is an ace flyer, navigator, and survivalist, most comfortable behind the stick of a starship or a support vehicle. In a galaxy where the distance between habitable worlds is measured in light-years and the space between them is filled with pirates, anomalies, and the encroaching Eclipse, the Pilot is the only thing keeping the crew alive. They are thrill-seekers, adrenaline junkies, and consummate professionals who view a ship not as a machine, but as an extension of their own body.

Pilots come from the prestigious naval academies of the Terran Coalition, the brutal dogfighting arenas of the Kaelen Syndicate, or the solitary scout ships of the Valari Collective. They are defined by their reflexes, their spatial awareness, and their refusal to back down from impossible odds.

sections.title: Playing a Pilot
sections.content:
**Your Role:** Skirmisher / Vehicle Specialist
**Domains:** Bone, Sage
**Base HP:** 6
**Base Evasion:** 12

**Hope Feature: Evasive Action**
Spend 1 Hope to force an enemy to reroll a successful attack against you or your vehicle. You must accept the new result.

**Class Feature: Ace in the Hole**
You gain Advantage on all Agility rolls related to piloting, driving, or navigating a vehicle through hazardous terrain.

### The Pilot in the Galaxy

On Nexus 9, Pilots are the lifeblood of the station. They are the smugglers running blockades, the couriers delivering vital intelligence, and the fighter escorts protecting the massive trade convoys. Without them, the station starves.

**In Starship Combat:** Helm. You are responsible for evasive maneuvers, pursuit, and positioning the ship for optimal firing arcs.
**In Diplomacy:** Extraction planning. While the Envoy negotiates, you are analyzing flight logs, securing a fast exit route, and ensuring the ship is fueled and ready to burn.

sections.title: Pilot Progression Table
sections.content:
| Level | Tier | HP | Evasion | Proficiency | Features Gained |
|-------|------|----|---------|-------------|-----------------|
| 1 | 1 | 6 | 12 | 1 | Choose Subclass (Ace or Outrider), 1 Domain Card |
| 2 | 1 | 7 | 12 | 1 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 3 | 1 | 8 | 13 | 2 | Subclass Feature: Flight Instinct, 1 Domain Card |
| 4 | 2 | 9 | 13 | 2 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 5 | 2 | 10 | 14 | 3 | Subclass Specialization, 1 Domain Card |
| 6 | 2 | 11 | 14 | 3 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 7 | 3 | 12 | 15 | 4 | Subclass Feature: Evasive Maneuvers, 1 Domain Card |
| 8 | 3 | 13 | 15 | 4 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 9 | 4 | 14 | 16 | 5 | Subclass Mastery, 1 Domain Card |
| 10 | 4 | 15 | 16 | 5 | +1 to two Traits, Capstone: One with the Ship |

sections.title: Subclasses
sections.content:
### The Ace
The Ace focuses on dogfighting, pushing vehicles beyond their limits, and outmaneuvering enemy fire. They are the undisputed masters of ship-to-ship combat.

**Foundation: Evasive Maneuvers**
When you are piloting a vehicle or ship, its Evasion increases by +2. If you are on foot, you gain +1 to your personal Evasion while moving.

**Specialization: Target Lock**
When you attack with a vehicle weapon, you can mark 1 Stress to gain Advantage on the roll. If the attack hits, you deal an additional 1d8 damage.

**Mastery: The Needle Threader**
You can navigate your ship or vehicle through impossible hazards (asteroid fields, closing blast doors, heavy crossfire) without needing to make a roll. You automatically succeed, though you may still need to spend Hope for particularly cinematic maneuvers.

### The Frontier Scout
The Frontier Scout focuses on exploration, surviving hostile environments, and tracking targets across the galaxy. They are the pathfinders who map the unknown.

**Foundation: Survivalist**
You gain Advantage on all Instinct rolls to track targets, find safe paths, or survive in hostile environments (extreme cold, radiation, toxic atmospheres).

**Specialization: Ambush Predator**
In the first round of combat, your movement speed is doubled, and your first attack deals an additional 1d8 damage if you act before your target.

**Mastery: Unseen Observer**
You can spend 2 Hope to perfectly blend into any natural or urban environment, becoming undetectable by sight, sound, or thermal sensors as long as you remain motionless. You can maintain this state indefinitely.

---

chapters.title: The Marine
chapters.content:
*“Diplomacy is what happens when the other side realizes they can’t win a firefight. I’m the reason they realize it.”*

The Marine is a shock trooper, trained for zero-G boarding actions, heavy firefights, and holding the line against overwhelming odds. They are the crew's primary physical deterrent, the armored fist that smashes through obstacles when negotiations fail. In a galaxy teetering on the brink of war, the Marine is the soldier who has already accepted that peace is an illusion.

Marines are the veterans of the Aurelian Legions, the heavy assault troopers of the Terran Coalition, or the hardened mercenaries of the frontier. They are defined by their discipline, their firepower, and their willingness to put themselves between their crew and certain death.

sections.title: Playing a Marine
sections.content:
**Your Role:** Frontline Combatant
**Domains:** Blade, Bone
**Base HP:** 6
**Base Evasion:** 11

**Hope Feature: Adrenaline Surge**
Spend 1 Hope to immediately clear 2 Stress or heal 1d4 HP as a Free Action.

**Class Feature: Combat Veteran**
You ignore the "Pinned Down" condition from suppressive fire, and you gain Advantage on rolls to resist being Frightened or Intimidated.

### The Marine in the Galaxy

On Nexus 9, Marines are the muscle that enforces the fragile peace. They are the station security officers, the private military contractors, and the bodyguards for high-ranking diplomats. When the Eclipse or the Syndicate makes a move, the Marine is the one who holds the line.

**In Starship Combat:** Tactical Officer. You are responsible for firing ship weapons, establishing target locks, and coordinating boarding actions or repelling boarders.
**In Diplomacy:** Physical intimidation. While the Envoy talks, you are analyzing the room for threats, identifying the enemy's heavy hitters, and making sure everyone knows what happens if the meeting goes south.

sections.title: Marine Progression Table
sections.content:
| Level | Tier | HP | Evasion | Proficiency | Features Gained |
|-------|------|----|---------|-------------|-----------------|
| 1 | 1 | 6 | 11 | 1 | Choose Subclass Foundation, 1 Domain Card |
| 2 | 1 | 7 | 11 | 1 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 3 | 1 | 8 | 12 | 2 | 1 Domain Card |
| 4 | 2 | 9 | 12 | 2 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 5 | 2 | 10 | 13 | 3 | Choose Subclass Specialization, 1 Domain Card |
| 6 | 2 | 11 | 13 | 3 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 7 | 3 | 12 | 14 | 4 | 1 Domain Card |
| 8 | 3 | 13 | 14 | 4 | Choose Subclass Mastery, 1 Domain Card |
| 9 | 4 | 14 | 15 | 5 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 10 | 4 | 15 | 15 | 5 | Epic Boon, 1 Domain Card |

sections.title: Subclasses
sections.content:
### The Shock Trooper
The Shock Trooper focuses on close-quarters combat, breaching tactics, and overwhelming physical force. They are the tip of the spear in any assault.

**Foundation: Breaching Charge**
You gain Advantage on Strength rolls to break down doors, smash through barricades, or grapple enemies. When you successfully breach an obstacle, you and your allies gain +1 to your next attack roll.

**Specialization: Unstoppable Momentum**
When you reduce an enemy to 0 HP with a melee attack, you can immediately move up to Close range without triggering reactions, and make a second attack against a different target.

**Mastery: Juggernaut**
Once per Long Rest, you can spend 3 Hope to ignore all damage from a single attack, regardless of its severity. You simply shrug off the blow and continue fighting.

### The Heavy Gunner
The Heavy Gunner focuses on suppressive fire, area-of-effect weapons, and controlling the battlefield through sheer volume of fire.

**Foundation: Suppressive Fire**
When you attack with a two-handed ranged weapon, you can mark 1 Stress to force the target and all enemies within Very Close range of them to roll their next attack with Disadvantage.

**Specialization: Overcharge**
When you roll maximum damage on any damage die with a ranged weapon, you can reroll that die and add the new result to the total. You can continue doing this as long as you roll the maximum value.

**Mastery: Walking Artillery**
You can wield two-handed heavy weapons with one hand, allowing you to carry a shield or a secondary weapon without penalty. You also ignore the "Heavy" burden trait on all weapons.

chapters.title: The Engineer
chapters.content:
*“A ship is just a million moving parts waiting to break. I’m the only reason they haven’t killed us yet.”*

The Engineer is a technical savant, capable of keeping a crippled ship flying, turning scavenged parts into deadly weapons, and bypassing the most advanced security systems in the galaxy. In a universe where survival depends on the integrity of a hull and the reliability of a life-support system, the Engineer is the crew's most vital asset. They are problem-solvers, inventors, and mechanics who see the world not as people and politics, but as systems and solutions.

Engineers are often former military technicians, corporate researchers, or self-taught prodigies from the frontier. They are defined by their ingenuity, their resourcefulness, and their ability to fix anything with a hydro-spanner and a roll of conductive tape.

sections.title: Playing an Engineer
sections.content:
**Your Role:** Protector / Support Tank
**Domains:** Valor, Blade
**Base HP:** 7
**Base Evasion:** 9

**Hope Feature: Jury-Rig**
Spend 1 Hope to temporarily repair a broken piece of equipment, bypass a locked door, or restore 1 Armor Slot to an ally.

**Class Feature: Shield-Bearer**
You can use your action to grant an ally in cover an additional +2 to their Evasion, or to project a localized energy shield that absorbs 1d6 damage for them.

### The Engineer in the Galaxy

On Nexus 9, Engineers are the unsung heroes who keep the station from spinning out of orbit. They are the maintenance crews in the Deep Core, the cyber-security experts for the major factions, and the black-market armorers who supply the Syndicate.

**In Starship Combat:** Chief Engineer. You are responsible for rerouting power, managing emergency repairs, and keeping the engines online when the ship takes a critical hit.
**In Diplomacy:** Analyzing technology. While the Envoy talks, you are recovering corrupted data, bypassing security feeds, and identifying the make and model of the enemy's weapons.

sections.title: Engineer Progression Table
sections.content:
| Level | Tier | HP | Evasion | Proficiency | Features Gained |
|-------|------|----|---------|-------------|-----------------|
| 1 | 1 | 7 | 9 | 1 | Choose Subclass (Machinist or Void Warden), 1 Domain Card |
| 2 | 1 | 8 | 9 | 1 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 3 | 1 | 9 | 10 | 2 | Subclass Feature: Field Modification, 1 Domain Card |
| 4 | 2 | 10 | 10 | 2 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 5 | 2 | 11 | 11 | 3 | Subclass Specialization, 1 Domain Card |
| 6 | 2 | 12 | 11 | 3 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 7 | 3 | 13 | 12 | 4 | Subclass Feature: Overclocked Systems, 1 Domain Card |
| 8 | 3 | 14 | 12 | 4 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 9 | 4 | 15 | 13 | 5 | Subclass Mastery, 1 Domain Card |
| 10 | 4 | 16 | 13 | 5 | +1 to two Traits, Capstone: Master Engineer |

sections.title: Subclasses
sections.content:
### The Void Warden
The Void Warden focuses on personal armor, protecting allies, and absorbing damage. They are walking tanks, designed to hold the line and keep the crew safe.

**Foundation: Defensive Grid**
You can project a localized energy shield. Allies within Melee range of you gain +1 to their Armor Score, and you gain Advantage on rolls to resist being moved or knocked prone.

**Specialization: Reactive Shielding**
When an ally within Close range takes damage, you can mark 1 Stress to reduce that damage by your Armor Score. You can do this after the damage is rolled but before it is applied.

**Mastery: Aegis Protocol**
Once per Long Rest, you can spend 4 Hope to make yourself and all allies within Close range immune to all damage for 1 round. The shield absorbs everything, from small-arms fire to ship-scale weaponry.

### The Combat Technician
The Combat Technician focuses on hacking, drones, and modifying weapons. They are battlefield controllers who use technology to outsmart and outgun the enemy.

**Foundation: Jury-Rig**
During a Short Rest, you can repair one broken piece of equipment or restore 1 Armor Slot to an ally for free, without needing tools or spare parts.

**Specialization: Drone Swarm**
You deploy a permanent companion drone. It can take the Help action once per round without consuming your action economy, granting an ally Advantage on their next roll.

**Mastery: System Overload**
You can spend 2 Hope to force any technological device, weapon, or vehicle within Far range to immediately shut down or malfunction for 1d4 rounds. This includes enemy cybernetics and powered armor.

---

chapters.title: The Mystic
chapters.content:
*“The universe is not empty space. It is a web of thoughts, emotions, and memories. I just know how to pluck the strings.”*

The Mystic is a telepath, a spiritual adept, or a technomancer capable of manipulating the minds of others or sensing the metaphysical currents of the universe. In a galaxy dominated by technology and firepower, the Mystic wields a power that cannot be bought, built, or easily defended against. They are the readers of minds, the seers of futures, and the manipulators of reality.

Mystics are often born into the Mindclave, trained from childhood to harness their psychic potential. Others are rogue telepaths hiding from the authorities, or Valari spiritualists who view their abilities as a divine gift. They are defined by their connection to the unseen world and the heavy toll that power takes on their minds.

sections.title: Playing a Mystic
sections.content:
**Your Role:** Ranged Damage / Telepathy
**Domains:** Arcana, Midnight
**Base HP:** 6
**Base Evasion:** 10

**Hope Feature: Mind Trick**
Spend 1 Hope to plant a brief, harmless suggestion in an NPC's mind, or to make them forget the last 30 seconds of conversation.

**Class Feature: Psychic Resonance**
You can communicate telepathically with any willing ally within line of sight, or with any ally you have previously bonded with, regardless of distance (provided they are on the same planet or station).

### The Mystic in the Galaxy

On Nexus 9, Mystics are both feared and highly sought after. They are the ultimate interrogators, the perfect spies, and the most dangerous wildcards in any negotiation. The Mindclave tightly controls registered telepaths, but rogue Mystics operate in the shadows, selling their services to the highest bidder.

**In Starship Combat:** Sensor Operator. You are responsible for sensor sweeps, detecting cloaked ships, and sensing the emotional state of enemy crews before they even open comms.
**In Diplomacy:** Reading surface thoughts. While the Envoy talks, you are detecting lies, sensing emotional imprints, and uncovering the hidden motives of the people across the table.

sections.title: Mystic Progression Table
sections.content:
| Level | Tier | HP | Evasion | Proficiency | Features Gained |
|-------|------|----|---------|-------------|-----------------|
| 1 | 1 | 6 | 10 | 1 | Choose Subclass (Void Seer or Mindbreaker), 1 Domain Card |
| 2 | 1 | 7 | 10 | 1 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 3 | 1 | 8 | 11 | 2 | Subclass Feature: Psychic Resonance, 1 Domain Card |
| 4 | 2 | 9 | 11 | 2 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 5 | 2 | 10 | 12 | 3 | Subclass Specialization, 1 Domain Card |
| 6 | 2 | 11 | 12 | 3 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 7 | 3 | 12 | 13 | 4 | Subclass Feature: Deep Communion, 1 Domain Card |
| 8 | 3 | 13 | 13 | 4 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 9 | 4 | 14 | 14 | 5 | Subclass Mastery, 1 Domain Card |
| 10 | 4 | 15 | 14 | 5 | +1 to two Traits, Capstone: Transcendence |

sections.title: Subclasses
sections.content:
### The Telepath
The Telepath focuses on precognition, sensing danger, and reading emotional residue. They are the ultimate investigators and manipulators of the mind.

**Foundation: Mind Reading**
You can spend 1 Hope to read the surface thoughts of a target within Close range for one minute. You gain Advantage on all social rolls against them during this time.

**Specialization: Psychic Assault**
When you deal energy damage with a Domain Card, you can mark 1 Stress to change the damage type to psychic and force the target to mark 1 Stress. This bypasses physical armor.

**Mastery: Mind Control**
Once per Long Rest, you can spend 5 Hope to take complete control of an NPC's actions for the duration of the scene. They will follow your commands to the best of their ability, though self-destructive commands grant them a chance to break free.

### The Technomancer
The Technomancer focuses on telekinetic force and interfacing with machines. They bridge the gap between the biological and the artificial, controlling technology with their minds.

**Foundation: Machine Whisperer**
You can interface with computers, droids, and ship systems using your mind, requiring no physical connection or terminal. You gain Advantage on all Knowledge rolls to hack or slice.

**Specialization: Construct Override**
You can spend 2 Hope to take control of an enemy drone, turret, or synthetic for 1d4 rounds. It acts on your turn and follows your mental commands.

**Mastery: Ghost in the Machine**
You can permanently transfer your consciousness into a ship's mainframe or a secure network, allowing you to operate its systems as if they were your own body. You can return to your physical body as a Free Action.

chapters.title: The Broker
chapters.content:
*“Everything has a price. The trick isn’t knowing what it is; the trick is knowing who’s willing to pay it.”*

The Broker is a fixer, a smuggler, and a master of logistics. They know how to acquire rare goods, move them without attracting attention, and find the people who don't want to be found. In a galaxy where the flow of information and resources is tightly controlled by the major factions, the Broker operates in the grey areas, ensuring that the right people get the right things at exactly the right time.

Brokers are the lifeblood of the frontier and the underbelly of Nexus 9. They are former corporate executives who realized the real money was in the black market, independent traders who refuse to bow to the Syndicate, or information peddlers who trade in secrets rather than credits. They are defined by their connections, their resourcefulness, and their ability to turn a profit in any situation.

sections.title: Playing a Broker
sections.content:
**Your Role:** Utility / Controller
**Domains:** Sage, Arcana
**Base HP:** 6
**Base Evasion:** 10

**Hope Feature: Hidden Cache**
Spend 1 Hope to produce a common item that you "happened" to have on you, or to reveal a hidden compartment containing a small weapon or tool.

**Class Feature: Black Market Ties**
You can always find a buyer or a seller for illegal goods, regardless of your location. You gain Advantage on all Presence rolls to negotiate prices or secure rare items.

### The Broker in the Galaxy

On Nexus 9, Brokers are the people you go to when you need something done quietly. They are the ones who secure the forged transit papers, who find the untraceable weapons, and who know exactly which customs official is willing to look the other way.

**In Starship Combat:** Quartermaster. You are responsible for managing cargo, optimizing fuel consumption, and ensuring the ship's systems are running at peak efficiency.
**In Diplomacy:** Following the money. While the Envoy talks, you are identifying the black-market origins of the enemy's gear, bribing the guards, and finding the financial leverage needed to close the deal.

sections.title: Broker Progression Table
sections.content:
| Level | Tier | HP | Evasion | Proficiency | Features Gained |
|-------|------|----|---------|-------------|-----------------|
| 1 | 1 | 6 | 10 | 1 | Choose Subclass (Fixer or Quartermaster), 1 Domain Card |
| 2 | 1 | 7 | 10 | 1 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 3 | 1 | 8 | 11 | 2 | Subclass Feature: Network Leverage, 1 Domain Card |
| 4 | 2 | 9 | 11 | 2 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 5 | 2 | 10 | 12 | 3 | Subclass Specialization, 1 Domain Card |
| 6 | 2 | 11 | 12 | 3 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 7 | 3 | 12 | 13 | 4 | Subclass Feature: Market Manipulation, 1 Domain Card |
| 8 | 3 | 13 | 13 | 4 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 9 | 4 | 14 | 14 | 5 | Subclass Mastery, 1 Domain Card |
| 10 | 4 | 15 | 14 | 5 | +1 to two Traits, Capstone: The Invisible Hand |

sections.title: Subclasses
sections.content:
### The Smuggler
The Smuggler focuses on hiding contraband, bypassing customs inspections, and moving goods across the galaxy without detection. They are the masters of the hidden compartment and the forged manifest.

**Foundation: Hidden Compartments**
You can conceal up to 3 small items on your person or in your ship that cannot be found by standard security scans or physical searches. You gain Advantage on all Finesse rolls to hide objects.

**Specialization: The Right Price**
You can spend 1 Hope to automatically succeed on a Persuasion roll to bribe a guard, official, or informant, provided you offer them something of value (credits, information, or a favor).

**Mastery: Untraceable**
Your ship and your personal comms cannot be tracked, intercepted, or recorded by any faction, regardless of their technological superiority. You are a ghost in the system.

### The Information Broker
The Information Broker focuses on acquiring information, forging documents, and using blackmail to achieve their goals. They trade in secrets, and they always know more than they let on.

**Foundation: A Guy Who Knows A Guy**
Once per session, you can declare that you have a contact in your current location who owes you a favor. This contact will provide valuable information or minor assistance without requiring a roll.

**Specialization: Blackmail**
When you interact with a named NPC, you can mark 2 Stress to reveal a piece of damaging information you hold over them, forcing them to comply with a reasonable request or suffer the consequences.

**Mastery: The Spider's Web**
You can spend 3 Hope to instantly know the location, current status, and immediate plans of any single individual in the galaxy, provided you have met them before or have a piece of their personal property.

---

chapters.title: The Medic
chapters.content:
*“I don’t care what uniform you’re wearing or who shot you. You’re on my table now, and that means you don’t get to die until I say so.”*

The Medic is a trauma surgeon, xenobiologist, and combat lifesaver, essential for keeping the crew alive in a hostile galaxy. In a universe where a single plasma burn or a ruptured environmental seal can mean agonizing death, the Medic is the line between survival and becoming another statistic. They are healers, scientists, and sometimes, the only people who truly understand the biological horrors lurking in the dark.

Medics are often former military doctors, researchers from the Aurelian Empire, or frontier healers who have learned to make do with whatever supplies they can scavenge. They are defined by their compassion, their clinical detachment, and their refusal to let anyone die on their watch.

sections.title: Playing a Medic
sections.content:
**Your Role:** Healer / Support
**Domains:** Splendor, Valor
**Base HP:** 7
**Base Evasion:** 9

**Hope Feature: Combat Triage**
Spend 1 Hope to heal an ally for 1d8 HP as a Free Action, provided you are within Melee range of them.

**Class Feature: Xenobiology**
You gain Advantage on all Knowledge rolls to identify alien toxins, diseases, or biological weaknesses, and on rolls to synthesize antidotes or treatments.

### The Medic in the Galaxy

On Nexus 9, Medics are the most respected and the most overworked people on the station. They are the trauma surgeons in the lower rings, the researchers studying the effects of Eclipse corruption, and the combat medics who patch up the Syndicate's enforcers no questions asked.

**In Starship Combat:** Chief Medical Officer. You are responsible for treating injuries during combat, managing life support systems, and ensuring the crew doesn't succumb to radiation or decompression.
**In Diplomacy:** Autopsies and analysis. While the Envoy talks, you are identifying the toxins used in an assassination, analyzing biological evidence, and determining the true cause of death.

sections.title: Medic Progression Table
sections.content:
| Level | Tier | HP | Evasion | Proficiency | Features Gained |
|-------|------|----|---------|-------------|-----------------|
| 1 | 1 | 7 | 9 | 1 | Choose Subclass (Combat Medic or Xenobiologist), 1 Domain Card |
| 2 | 1 | 8 | 9 | 1 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 3 | 1 | 9 | 10 | 2 | Subclass Feature: Triage Protocol, 1 Domain Card |
| 4 | 2 | 10 | 10 | 2 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 5 | 2 | 11 | 11 | 3 | Subclass Specialization, 1 Domain Card |
| 6 | 2 | 12 | 11 | 3 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 7 | 3 | 13 | 12 | 4 | Subclass Feature: Emergency Response, 1 Domain Card |
| 8 | 3 | 14 | 12 | 4 | +1 to two Traits, 1 Domain Card |
| 9 | 4 | 15 | 13 | 5 | Subclass Mastery, 1 Domain Card |
| 10 | 4 | 16 | 13 | 5 | +1 to two Traits, Capstone: Miracle Worker |

sections.title: Subclasses
sections.content:
### The Combat Surgeon
The Combat Surgeon focuses on rapid healing, stabilizing critically injured allies, and keeping the crew fighting through impossible pain. They are the angels of the battlefield.

**Foundation: Triage**
When you heal an ally who is at 0 HP, they clear an additional 1d6 HP. You gain Advantage on all rolls to stabilize dying characters.

**Specialization: Stim-Pack**
When you heal an ally, you can mark 1 Stress to also grant them Advantage on their next action roll, as the combat stimulants flood their system.

**Mastery: Miracle Worker**
Once per Long Rest, you can spend 5 Hope to completely restore an ally to full HP and clear all their Stress, even if they have just died (provided they died within the last minute and their body is mostly intact).

### The Xenobiologist
The Xenobiologist focuses on analyzing alien lifeforms, synthesizing antidotes, and turning the enemy's biology against them. They are scientists who use their knowledge as a weapon.

**Foundation: Anatomical Insight**
You gain Advantage on all Knowledge rolls related to alien biology, toxins, and diseases. When you succeed with Hope, you identify a critical weakness in the target's physiology.

**Specialization: Targeted Strike**
When you attack an organic enemy, you can mark 1 Stress to ignore their Armor Score by striking a vital nerve cluster, organ, or weak point you have identified.

**Mastery: Bio-Manipulation**
You can spend 3 Hope to alter the biology of a willing ally for the duration of the scene, granting them immunity to a specific damage type (e.g., acid, radiation, poison) or allowing them to breathe in a toxic atmosphere.

sections.title: The Vault and The Loadout
sections.content:
As characters level up in *Nexus 9*, they acquire new Domain Cards. However, a character cannot have every ability ready at all times.

*   **The Vault:** This is the collection of all Domain Cards your character has unlocked. Cards in your Vault cannot be used during a session.
*   **The Loadout:** This is your active hand of cards. You can only use the abilities printed on the cards currently in your Loadout.

sections.title: Managing Your Loadout
sections.content:
*   **Loadout Limit:** A character can have a maximum of **5 Domain Cards** in their active Loadout at any given time.
*   **Swapping Cards:** During a **Long Rest**, you can swap any number of cards between your Vault and your Loadout. During a **Short Rest**, you can swap exactly **one** card.
*   **Card Levels:** You can only place a card in your Loadout if your character's Level is equal to or higher than the card's Level.

sections.title: Card Types and Action Economy
sections.content:
Every Domain Card specifies when it can be used:
*   **(Action):** Using this card requires you to take the Spotlight and make an Action Roll. It consumes your turn in the action economy.
*   **(Reaction):** Using this card does not require the Spotlight. It is played in response to a specific trigger (e.g., "When you are attacked").
*   **(Passive):** This card provides a constant benefit as long as it is in your active Loadout. It does not require an action or a trigger to use.
*   **(Downtime):** This card can only be used during a Short or Long Rest, or during narrative downtime between missions.

sections.title: Costs
sections.content:
Many Domain Cards require a resource expenditure to activate:
*   **Spend Hope:** You must discard the specified number of Hope tokens from your character sheet. If you do not have enough Hope, you cannot use the card.
*   **Mark Stress:** You must mark the specified number of Stress boxes on your character sheet. If marking this Stress would cause you to exceed your maximum Stress, you take HP damage for the overflow as per the core rules.

---

chapters.title: 11. Cross-Class Domain Reference
chapters.content:
Because the 8 classes share the 9 Domains, players will often find themselves with access to the same pools of abilities. This encourages synergy and allows for diverse builds within the same class.

| Domain | Theme | Shared By |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Arcana** | Telepathy, Technomancy, Spacetime Manipulation | Mystic, Broker |
| **Blade** | Melee Superiority, Breaching, Physical Resilience | Marine, Engineer |
| **Bone** | Tactics, Evasion, Battlefield Positioning | Pilot, Marine |
| **Codex** | Law, Lore, Bureaucracy, System Manipulation | Envoy |
| **Grace** | Diplomacy, Deception, Social Engineering | Envoy, Operative |
| **Midnight** | Stealth, Sabotage, Assassination | Operative, Mystic |
| **Sage** | Survival, Logistics, Environmental Control | Pilot, Broker |
| **Splendor** | Medicine, Morale, Radiation/Light | Medic |
| **Valor** | Protection, Heavy Weapons, Holding the Line | Engineer, Medic |

*(Note: In the current 8-class structure, Codex and Splendor are exclusive to the Envoy and Medic, respectively. A future 9th class may share these domains).*

---

chapters.content:
