| Below Minor | 0 | 0 |
| Meets/Exceeds Minor | 1 | 0 |
| Meets/Exceeds Major | 2 | 1 |
| Meets/Exceeds Severe | 3 | 2 |

When a ship's **System Stress** is maxed out, the GM rolls on the **Critical System Failure Table** (see Book Four).

When a ship's **Hull Points** reach 0, the ship is **disabled** — it is dead in space, venting atmosphere, and the crew must abandon ship or attempt emergency repairs (Engineering roll, Difficulty 20).

---

sections.title: Boarding Actions
sections.content:
When two ships are at Melee range (hulls touching), either side may attempt a boarding action. Boarding transitions the encounter from ship-scale to personal-scale combat:

**1. The attacking ship's Helm** must succeed on an Agility roll (Difficulty 13) to lock docking clamps or breach the hull.

**2. Once locked**, the boarding party (some or all of the crew) enters the enemy ship. Combat switches to personal-scale rules.

**3. The remaining crew** on the player's ship continue to operate their stations at ship scale, defending against enemy fighters, maintaining systems, or providing fire support.

**4. The boarding party** fights through the enemy ship using personal combat rules. Their objective might be to reach the bridge, disable the engines, rescue prisoners, or plant explosives.

---

sections.title: Pursuit and Escape
sections.content:
If one side attempts to flee, the engagement becomes a **pursuit**:

**1.** The fleeing ship's Helm rolls Agility vs. the pursuing ship's Helm roll each spotlight turn.

**2.** On success, the fleeing ship increases range by one band. On failure, the range stays the same (or closes if the pursuer rolled a critical success).

**3.** If the fleeing ship reaches **beyond Very Far range**, it escapes. If it has a functioning hyperspace drive, it can attempt an emergency jump (Knowledge roll, Difficulty 15). Success means immediate escape; failure means the drive misfires and the ship drops out at a random, potentially dangerous location.

---

sections.title: Quick Reference: Ship Combat Checklist
sections.content:
| Step | Action |
|:---:|:---|
| 1 | Establish starting range and Fear budget |
| 2 | Players declare crew stations |
| 3 | First player takes the spotlight |
| 4 | Player chooses a station action and rolls 2d12 + Trait |
| 5 | Resolve Hope/Fear — spotlight passes |
| 6 | GM spends Fear for enemy actions |
| 7 | Repeat until encounter resolves |
| 8 | If ships reach Melee, boarding is possible |
| 9 | If a ship flees beyond Very Far, it escapes |

chapters.title: 1. Station Ring Encounter Tables

sections.title: The Blue Ring (Diplomatic Quarter)
sections.content:
*Atmosphere: Ozone, expensive incense, cool lighting, quiet corridors.*

| d12 | Encounter |
|:---:|:---|
| **1** | A Mindclave Hound (Tier 2 Skulk) is subtly scanning the minds of passing diplomats. If confronted, they claim official business. |
| **2** | Two Aurelian nobles are engaged in a heated, whispered argument about a "temporal echo" that contradicts official policy. |
| **3** | A Valari Path of the Flame procession blocks the corridor, chanting in harmonic resonance. Interrupting them is a severe diplomatic insult. |
| **4** | A Terran Coalition Senator is surrounded by journalists, loudly demanding increased security funding for the station. |
| **5** | A League of Unaligned Worlds ambassador drops a physical data-chit. Do the players return it, or slice it? |
| **6** | A sudden, localized gravity fluctuation (Hazard) causes several expensive art pieces to shatter. |
| **7** | A member of the Valari **Order of the Sentinel** (the ancient guardian order) (in disguise) makes a dead-drop exchange with a contact, but the contact looks terrified. |
| **8** | An Eclipse-Corrupted Diplomat (Tier 2 Support) is charming a group of young aides, asking them what they truly desire. |
| **9** | A Kaelen Syndicate representative is being denied entry to a high-level meeting, and they are losing their temper. |
| **10** | A maintenance drone malfunctions, spraying cleaning foam everywhere and causing a minor panic. |
| **11** | The Emissary (Luminary) glides past. It stops, looks directly at one of the PCs, and says, "You are out of time," before moving on. |
| **12** | An assassination attempt! A sniper fires from a ventilation shaft at a passing VIP. The players are the only ones close enough to intervene. |

sections.title: The Green Ring (Commercial District)
sections.content:
*Atmosphere: Exotic spices, roasting meats, neon signs, chaotic crowds.*

| d12 | Encounter |
|:---:|:---|
| **1** | A street vendor is selling "genuine" Progenitor artifacts. A Knowledge roll reveals they are cheap fakes, but one item in the pile is real. |
| **2** | A brawl breaks out between off-duty Terran Marines and Kaelen Syndicate thugs. Coalition Security is nowhere to be seen. |
| **3** | A pickpocket bumps into a PC. Make an Instinct roll to notice the theft of 1d10 x 10 Credits or a minor item. |
| **4** | A holographic advertisement malfunctions, looping a subliminal Mindclave recruitment message. |
| **5** | A smuggler from the Underbelly offers the crew a highly illegal weapon modification at a steep discount, but they need the Credits *now*. |
| **6** | A sudden power surge plunges the Promenade into darkness for two minutes. When the lights return, someone is dead. |
| **7** | A Valari Path of the Blade member is challenging passersby to honorable non-lethal combat to test their worth. |
| **8** | A frantic refugee begs the crew for help, claiming they are being hunted by a Bounty Hunter (Tier 2 Skulk). |
| **9** | A high-stakes gambling game is taking place in a VIP lounge. The buy-in is steep, but the information being traded is priceless. |
| **10** | A street preacher is screaming about the return of Aethelgard and the end of the cycle, drawing a large, agitated crowd. |
| **11** | A Coalition Security patrol is conducting random, aggressive "stop and frisk" searches, targeting non-humans. |
| **12** | A Syndicate Fixer (Tier 1 Support) approaches the crew with a job offer that sounds too good to be true. |

sections.title: The Red Ring (Military/Security)
sections.content:
*Atmosphere: Gun oil, antiseptic, harsh lighting, 1.1g gravity.*

| d12 | Encounter |
|:---:|:---|
| **1** | A squad of Terran Marines (Tier 1 Standard) is conducting a live-fire drill in a restricted corridor. |
| **2** | A Coalition Security Officer (Tier 1 Standard) demands to see the crew's identification and travel permits. |
| **3** | A massive Aurelian Imperial Dreadnought docks at Bay Alpha, its weapons visibly powered up. |
| **4** | A prisoner transfer goes wrong. A Syndicate Breacher (Tier 2 Bruiser) breaks their restraints and attacks the guards. |
| **5** | A low-level radiation leak (Hazard) triggers an automated lockdown, trapping the crew in a corridor for an hour. |
| **6** | A group of mechanics is arguing over how to repair a damaged jump engine. A PC with high Knowledge could help and earn a favor. |
| **7** | A Vanguard operative is caught trying to slice into the station's primary sensor array. |
| **8** | A Kaelen Syndicate smuggling ship is impounded, and the crew is watching from the shadows, planning a heist to get their cargo back. |
| **9** | Commander Vance is personally inspecting the docking bays, looking exhausted and angry. |
| **10** | A shipment of military-grade combat stims is left unguarded for exactly two minutes. |
| **11** | A Mindclave Inquisitor (Tier 2 Support) is interrogating a terrified dock worker. |
| **12** | An automated Station Security Drone (Tier 1 Ranged) malfunctions and targets the crew, classifying them as "hostile intruders." |

| **11** | A pair of Mindclave **Silencers** are escorting a hooded prisoner through the corridor. They warn everyone to look away. |

sections.title: The Gold Ring (Scientific)
sections.content:
*Atmosphere: Sterile scrubbers, chemical preservatives, bright clinical lighting.*

| d12 | Encounter |
|:---:|:---|
| **1** | A containment breach in the Xenobiology Lab! A Valari Bio-Construct (Tier 2 Bruiser) is loose in the corridors. |
| **2** | A researcher frantically asks the crew to help them transport a volatile chemical compound before it degrades. |
| **3** | A Mindclave telepath is undergoing a painful, experimental procedure to increase their Resonance Index. |
| **4** | A group of scientists is debating the ethical implications of reverse-engineering Eclipse crystalline technology. |
| **5** | A medical emergency requires a PC with high Finesse or Knowledge to assist in a life-saving surgery. |
| **6** | A strange, localized temporal anomaly causes the crew to experience the same three minutes twice. |
| **7** | A researcher offers to pay the crew handsomely if they will test an experimental combat stim (with unknown side effects). |
| **8** | A Terran Combat Medic (Tier 1 Support) is stealing supplies to treat refugees in the Grey Ring. |
| **9** | A Valari priest is arguing with a scientist over the dissection of a creature they consider sacred. |
| **10** | A data-terminal is left logged in, displaying classified research on the Nexus Core. |
| **11** | A sudden drop in temperature and a feeling of profound dread indicates the presence of a Void-Spawn (Tier 1 Minion) hiding in the vents. |
| **12** | An explosion rocks a nearby lab. The crew must navigate fire and toxic smoke to rescue the survivors. |

sections.title: The Grey Ring (Industrial/Residential)
sections.content:
*Atmosphere: Sweat, rust, recycled water, erratic lighting, inconsistent gravity.*

| d12 | Encounter |
|:---:|:---|
| **1** | A gang of Underbelly Thugs (Tier 1 Minions) attempts to shake down the crew for a "transit toll." |
| **2** | A localized gravity failure (Hazard) sends heavy cargo crates floating dangerously through the air. |
| **3** | A black-market auction is taking place, selling stolen military hardware and illegal cybernetics. |
| **4** | A group of refugees is huddled around a failing atmospheric scrubber, struggling to breathe. |
| **5** | A Syndicate "Cleaner" (Tier 3 Skulk) is disposing of a body in a waste reclamation vat. |
| **6** | A secret Vanguard meeting is interrupted by a Coalition Security raid. |
| **7** | A cult dedicated to the Eclipse is holding a dark ritual in an abandoned cargo bay. |
| **8** | A massive, jury-rigged machine explodes, causing a hull breach (Hazard) that must be sealed immediately. |
| **9** | A child steals a vital piece of equipment from a PC and darts into a maze of narrow ventilation shafts. |
| **10** | A turf war breaks out between two rival smuggling gangs, catching the crew in the crossfire. |
| **11** | A rumor spreads that the entrance to Grey Level 17 has been found, and a mob is forming to explore it. |
| **12** | A Whisperer in the Dark (Tier 3 Skulk) stalks the crew through a section of unlit, abandoned corridors. |

chapters.content:
Nexus 9 is a massive, densely populated city in space. It is not a static backdrop; it is a living, breathing ecosystem of diplomats, smugglers, refugees, soldiers, and spies. When the crew is moving through the station, the GM can use the **Ring Encounter Tables** to generate immediate complications, atmospheric flavor, or unexpected opportunities.

These encounters are not always combat. Many are social, environmental, or narrative hooks that can lead to new alliances or side missions.

### When to Roll for an Encounter

The GM should roll a **d12** on the appropriate Ring table in the following situations:

- **Transit:** When the crew travels from one Ring to another (e.g., taking the central transit tram from the Green Ring to the Blue Ring).
- **Investigation:** When the crew is actively searching for information, a contact, or a hidden location within a specific Ring.
- **Pacing:** When the session needs a sudden injection of tension, or when the players roll a failure with Fear on a non-combat check while exploring the station.
- **Downtime:** When a character is pursuing a Downtime Activity (like Carousing or Gathering Intel) and the GM wants to introduce a complication.

### How to Use the Tables

The tables provide a starting point. The GM should adapt the encounter to fit the current narrative context. If the crew is currently allied with the Kaelen Syndicate, a Kaelen encounter might be an opportunity rather than a threat. If the station is on high alert due to a recent terrorist attack, even a benign encounter might be fraught with paranoia and heavy security.

If you roll an encounter that doesn't make sense for the current situation, simply choose the one above or below it, or use the rolled encounter as inspiration to create your own.

### Encounter Severity

Not every encounter needs to be a full scene. Some are atmospheric — a brief glimpse of station life that reminds the players where they are. Others are hooks that can lead to entire side missions. The GM should gauge the table's energy and the session's pacing before deciding how deeply to develop a rolled encounter.

- **Atmospheric (1-2 minutes):** Describe the encounter in a sentence or two. No rolls required. It's flavor.
- **Complication (5-10 minutes):** The encounter requires a single roll or a brief social interaction. It might cost the crew time, credits, or a minor resource.
- **Scene (15-30 minutes):** The encounter develops into a full scene with multiple rolls, NPC interactions, and potential consequences.

chapters.title: 2. Off-Station Exploration Procedures

sections.title: The Exploration Turn
sections.content:
Exploration is divided into **Turns**, representing roughly 4-6 hours of travel, scanning, or deep-space maneuvering. During each turn, the crew must assign roles (Helm, Sensors, Engineering) and make a **Survey Roll**.

**The Survey Roll:** The Sensor Operator (or any character with high Instinct) rolls 2d12 + Instinct against a Difficulty set by the environment (usually 12 for known space, 15 for the Frontier, 18 for the Rimward Marches).

*   **Success with Hope:** The crew discovers something valuable (salvage, a hidden route, a tactical advantage) and avoids any hazards.
*   **Success with Fear:** The crew navigates safely, but the GM gains 1 Fear or a complication arises (e.g., a minor system failure, a delayed arrival).
*   **Failure with Hope:** The crew encounters a hazard or enemy, but they have the upper hand or a chance to avoid it.
*   **Failure with Fear:** The crew is ambushed, suffers a major system failure, or blunders into a deadly hazard.

sections.title: Deep Space Encounter Table
sections.content:
If an encounter is triggered during an Exploration Turn, roll a **d12**:

| d12 | Deep Space Encounter |
|:---:|:---|
| **1** | **Derelict Ship:** A centuries-old Elder vessel drifting without power. High salvage potential, but likely guarded by automated defenses or Void-Spawn. |
| **2** | **Pirate Ambush:** A Kaelen Syndicate or Independent pirate crew (Tier 2) attacks from the cover of an asteroid field. |
| **3** | **Distress Signal:** A civilian transport is under attack or suffering a critical failure. Rescuing them earns major faction reputation. |
| **4** | **Hyperspace Anomaly:** A localized tear in space-time. The Helm must make a Difficulty 15 Agility roll to avoid taking 1d6 Hull Damage. |
| **5** | **Aurelian Patrol:** An Imperial cruiser demands to board and inspect the crew's ship for "contraband." |
| **6** | **Progenitor Relic:** A massive, geometric structure floating in the void. Approaching it causes strange telepathic whispers and visions. |
| **7** | **Silicate Swarm:** A cloud of crystalline, ship-eating organisms (Tier 2 Minions) descends on the vessel. |
| **8** | **Terran Blockade:** A Coalition fleet has locked down a hyperspace gate, demanding exorbitant tolls or bribes to pass. |
| **9** | **Technoseer Outpost:** A hidden, cloaked station. The Technoseers offer rare technology in exchange for dangerous favors. |
| **10** | **Asteroid Field:** A dense field of debris. The Helm must navigate carefully, or Engineering must constantly repair minor hull breaches. |
| **11** | **Mindclave Black Site:** A heavily guarded, off-the-books prison facility. Approaching too closely triggers a psychic warning to turn back. |
| **12** | **Hyperspace Phantom:** A terrifying, incorporeal entity (Tier 3 Solo) stalks the ship while it is in hyperspace, attempting to drive the crew mad. |

---

sections.title: Navigation Hazards & Fuel Management
sections.content:
Space travel is not free. Every jump, every evasive maneuver, and every hour spent scanning a derelict consumes resources. The primary resource tracked during exploration is **Fuel Cells**.

### Fuel Consumption

A standard starship frame holds a maximum of **10 Fuel Cells**. Fuel is consumed in the following ways:

| Action | Fuel Cost |
|:---|:---:|
| Standard Jump (known hyperspace lane) | 1 Cell |
| Frontier Jump (uncharted territory) | 2 Cells |
| Evasive Maneuvers (fleeing or navigating hazards) | 1 Cell |
| Extended Operations (per 24 hours beyond 48) | 1 Cell |
| Emergency Burn (double speed for one turn) | 2 Cells |

If a ship reaches **0 Fuel Cells**, it is stranded. The crew must either broadcast a distress signal (which is just as likely to attract pirates as rescue) or attempt a dangerous, unpowered drift toward the nearest gravity well. An unpowered drift requires a DC 15 Agility roll from the Pilot; on a failure, the ship takes 2d8 Hull damage from micrometeorites and gravitational stress.

### Refueling

Fuel Cells can be purchased at any station or outpost for **50 Credits per Cell**. In the Frontier, prices double. In the Rimward Marches, fuel is only available through barter or salvage.

### Environmental Hazards

The void is filled with natural and unnatural dangers. When the crew enters a new sector, or when a Survey Roll results in a failure with Fear, the GM can introduce an Environmental Hazard.

| d6 | Hazard | Effect |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| **1** | **Ion Storm** | Scrambles sensors and communications. All Instinct and Knowledge rolls for scanning or navigation are made with Disadvantage for 1d4 Exploration Turns. |
| **2** | **Gravity Shear** | Unpredictable gravitational eddies stress the ship's frame. The Pilot must make a DC 13 Agility roll; on a failure, the ship takes 1d8 Hull damage. |
| **3** | **Eclipse Drift Zone** | An area of space corrupted by the Fraying Dark. All crew must make a DC 12 Presence roll or take 1 Stress from psychic intrusion. |
| **4** | **Debris Field** | Dense wreckage from an ancient battle or collapsed station. The Pilot must make a DC 14 Agility roll to navigate safely; failure means 2d6 Hull damage. |
| **5** | **Radiation Burst** | A nearby star emits a sudden flare. All crew in exposed areas take 1d6 damage unless they reach shielded compartments within 1 round. |
| **6** | **Sensor Ghost** | The ship's sensors detect a massive object that isn't there. The GM gains 2 Fear. The crew wastes 1 Fuel Cell investigating. |

sections.title: Salvage & Discovery Procedures
sections.content:
The primary reason crews risk the deep frontier is the promise of salvage. Derelict ships, abandoned mining outposts, and Progenitor ruins offer wealth and technology that cannot be found on Nexus 9.

### The Salvage Roll

When the crew discovers a point of interest and decides to board or strip it for parts, they must make a **Salvage Roll**. This is typically an Intellect or Agility roll made by the Engineer or the character leading the salvage operation.

- **Success with Hope:** The crew secures high-value salvage (rare technology, intact data cores, or 2d10 x 100 Credits worth of raw materials) without incident.
- **Success with Fear:** The crew secures the salvage, but triggers a complication. A dormant security system activates, the structural integrity of the derelict begins to fail, or a hostile entity is awakened.
- **Failure with Hope:** The salvage is ruined or inaccessible, but the crew discovers a valuable piece of intelligence or a safe route out before things get worse.
- **Failure with Fear:** The salvage is a trap. The crew takes damage, loses resources, or is ambushed by a Tier 2 threat.

### Salvage Value Table

| d8 | Salvage Type | Value | Notes |
|:---:|:---|:---:|:---|
| **1** | Scrap Metal & Components | 1d6 x 10 Credits | Common, no special properties |
| **2** | Intact Power Cells | 1d4 Fuel Cells | Can be used directly or sold at 50 Credits each |
| **3** | Military-Grade Hardware | 1d6 x 50 Credits | Weapons, armor plating, or targeting systems |
| **4** | Data Cores | 1d4 x 100 Credits | Encrypted; may contain star charts, faction intel, or personal logs |
| **5** | Medical Supplies | 1d6 x 30 Credits | Restores 1d8 HP to one character if used directly |
| **6** | Rare Minerals | 2d6 x 50 Credits | Highly sought after by the Gold Ring researchers |
| **7** | Progenitor Fragment | 1d4 x 500 Credits | Dangerous. Roll a d6: on 1-2, the fragment is active (see below) |
| **8** | Intact Relic-Tech Device | Special | A functional piece of Progenitor technology. The GM determines its properties |

### Progenitor Artifacts

The most valuable discoveries are Progenitor artifacts. These items are highly sought after by all major factions, particularly the Gold Ring scientists and the Kaelen Syndicate. However, they are also inherently dangerous, often emitting radiation or psychic resonance that can attract the Eclipse.

When the crew recovers a Progenitor artifact, the GM should secretly roll a d6. On a 1 or 2, the artifact is "active" and will begin to cause anomalous events aboard the crew's ship until it is properly contained or sold. Active artifacts attract Eclipse attention — the GM should advance the Eclipse faction clock by 1 segment for each active artifact the crew carries.

chapters.content:
While Nexus 9 is the political and social heart of the campaign, the galaxy is vast, dangerous, and full of ancient mysteries. When the crew leaves the safety of the station to explore the deep frontier, investigate derelict vessels, or navigate treacherous anomalies, the GM uses the **Exploration Procedure** to generate content, manage pacing, and track resources.

Exploration in *Nexus 9: The Fraying Dark* is not a hex-crawl; it is a narrative procedure focused on tension, discovery, and the slow drain of ship resources. The void is hostile, and every jump carries the risk of encountering pirates, Eclipse phenomena, or the remnants of the Progenitors.

### The Pillars of Exploration

When running an exploration sequence, the GM should focus on three core pillars:

1. **Tension:** The constant threat of the unknown. The void is silent, cold, and unforgiving. Every sensor ping could be a derelict, a pirate, or something far worse.
2. **Resource Management:** Fuel, hull integrity, and crew stress are finite resources that must be carefully managed. The crew cannot stay in the void forever.
3. **Discovery:** The reward for braving the dark. Ancient technology, hidden hyperspace lanes, lost colonies, and the truth about the Progenitors.

chapters.title: 3. The Station Event Generator

sections.title: Rolling a Station Event
sections.content:
Roll **1d12** to determine the category of the event, then roll **1d6** to determine the specific occurrence.

#### 1-3: Economic & Infrastructure Events
| d6 | Event | Effect on Play |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| **1** | **Supply Chain Disruption:** A major freighter convoy is delayed by Kaelen pirates. | Prices for all consumables and gear increase by 50% for the session. |
| **2** | **Docking Bay Strike:** The Stevedores' Union in the Red Ring goes on strike over hazardous working conditions. | The crew cannot access their ship or heavy cargo without negotiating, bribing, or sneaking past the picket line. |
| **3** | **Power Grid Fluctuation:** The Nexus Core experiences a micro-surge, causing rolling blackouts in the Grey and Green Rings. | All Agility and Finesse rolls in affected areas suffer Disadvantage due to poor lighting and failing gravity plating. |
| **4** | **Black Market Crackdown:** Commander Vance orders a massive sweep of the Underbelly. | Finding illegal goods or contacts requires a Difficulty 16 Street Smarts roll. Failure results in Coalition Security questioning. |
| **5** | **Tech Boom:** A new shipment of salvaged Progenitor tech floods the market. | The crew can purchase one Tier-appropriate Relic or Ship Upgrade at a 25% discount, but it may be defective or tracked. |
| **6** | **Quarantine Protocol:** A strange, highly contagious pathogen is detected in the Gold Ring. | The Gold Ring is sealed. Anyone entering or leaving must pass a Difficulty 15 Knowledge (Medicine) check or become infected (take 1 Stress per hour). |

#### 4-6: Diplomatic & Political Events
| d6 | Event | Effect on Play |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| **1** | **Aurelian State Visit:** A high-ranking Imperial Duke arrives, demanding luxury accommodations and absolute deference. | The Blue and Green Rings are flooded with Imperial Guards. Aurelian NPCs have Advantage on all social rolls. |
| **2** | **Valari Holy Day:** The station's Valari population observes a day of fasting and silent meditation. | Valari NPCs will not engage in commerce or combat unless attacked. The station is eerily quiet. |
| **3** | **Mindclave Audit:** Director Thorne initiates a station-wide "loyalty assessment" for all Terran Coalition personnel. | Terran NPCs are paranoid and uncooperative. Any telepathic activity draws immediate Mindclave attention. |
| **4** | **League Summit:** The League of Unaligned Worlds holds a chaotic, multi-day voting session in the Grand Assembly Hall. | The Blue Ring is impassable due to protests and security cordons. Rumors and misinformation run rampant. |
| **5** | **Syndicate Turf War:** Two rival Kaelen crime families openly clash in the Promenade. | The Green Ring is a war zone. Moving through it requires a Difficulty 14 Agility or Instinct roll to avoid stray crossfire (1d8 damage). |
| **6** | **Vanguard Leak:** A classified Vanguard dossier is leaked to the public, exposing a major faction's secret operations. | Choose one faction. Their reputation plummets, and their agents are actively hunted by rivals for the duration of the session. |

#### 7-9: Environmental & Cosmic Events
| d6 | Event | Effect on Play |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| **1** | **Solar Flare:** The local star emits a massive burst of radiation, interfering with station shielding. | All communications (commlinks, datapads) are scrambled. The crew must rely on face-to-face contact. |
| **2** | **Hyperspace Wake:** A massive, unidentified vessel drops out of hyperspace near the station, leaving a turbulent wake. | All ships attempting to dock or launch suffer Disadvantage on Piloting rolls. |
| **3** | **Micro-Meteoroid Shower:** A cloud of debris strikes the station's outer hull, causing localized breaches. | The Grey Ring experiences sudden decompression events. The crew must wear environmental suits or risk suffocation. |
| **4** | **Psychic Resonance:** A wave of unexplained telepathic static washes over the station, causing nightmares and headaches. | All characters (especially Mystics) start the session with 1 Stress. Telepathic abilities cost an additional 1 Stress to use. |
| **5** | **The Whisperer's Touch:** The Eclipse-Touched are active. A feeling of profound dread settles over a specific sector. | Choose one location. All Fear rolls made in that location generate 2 Fear instead of 1. |
| **6** | **Temporal Echo:** The station passes through a minor time-rift. | For one hour, characters experience intense déjà vu. Once per session, a player can force the GM to reroll a single die, claiming they "saw it happen already." |

#### 10-12: The Rumor Mill (Plot Hooks)
| d6 | Event | Effect on Play |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| **1** | **The Dead Drop:** A panicked informant shoves a data-chit into a PC's hand and runs, only to be gunned down moments later. | The chit contains coordinates to a hidden Eclipse cache or a corrupt official's blackmail file. |
| **2** | **The Missing Ship:** A Terran Coalition freighter vanished en route to Nexus 9, carrying vital medical supplies. | The crew is offered a bounty to find the ship. It was taken by Technoseers or Deep Space Horrors. |
| **3** | **The Defector:** A high-ranking Mindclave Hound is seeking asylum in the Valari embassy. | Both factions hire the crew—one to extract the defector, the other to silence them. |
| **4** | **The Ancient Signal:** The Nexus Core begins broadcasting a repeating, mathematical sequence that no one can decipher. | The crew must find a Progenitor Relic or a Technoseer to translate the signal before it attracts unwanted attention. |
| **5** | **The Smuggler's Debt:** Jax (or another Underbelly contact) calls in a favor. They need the crew to move a "hot" package off-station immediately. | The package is a living, breathing political prisoner or a dormant Eclipse-Corrupted entity. |
| **6** | **The Ghost of Aethelgard:** Sightings of a figure matching Aethelgard's description are reported in Grey Level 17. | The Vanguard mobilizes to investigate, but it might be a trap set by The Whisperer. |

---

chapters.content:
Nexus 9 is a city of millions, and it does not wait for the players to act. To make the station feel alive, the GM should roll on the **Station Event Generator** at the start of every session, or whenever the crew returns to the station after a long absence.

These events provide background flavor, alter the station's economy, or create immediate complications for the crew. They are the pulse of the station — reminding the players that they are part of a larger ecosystem that is constantly shifting in response to political pressure, criminal enterprise, and the looming threat of the Eclipse.

### When to Roll

Roll on the Station Event Generator in the following situations:

- **Session Start:** At the beginning of every session that takes place on Nexus 9.
- **Return from Mission:** Whenever the crew returns to the station after an extended absence (more than a few days).
- **Extended Downtime:** When the crew spends a week or more on the station between campaign chapters.
- **Dramatic Pacing:** Whenever the GM wants to inject a complication or shift the tone of the session.

### Stacking Events

If the crew has been away from the station for an extended period (e.g., a multi-session exploration arc), the GM should roll multiple events to represent the accumulated changes. Roll once for each week the crew was absent, applying the effects cumulatively. This creates a sense that the station has continued to evolve in their absence.

The **Triune Assembly** may also dispatch envoys during periods of heightened tension, adding a layer of spiritual authority to the political negotiations.

chapters.title: 4. The Faction Turn System

sections.title: The Core Mechanics
sections.content:
Each major faction (Terran Coalition, Aurelian Empire, Kaelen Syndicate, Valari Collective, Mindclave, and Eclipse) has three key attributes:

1.  **Force:** The faction's military might, security presence, and capacity for direct violence.
2.  **Cunning:** The faction's espionage network, technological edge, and ability to operate in the shadows.
3.  **Wealth:** The faction's economic power, diplomatic influence, and logistical resources.

These attributes are rated from 1 to 5. When a faction takes an action, the GM rolls a number of d12s equal to the relevant attribute. The highest die determines the outcome.

### Faction Goals and Clocks
Every faction has a **Primary Goal** (a long-term objective, such as "Annex the disputed border colonies" or "Awaken the Eclipse sleeper agents") and a **Secondary Goal** (a short-term objective, such as "Assassinate the Terran Ambassador" or "Secure a monopoly on Progenitor artifacts").

These goals are tracked using **Clocks**—circles divided into 4, 6, or 8 segments. When a faction successfully completes an action related to a goal, they fill in segments on the clock. When the clock is full, the goal is achieved, and the state of the galaxy changes significantly.

sections.title: Running the Faction Turn
sections.content:
The Faction Turn consists of three phases:

### Phase 1: Faction Actions
In initiative order (determined by the GM based on the current narrative focus), each faction takes one Action. The GM chooses the action that best aligns with the faction's current goals and resources.

**The Action Menu:**
*   **Assault (Force):** Attack a rival faction's asset, territory, or personnel. *Success:* Inflict damage, seize territory, or fill 2 segments on a military goal clock.
*   **Espionage (Cunning):** Steal secrets, sabotage infrastructure, or assassinate a key figure. *Success:* Gain valuable intelligence, disrupt a rival's clock, or fill 2 segments on a covert goal clock.
*   **Diplomacy/Commerce (Wealth):** Negotiate treaties, secure trade routes, or bribe officials. *Success:* Form an alliance, gain a new asset, or fill 2 segments on an economic/political goal clock.
*   **Consolidate (Any):** Fortify existing positions, recruit new assets, or recover from losses. *Success:* Increase the relevant attribute by 1 (maximum 5) for the next Faction Turn, or clear negative conditions.

**Resolving the Action:**
The GM rolls a number of d12s equal to the faction's relevant attribute.
*   **1-5 (Failure):** The action fails. The faction suffers a setback, loses an asset, or a rival faction gains an advantage.
*   **6-9 (Mixed Success):** The action succeeds, but with a cost or complication. Fill 1 segment on the goal clock, but the faction is exposed, loses resources, or provokes a retaliation.
*   **10-12 (Full Success):** The action succeeds perfectly. Fill 2 segments on the goal clock.
*   **Critical (Two 12s):** The action is a spectacular success. Fill 3 segments on the goal clock and gain a permanent new asset or advantage.

### Phase 2: Player Interference
The players are not passive observers. During their adventures, they will inevitably interfere with faction goals. If the players directly thwarted a faction's plans during the previous chapter, the GM should remove 1 or 2 segments from that faction's relevant goal clock. Conversely, if the players unwittingly aided a faction, the GM should add segments.

### Phase 3: The News Feed
The results of the Faction Turn are not kept secret from the players. The GM translates the mechanical outcomes into narrative events that the players hear about through the station's news feeds, rumors in the Grand Promenade, or direct briefings from their contacts.

*Example: If the Kaelen Syndicate successfully uses Espionage against the Terran Coalition, the players might hear a news report about a massive data breach at Coalition Defense Force Command, or they might notice increased security patrols in the Blue Ring.*

sections.title: Faction Attributes (Starting Values)
sections.content:
Each major faction has three key attributes: Force, Cunning, and Wealth. These attributes are rated from 1 to 5 and are used to resolve Faction Actions during the Faction Turn.

| Faction | Force | Cunning | Wealth | Starting Goal (Clock) |
| :--- | :---: | :---: | :---: | :--- |
| **Terran Coalition** | 4 | 2 | 3 | Secure the Red Ring (6-segment) |
| **Aurelian Empire** | 5 | 3 | 4 | Annex Border Colonies (8-segment) |
| **Kaelen Syndicate** | 2 | 4 | 5 | Monopolize Artifact Trade (6-segment) |
| **Valari Collective** | 3 | 5 | 2 | Awaken the Progenitor Vault (8-segment) |
| **The Mindclave** | 2 | 5 | 3 | Infiltrate the Grand Assembly (6-segment) |
| **The Eclipse** | 5 | 5 | 1 | Breach the Nexus 9 Defenses (10-segment) |

*Note: The Eclipse's Wealth is effectively 0, but they do not use traditional commerce. Their actions are almost entirely based on Force and Cunning.*

### Faction Goals and Clocks

Each faction begins the campaign with a **Goal Clock** — a segmented progress tracker that represents their primary strategic objective. When a faction successfully completes an action that advances their goal, the GM fills in one or more segments of the clock.

The number of segments determines the difficulty and scope of the goal. A 6-segment clock represents a moderate objective (securing a district, monopolizing a trade route). An 8-segment clock represents a major objective (annexing territory, awakening ancient technology). A 10-segment clock represents a campaign-defining objective (the Eclipse breaching the station's defenses).

When a clock is completed, the faction achieves its goal, and the consequences ripple across the entire campaign. The GM should then assign the faction a new goal based on the current narrative situation.

sections.title: Escalation and Consequences
sections.content:
As faction clocks fill, the tension on Nexus 9 escalates. The GM should use the progress of these clocks to drive the campaign narrative. If the Aurelian Empire's "Annex Border Colonies" clock reaches 6/8, the players should feel the imminent threat of war. Station security will tighten, prices for weapons will skyrocket, and diplomatic channels will break down.

When a major clock is completed, it should trigger a significant event that directly impacts the players — a terrorist attack on the station, a blockade of the primary hyperspace lane, or the assassination of a key NPC. The players should have the opportunity to intervene, but if they fail or choose not to act, the consequences are permanent.

### Escalation Triggers

| Clock Progress | Narrative Effect |
|:---|:---|
| **25% filled** | Rumors and whispers. NPCs mention the faction's activity in passing. Prices for related goods shift slightly. |
| **50% filled** | Open moves. The faction's actions become visible to the public. News broadcasts cover the situation. Other factions begin to react. |
| **75% filled** | Crisis point. The station is on edge. Security is heightened, curfews are imposed, and the crew is directly affected by the faction's actions. |
| **100% (completed)** | The faction achieves its goal. The consequences are immediate, dramatic, and permanent. The political landscape of the campaign shifts. |

### Example Consequences

- **Terran Coalition Secures the Red Ring:** Martial law is declared. All weapons must be registered, and random searches become common. The crew's ship is impounded if they lack proper permits. Reputation with the Terran Coalition matters more than ever.
- **Aurelian Empire Annexes Border Colonies:** War is imminent. The Aurelians begin fortifying their positions and demanding tribute from neutral stations. Nexus 9 becomes a staging ground for military operations.
- **Kaelen Syndicate Monopolizes Artifact Trade:** The price of Progenitor technology triples. The Syndicate gains a massive influx of Wealth, allowing them to bribe key officials and operate with impunity. The Gold Ring scientists are furious.
- **Valari Collective Awakens the Progenitor Vault:** A massive burst of psychic energy ripples across the galaxy. The Eclipse is drawn to the Vault like moths to a flame. The Valari gain access to ancient technology, but at a terrible cost.
- **The Mindclave Infiltrates the Grand Assembly:** Key political decisions begin to shift in the Mindclave's favor. Telepathic screening becomes mandatory for all Assembly delegates. Paranoia grips the station.
- **The Eclipse Breaches Defenses:** A section of the station is corrupted by the Fraying Dark. The area becomes a hazard zone, filled with Void-Spawn and psychic anomalies. The station's population panics. Evacuation procedures begin.

chapters.content:
In *Nexus 9: The Fraying Dark*, the major powers do not sit idle while the players adventure. The galaxy is a living, breathing ecosystem of political maneuvering, espionage, and military escalation. To simulate this shifting landscape, the GM uses the **Faction Turn System**.

The Faction Turn is a mechanical procedure run by the GM between major campaign chapters or during significant periods of downtime. It determines what the major factions are doing off-screen, how their goals advance, and how the balance of power on Nexus 9 changes. This system ensures that the world reacts to the players' actions and that the campaign narrative is driven by the conflicting agendas of the Terran Coalition, Aurelian Empire, Kaelen Syndicate, Valari Collective, Mindclave, and the Eclipse.

### Design Intent

The Faction Turn is not meant to be a complex wargame. It is a narrative tool that gives the GM a structured way to advance the world's political situation without improvising everything from scratch. Each turn should take no more than 10-15 minutes of prep time and produce 2-3 narrative hooks that the GM can weave into the next session.

### When to Run a Faction Turn

Run a Faction Turn in the following situations:

- **Between Campaign Chapters:** After each major chapter of the campaign concludes, run a Faction Turn to advance the political landscape.
- **Extended Downtime:** If the crew spends more than two weeks on the station or in deep space, run a Faction Turn to represent the passage of time.
- **Major Player Decision:** If the players make a decision that significantly impacts a faction (e.g., delivering a Progenitor artifact to the Aurelians, or assassinating a Syndicate boss), run an immediate Faction Turn to reflect the consequences.

chapters.title: 5. Using These Tools in Play
chapters.content:
These procedural tools are designed to reduce GM prep time and ensure that Nexus 9 feels like a living, dangerous place. They are not meant to replace the core narrative, but to support it — providing structure when the GM needs inspiration and mechanical weight when the players' decisions demand consequences.

### Tool Selection Guide

| Situation | Tool to Use | Frequency |
|:---|:---|:---|
| Crew is traveling between Rings | Ring Encounter Tables | As needed |
| Start of a new session on the station | Station Event Generator | Every session |
| Crew leaves the station to explore | Exploration Procedures | Every Exploration Turn |
| Between major campaign chapters | Faction Turn System | Every 2-4 sessions |
| Crew has extended downtime | Downtime Activities + Reputation | Between chapters |

### Session Prep Checklist

Before each session, the GM should complete the following steps:

1. **Review Faction Clocks.** Check the current progress of all active Faction Goal Clocks. Determine if any are close to completion and plan narrative foreshadowing accordingly.
2. **Roll a Station Event.** Use the Station Event Generator to determine the session's background context. This event should be mentioned in the opening narration.
3. **Update Reputation.** Based on the crew's actions in the previous session, adjust their Reputation with any affected factions.
4. **Prepare Encounters.** Based on the Rings the crew is likely to visit, pre-roll or select 2-3 encounters from the appropriate tables.
5. **Check Downtime.** If the crew has earned Extended Downtime, prepare the Downtime Activity options and any associated complications.

### Layering Tools

The real power of these tools comes from layering them. A Station Event ("Docking Bay Strike") combined with a Ring Encounter ("Syndicate Thugs shaking down refugees") creates a rich, interconnected narrative moment. The GM should look for opportunities to connect the outputs of different tools into a cohesive session experience.

For example: The Faction Turn reveals that the Kaelen Syndicate has advanced their "Monopolize Artifact Trade" clock. The Station Event Generator produces "Black Market Crackdown." The Ring Encounter Table produces "A street vendor selling fake Progenitor artifacts." Together, these three results paint a picture of a station where the Syndicate is tightening its grip on the artifact trade, Coalition Security is cracking down, and the crew is caught in the middle.

chapters.title: Downtime and Reputation
chapters.content:
## Life Between Missions

Not every moment in *Nexus 9* is spent dodging plasma fire or negotiating with hostile diplomats. Between major operations, the crew has time to pursue personal goals, build relationships, and establish their reputation across the station. This chapter provides structured rules for **Downtime Activities** and the **Reputation System** — two interconnected systems that reward players for engaging with the world beyond combat.

---

## Downtime Activities

Downtime occurs during **Long Rests** and between major campaign chapters. During a Long Rest, each character may perform **two Downtime Moves** (as listed in Book Four). Between campaign chapters, the GM may grant an **Extended Downtime** — a period of several days or weeks where each character may perform **four Downtime Moves** and one **Major Project**.

### Major Projects

Major Projects are long-term undertakings that require multiple sessions of downtime to complete. When a character begins a Major Project, the GM assigns it a **Progress Clock** with 4, 6, or 8 segments based on complexity. Each Extended Downtime spent working on the project fills one segment (or more, on a critical success).

| Major Project | Clock Size | Trait | Description |
|:---|:---:|:---|:---|
| **Build a Custom Weapon** | 6 | Knowledge | Design and fabricate a unique weapon with a custom Feature. Requires materials (500-2,000 Credits depending on Tier). |
| **Establish a Safe House** | 4 | Finesse or Presence | Secure a hidden location on the station where the crew can hide, store contraband, or meet contacts without surveillance. |
| **Cultivate an Informant** | 6 | Presence | Develop a relationship with an NPC who will provide regular intelligence on a specific faction's activities. |
| **Decrypt a Progenitor Archive** | 8 | Knowledge | Slowly decode an ancient data crystal, revealing fragments of Progenitor history and potentially a map to a hidden site. |
| **Train a Skill** | 4 | Any | Intensive practice. On completion, permanently increase one Experience by +1 (maximum +4). |
| **Repair or Upgrade the Ship** | 6 | Knowledge | Perform a major overhaul. On completion, install one Ship Upgrade from the Ship Upgrades table (Book Four) at half cost. |
| **Write a Manifesto** | 4 | Presence | Author a political document, religious text, or propaganda piece. On completion, gain +2 to Presence rolls when dealing with the target audience for one campaign chapter. |
| **Investigate a Conspiracy** | 8 | Instinct or Knowledge | Follow a thread of evidence across multiple leads. On completion, the GM reveals a major piece of campaign information that the crew would not otherwise have access to. |

### Downtime Complications

Downtime is not always peaceful. At the start of each Extended Downtime, the GM rolls **1d6** on the Downtime Complications table. On a 1-2, a complication arises that the crew must deal with before they can pursue their personal projects.

| d6 | Complication |
|:---:|:---|
| 1 | **Debt Collector:** Your Creditor sends a representative demanding an immediate payment or a dangerous favor. |
| 2 | **Unwanted Attention:** A faction has noticed the crew's recent activities. An agent approaches with a warning, a bribe, or a threat. |
| 3-6 | **No complication.** The downtime proceeds normally. |

---

## The Reputation System

As the crew operates on Nexus 9 and across the Known Galaxy, their actions build (or destroy) their reputation with the major factions. The Reputation System tracks how each faction views the crew and provides concrete mechanical benefits and consequences.

### Reputation Tracks

Each major faction has a **Reputation Track** ranging from **-5 (Hostile)** to **+5 (Allied)**. All factions start at **0 (Neutral)** unless the crew's backstory or Session Zero choices dictate otherwise.

| Reputation Score | Standing | Effect |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| -5 | **Kill on Sight** | The faction actively hunts the crew. Their agents attack without provocation. Bounties are posted. |
| -4 | **Hostile** | The faction refuses all dealings. Their territory is extremely dangerous for the crew. |
| -3 | **Antagonistic** | The faction works against the crew's interests. Prices are doubled; information is denied. |
| -2 | **Distrusted** | The faction is suspicious. Social rolls with their members suffer Disadvantage. |
| -1 | **Cool** | The faction is wary but will deal with the crew at arm's length. |
| 0 | **Neutral** | No special treatment. Standard prices and interactions. |
| +1 | **Recognized** | The faction knows the crew by name. Minor courtesies are extended. |
| +2 | **Respected** | The faction offers fair deals and occasional tips. Social rolls with their members gain +1. |
| +3 | **Trusted** | The faction shares intelligence and offers discounts (25% off goods and services). Access to restricted areas. |
| +4 | **Honored** | The faction treats the crew as valued allies. Military escort, safe houses, and priority docking are available. Social rolls gain Advantage. |
| +5 | **Allied** | The faction considers the crew part of their inner circle. They will commit significant resources to help the crew, including military support, political cover, and access to their most guarded secrets. |

### Gaining and Losing Reputation

Reputation shifts based on the crew's actions during play. The GM adjusts reputation at the end of each campaign chapter based on the following guidelines:

| Action | Reputation Change |
|:---|:---:|
| Complete a mission for the faction | +1 |
| Complete a critical mission that advances the faction's core goal | +2 |
| Save a faction leader's life or prevent a major disaster | +2 |
| Publicly support the faction in a political dispute | +1 |
| Betray the faction or sabotage their operations | -2 |
| Kill a faction member (even in self-defense) | -1 |
| Ally openly with the faction's primary rival | -1 |
| Expose the faction's secrets to the public or a rival | -2 |
| Refuse a direct request from a faction leader | -1 |

### Reputation and the Faction Turn

The crew's Reputation directly affects the Faction Turn system (Book Five). When a faction makes its Faction Turn roll:
- If the crew's Reputation with that faction is **+3 or higher**, the faction gains **Advantage** on its roll (as if the crew had helped them).
- If the crew's Reputation is **-3 or lower**, the faction gains **Advantage** on rolls that specifically target or harm the crew.

### Conflicting Reputations

It is nearly impossible to be Allied with every faction simultaneously. Gaining reputation with one faction often costs reputation with its rivals. The GM should enforce this tension:

| If You Gain Reputation With... | You May Lose Reputation With... |
|:---|:---|
| Terran Coalition | Kaelen Syndicate, sometimes Aurelian Empire |
| Aurelian Empire | Kaelen Syndicate, League of Unaligned Worlds |
| Kaelen Syndicate | Terran Coalition, Aurelian Empire |
| The Mindclave | The Vanguard, anyone who distrusts telepaths |
| The Valari | No automatic enemies, but the Aurelians may view closeness with suspicion |
| The Vanguard | The Mindclave, any faction whose secrets the Vanguard exposes |

This tension is intentional. The crew must make hard choices about who to support, and those choices have lasting consequences.

---

## Reputation on the Character Sheet

Track your crew's Reputation on the Ship Sheet (see Appendix). Each faction has a simple -5 to +5 track. Mark the current standing with a token or pencil. Update it at the end of each campaign chapter during the GM's Faction Turn.

sections.title: Reputation on the Character Sheet
sections.content:
Track your crew's Reputation on the Ship Sheet (see Appendix). Each faction has a simple -5 to +5 track. Mark the current standing with a token or pencil. Update it at the end of each campaign chapter during the GM's Faction Turn, or immediately following a major narrative decision by the players.

### How to Track Reputation

Reputation changes are cumulative and should be recorded at the end of each session or campaign chapter.

| Action | Reputation Change |
|:---|:---:|
| Completing a standard mission for a faction | +1 |
| Providing valuable intelligence or resources | +1 |
| Saving a high-ranking faction member | +2 |
| Delivering a critical asset (e.g., Progenitor artifact) | +2 |
| Completing a mission that significantly advances their goals | +2 |
| Failing a mission for a faction | -1 |
| Getting caught stealing from a faction | -1 |
| Publicly insulting a faction representative | -1 |
| Actively sabotaging a faction's operation | -2 |
| Killing a high-ranking faction member | -2 |
| Delivering a critical asset to their primary rival | -2 |

### Reputation Thresholds

| Range | Status | Effect |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| +4 to +5 | **Allied** | The faction provides significant resources, safe houses, and military support. The crew is considered part of the organization. Gear discounts: 50%. |
| +1 to +3 | **Friendly** | The faction offers discounts on gear (25%), access to restricted areas, and minor favors. |
| 0 | **Neutral** | The faction treats the crew as independent contractors. No special benefits or penalties. |
| -1 to -3 | **Hostile** | The faction actively works against the crew, increasing prices by 50%, denying access to their territory, and occasionally sending thugs to harass them. |
| -4 to -5 | **Enemy** | The faction has a kill-on-sight order for the crew. They will send elite operatives and assassins to eliminate them. The crew cannot enter faction-controlled territory without disguise. |

### Cross-Faction Dynamics

Reputation with one faction can affect your standing with others. The major rivalries are:

- **Terran Coalition vs. Kaelen Syndicate:** Gaining +2 or more with one automatically costs -1 with the other.
- **Aurelian Empire vs. Valari Collective:** Gaining +2 or more with one automatically costs -1 with the other.
- **The Mindclave vs. Everyone:** The Mindclave's reputation is tracked separately and does not automatically affect other factions, but publicly allying with them makes all other factions suspicious (-1 to all others if Mindclave reaches +4).
- **The Eclipse:** The Eclipse does not have a traditional reputation track. Any crew member who interacts with Eclipse entities gains **Corruption** instead (see Book Six: Threats from the Dark).

chapters.title: 6. Session Zero & Campaign Setup
chapters.content:
Before the first session of *Nexus 9: The Fraying Dark*, the GM should run a **Session Zero** — a dedicated session focused on establishing the campaign's tone, creating characters, and building the crew's shared history. Session Zero is not optional; it is the foundation upon which the entire campaign is built.

### The Purpose of Session Zero

Session Zero accomplishes three critical goals:

1. **Safety and Expectations.** The group establishes boundaries, discusses content warnings, and agrees on the tone of the campaign. *Nexus 9* deals with themes of war, political corruption, psychic violation, and existential dread. Not every player will be comfortable with every theme, and that is perfectly fine. Use safety tools like the X-Card, Lines and Veils, or the Script Change framework to ensure everyone is on the same page.

2. **Character and Crew Creation.** The players create their characters together, ensuring that the crew has a balanced mix of skills and that each character has a personal connection to at least one other crew member. The crew should also establish their **Crew Concept** — a one-sentence description of who they are and what they do (e.g., "We are freelance couriers who take any job that pays, no questions asked" or "We are a Terran Coalition black-ops team operating without official sanction").

3. **Campaign Calibration.** The GM presents the campaign's premise and the players discuss what kind of stories they want to tell. Do they want a politically focused campaign centered on the Grand Assembly? A military campaign focused on the escalating war? A mystery campaign focused on the Progenitors and the Eclipse? The answer will shape which factions, NPCs, and locations the GM emphasizes.

### The Crew Sheet

During Session Zero, the group fills out the **Crew Sheet**, which tracks:

- **Crew Name:** What does the crew call themselves?
- **Crew Concept:** A one-sentence description of the crew's identity and purpose.
- **Ship Name and Frame:** The crew's starting ship (see Book Four).
- **Home Base:** Where does the crew live on Nexus 9? A rented berth in the Red Ring? A converted cargo bay in the Grey Ring? A luxury suite in the Blue Ring?
- **Faction Ties:** Each crew member chooses one faction they have a positive history with (+1 Reputation) and one they have a negative history with (-1 Reputation).
- **Shared Secret:** The crew has one shared secret — something they all know that binds them together. This could be a crime they committed, a person they saved, or a discovery they made.

### Campaign Tone Dials

The GM should present the following tone dials and let the group set them:

| Dial | Low Setting | High Setting |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Political Intrigue** | Background noise; factions are distant | Central focus; every NPC has an agenda |
| **Military Action** | Rare; combat is a last resort | Frequent; the crew is on the front lines |
| **Horror / Eclipse** | Slow burn; the Eclipse is a distant threat | Pervasive; the Fraying Dark is everywhere |
| **Personal Drama** | Light; focus on missions | Heavy; relationships and backstories drive the plot |
| **Humor** | Serious; gallows humor only | Frequent; the crew jokes in the face of danger |

These dials are not fixed. The GM should revisit them every few sessions to ensure the campaign is still aligned with the group's preferences.

chapters.title: 7. NPC Relationship Tracker
chapters.content:
Nexus 9 is populated by hundreds of named NPCs, but only a handful will become truly important to the crew. The **NPC Relationship Tracker** is a simple tool that helps the GM track how key NPCs feel about the crew and how those relationships evolve over time.

### How It Works

For each recurring NPC, the GM tracks two things:

1. **Disposition:** A simple scale from -3 (Hostile) to +3 (Devoted). This represents the NPC's current attitude toward the crew.
2. **Loyalty Clock:** A 4-segment clock that represents the NPC's willingness to take risks for the crew. When the clock is full, the NPC will go to extraordinary lengths to help the crew — even at great personal cost.

### Disposition Scale

| Value | Disposition | Behavior |
|:---:|:---|:---|
| -3 | **Hostile** | Actively works against the crew. Will betray them if given the opportunity. |
| -2 | **Antagonistic** | Opposes the crew's goals. Will not help them under any circumstances. |
| -1 | **Suspicious** | Distrusts the crew. Will only cooperate if there is a clear personal benefit. |
| 0 | **Neutral** | No strong feelings. Will cooperate if the terms are fair. |
| +1 | **Friendly** | Likes the crew. Will offer minor favors and share non-sensitive information. |
| +2 | **Loyal** | Trusts the crew deeply. Will take moderate risks on their behalf. |
| +3 | **Devoted** | Would die for the crew. Will sacrifice personal safety, reputation, or resources to protect them. |

### Changing Disposition

Disposition changes based on the crew's actions toward the NPC and the NPC's faction:

- **+1 Disposition:** Saving the NPC from danger, completing a personal favor, or supporting their faction's goals.
- **-1 Disposition:** Failing to protect the NPC, betraying their trust, or acting against their faction.
- **+2 Disposition:** A dramatic, selfless act that directly benefits the NPC (e.g., risking the crew's lives to rescue them from an Eclipse attack).
- **-2 Disposition:** A dramatic betrayal (e.g., selling the NPC out to a rival faction, or allowing them to be captured when the crew could have intervened).

### Loyalty Clocks

Loyalty Clocks fill when the crew consistently supports an NPC over time. Each time the crew does something significant for the NPC (beyond simple transactions), the GM fills one segment of their Loyalty Clock.

When a Loyalty Clock is full, the NPC becomes a **True Ally**. True Allies will:
- Provide critical intelligence at personal risk.
- Intervene in combat on the crew's behalf.
- Use their faction connections to shield the crew from consequences.
- Sacrifice their own safety to protect a crew member.

However, if the crew betrays a True Ally, the consequences are severe. The NPC's Disposition drops to -3 immediately, and they become a recurring antagonist with intimate knowledge of the crew's weaknesses.

chapters.title: 8. Quick Reference: GM Procedures
chapters.content:
This page summarizes all GM procedures from Book Five in a single reference. Print this page or keep it open during play.

### Session Start
1. Roll a **Station Event** (d12 category, then d6 specific).
2. Narrate the event as part of the session opening.
3. Check **Faction Clocks** for any approaching thresholds.

### During Play (On-Station)
- When the crew travels between Rings: Roll a **d12** on the appropriate **Ring Encounter Table**.
- When a player fails a roll with Fear: Consider introducing a Ring Encounter as a complication.
- When the crew interacts with a faction: Update **Reputation** immediately if the action is significant.

### During Play (Off-Station)
- Each **Exploration Turn** (4-6 hours): Assign crew roles (Helm, Sensors, Engineering).
- Make a **Survey Roll** (2d12 + Instinct vs. DC 12/15/18).
- On encounter: Roll a **d12** on the **Deep Space Encounter Table**.
- Track **Fuel Cells** after every jump, evasive maneuver, or extended operation.
- On salvage: Make a **Salvage Roll** (Intellect or Agility), then roll on the **Salvage Value Table**.

### Between Sessions
- Run a **Faction Turn** if a campaign chapter has concluded or significant time has passed.
- Each faction takes one **Action** (Assault, Espionage, Diplomacy, or Consolidate).
- Roll 2d12 + Attribute vs. DC 12 to resolve each action.
- Advance **Faction Goal Clocks** based on results.
- Grant **Extended Downtime** if appropriate (4 Downtime Moves + 1 Major Project per character).

### Key DCs
| Difficulty | DC |
|:---|:---:|
| Easy | 10 |
| Standard | 12 |
| Hard | 15 |
| Very Hard | 18 |
| Nearly Impossible | 21 |

books.title: BOOK SIX: THREATS FROM THE DARK

sections.title: Reading Adversary Stat Blocks
sections.content:
> **GM Note — Reading Adversary Stat Blocks:** An adversary's **Difficulty** is the target number PCs must meet or exceed on their action roll to hit the adversary — it functions identically to a PC's Evasion. **Adversary Stress** is a resource pool, not a damage track. Adversaries spend Stress to activate their Features. When an adversary's Stress reaches 0, they can no longer use Stress-costing Features. PCs can force adversaries to mark Stress through certain abilities, reducing the adversary's tactical options. **Advantage and Disadvantage:** When a creature has Advantage, roll an additional d6 and add it to the total. When a creature has Disadvantage, roll an additional d6 and subtract it from the total.

---

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The Kaelen Syndicate is a brutal, pragmatic organization born from centuries of Aurelian occupation. They fight with salvaged gear, overwhelming aggression, and a complete disregard for "honorable" combat. Their forces are a mix of desperate revolutionaries and hardened criminals.

sections.title: Tier 1: Syndicate Enforcer
sections.content:
*The muscle of the Kaelen criminal empire, armed with cheap kinetic weapons and bad attitudes.*
*   **Type:** Minion
*   **Difficulty:** 10
*   **Attack Modifier:** +2
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Minor 3 / Major 6 / Severe 12
*   **Hit Points:** 3
*   **Stress:** 2
*   **Experience:** Intimidation (+2)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Scrap-Pistol* (Close range, 1d8+1 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Strength in Numbers.* When attacking a target that is engaged in Melee with another Syndicate ally, the Enforcer gains Advantage on the attack roll.
*   **GM Tactics:** Enforcers are cowards when alone but vicious in packs. They will always try to surround a single PC to trigger their passive feature.

sections.title: Tier 1: Syndicate Fixer
sections.content:
*The mid-level bosses who coordinate smuggling rings and extortion rackets.*
*   **Type:** Support
*   **Difficulty:** 12
*   **Attack Modifier:** +3
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Minor 4 / Major 7 / Severe 14
*   **Hit Points:** 4
*   **Stress:** 3
*   **Experience:** Street Smarts (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Concealed Blade* (Melee range, 1d6+2 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Mark the Target.* The Fixer spends 1 Stress to designate a PC. All Syndicate allies gain +2 to their attack rolls against that PC until the end of the round.
*   **GM Tactics:** Fixers stay behind cover, directing Enforcers to focus fire on the party's weakest member.

sections.title: Tier 2: Syndicate Breacher
sections.content:
*Heavily armored shock troops used for boarding actions and breaking stalemates.*
*   **Type:** Bruiser
*   **Difficulty:** 13
*   **Attack Modifier:** +5
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Minor 6 / Major 12 / Severe 20
*   **Hit Points:** 6
*   **Stress:** 4
*   **Experience:** Close Quarters Combat (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Heavy Shotgun* (Close range, 2d8+3 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Concussion Grenade.* The Breacher spends 2 Stress to throw a grenade. All PCs within Close range of the target point must make a Difficulty 13 Agility roll or take 2d6 physical damage and become Disoriented until the end of their next turn.
*   **GM Tactics:** Breachers lead the charge, using grenades to disrupt enemy formations before closing in for devastating shotgun blasts.

sections.title: Tier 3: Syndicate "Cleaner"
sections.content:
*A high-end assassin sent to erase problems that the Fixers couldn't handle.*
*   **Type:** Skulk
*   **Difficulty:** 17
*   **Attack Modifier:** +8
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Minor 6 / Major 12 / Severe 22
*   **Hit Points:** 8
*   **Stress:** 6
*   **Experience:** Master of Disguise (+4)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Silenced Sniper Rifle* (Very Far range, 2d12+5 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Unseen.* The Cleaner cannot be targeted by attacks or abilities if they are in cover or darkness, unless a PC first succeeds on a Difficulty 16 Instinct roll to locate them.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Vanish.* The Cleaner spends 1 Stress to immediately move up to Far range and enter stealth, ending any effects that require line of sight.
*   **GM Tactics:** Cleaners never fight fair. They shoot from maximum range, use *Vanish* to reposition, and target medics or support characters first.

sections.title: Tier 4: Syndicate Crime Lord (Boss)
sections.content:
*The untouchable head of a Kaelen cartel, fighting with expensive tech and disposable bodyguards.*
*   **Type:** Boss
*   **Difficulty:** 18
*   **Attack Modifier:** +8
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Minor 10 / Major 20 / Severe 35
*   **Hit Points:** 12
*   **Stress:** 8
*   **Experience:** Always Has an Escape Plan (+5)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Custom Hand-Cannon* (Far range, 3d10+6 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Meat Shields.* As long as the Crime Lord has at least one allied Minion or Standard adversary within Close range, the Crime Lord's Difficulty increases by +2.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Call in the Boys.* The GM spends 2 Fear to summon 1d4+1 Syndicate Enforcers (Tier 1 Minions) into empty spaces within Far range.
*   **Feature (Reaction):** *Rigged to Blow.* When the Crime Lord is reduced to 0 HP, they can spend 1 Fear to detonate hidden explosives. All PCs within Close range take 4d8 physical damage, and the Crime Lord escapes in the chaos.
*   **GM Tactics:** The Crime Lord never fights fair. They surround themselves with Enforcers to boost their Difficulty, shoot from a distance, and use *Rigged to Blow* to ensure they survive to fight another day.

---

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The Aurelian Empire fights with the arrogance of a civilization that believes it is destined to rule. Their troops are highly disciplined, equipped with top-tier energy weapons, and supported by devastating artillery.

sections.title: Tier 1: Aurelian Legionnaire
sections.content:
*Highly disciplined, heavily armored shock troops of the Empire.*
*   **Type:** Standard
*   **Difficulty:** 13
*   **Attack Modifier:** +4
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Minor 5 / Major 9 / Severe 16
*   **Hit Points:** 5
*   **Stress:** 4
*   **Experience:** Military Tactics (+2)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Mag-Rifle* (Far range, 1d10+2 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Reaction):** *Phalanx Formation.* When an adjacent Aurelian ally is attacked, the Legionnaire can spend 1 Stress to force the attacker to roll with Disadvantage.
*   **GM Tactics:** Legionnaires fight in tight formations, using *Phalanx Formation* to protect each other while laying down disciplined volley fire.

sections.title: Tier 2: Aurelian Centurion
sections.content:
*Veteran squad leaders who inspire their troops and coordinate complex maneuvers.*
*   **Type:** Leader
*   **Difficulty:** 14
*   **Attack Modifier:** +5
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Minor 6 / Major 11 / Severe 19
*   **Hit Points:** 6
*   **Stress:** 5
*   **Experience:** Command Presence (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Plasma Pistol* (Far range, 1d8+3 energy damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Hold the Line.* The Centurion spends 2 Stress. All Aurelian allies within Far range clear 1 Stress and gain +1 to their Difficulty until the end of the round.
*   **GM Tactics:** Centurions prioritize keeping their Legionnaires alive and effective, using *Hold the Line* when the party starts dealing heavy damage.

sections.title: Tier 2: Imperial Infiltrator
sections.content:
*Aurelian stealth operatives used for political assassinations and sabotage.*
*   **Type:** Skulk
*   **Difficulty:** 15
*   **Attack Modifier:** +6
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Minor 5 / Major 10 / Severe 18
*   **Hit Points:** 6
*   **Stress:** 5
*   **Experience:** Stealth and Silence (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Poisoned Dagger* (Melee range, 1d6+2 physical damage plus 1d6 energy damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Active Camouflage.* The Infiltrator is invisible while moving. A PC must succeed on a Difficulty 15 Instinct roll to target them with a ranged attack.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Throat Slit.* The Infiltrator spends 2 Stress to make a Standard Attack against an Exposed target. If it hits, the target takes an additional 2d6 physical damage and becomes Disoriented until the end of their next turn.
*   **GM Tactics:** Infiltrators bypass the frontline entirely, using *Active Camouflage* to reach the party's support characters, then using *Throat Slit* to shut them down.

sections.title: Tier 3: Aurelian Praetorian Guard
sections.content:
*The Emperor's personal guard, clad in powered armor and wielding relic-tech weapons.*
*   **Type:** Solo
*   **Difficulty:** 16
*   **Attack Modifier:** +7
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Minor 9 / Major 18 / Severe 32
*   **Hit Points:** 12
*   **Stress:** 8
*   **Experience:** Relentless Pursuit (+4)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Plasma Halberd* (Melee range, 3d10+6 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Powered Armor.* The Praetorian reduces all incoming physical damage by 5 before comparing it to their thresholds.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Overwhelming Strike.* The Praetorian spends 2 Stress to make a Standard Attack. If it hits, the target is knocked Prone and must mark 1 Stress.
*   **Feature (Reaction):** *Deflect.* When targeted by a ranged attack, the Praetorian can spend 1 Fear to roll a d20. On a 15 or higher, the attack is deflected and deals no damage.
*   **GM Tactics:** A Praetorian is a walking tank. They march straight into the center of the party, relying on their armor and *Deflect* to survive while crushing the biggest threat with their halberd.

sections.title: Tier 4: Imperial Dreadnought (Vehicle/Boss)
sections.content:
*A massive, heavily armed walker deployed only when a settlement needs to be erased.*
*   **Type:** Solo
*   **Difficulty:** 18
*   **Attack Modifier:** +9
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Minor 13 / Major 25 / Severe 45
*   **Hit Points:** 15
*   **Stress:** 10
*   **Experience:** Siege Warfare (+5)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Twin Rotary Cannons* (Far range, 4d8+8 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Heavy Plating.* The Dreadnought is immune to all physical damage from Tier 1 and Tier 2 weapons.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Orbital Bombardment.* The GM spends 3 Fear. The Dreadnought paints a target area. At the start of the GM's next turn, all creatures within Far range of that point take 5d10 energy damage.
*   **GM Tactics:** The Dreadnought is a slow, inevitable force of destruction. It uses *Orbital Bombardment* to force the party out of cover, then mows them down with its cannons.

---

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The Mindclave uses psychological warfare, telepathic domination, and specialized enforcement agents to maintain their monopoly on human telepaths. They rarely fight fair, preferring to break their enemies' minds before a single shot is fired.

sections.title: Tier 1: Mindclave Hound
sections.content:
*Low-level telepaths (RI-1-RI-3) trained to hunt down rogues and dissenters.*
*   **Type:** Skulk
*   **Difficulty:** 11
*   **Attack Modifier:** +3
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Minor 3 / Major 5 / Severe 10
*   **Hit Points:** 3
*   **Stress:** 3
*   **Experience:** Tracking (+2)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Stun Baton* (Melee range, 1d6+1 physical damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Surface Scan.* The Hound always knows the exact location of any PC within Close range, even if they are invisible or in stealth.
*   **GM Tactics:** Hounds use their *Surface Scan* to flush out hiding PCs, then close in to stun them for capture.

sections.title: Tier 2: Mindclave Inquisitor
sections.content:
*Telepathic interrogators who use psychic pressure to break their enemies.*
*   **Type:** Support
*   **Difficulty:** 14
*   **Attack Modifier:** +5
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Minor 5 / Major 10 / Severe 18
*   **Hit Points:** 6
*   **Stress:** 5
*   **Experience:** Psychological Warfare (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Neural Whip* (Close range, 1d8+3 psychic damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Mind Spike.* The Inquisitor spends 2 Stress to force a PC within Far range to make a Difficulty 14 Presence roll. On a failure, the PC marks 2 Stress and becomes Exposed until the end of their next turn.
*   **GM Tactics:** Inquisitors stay out of melee, using *Mind Spike* to soften up targets for their Hounds or allied mercenaries.

sections.title: Tier 3: Mindclave Handler
sections.content:
*High-level telepaths (RI-7-RI-9) who control squads of dominated thralls.*
*   **Type:** Leader
*   **Difficulty:** 16
*   **Attack Modifier:** +7
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Minor 7 / Major 14 / Severe 26
*   **Hit Points:** 9
*   **Stress:** 7
*   **Experience:** Puppet Master (+4)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Psychic Blast* (Far range, 2d10+4 psychic damage)
*   **Feature (Action):** *Command Thrall.* The Handler spends 1 Stress to command an allied Minion or Standard adversary to immediately make a Standard Attack as a Free Action.
*   **Feature (Reaction):** *Meat Shield.* When the Handler is attacked, they can spend 1 Fear to force an adjacent allied Minion to take the damage instead.
*   **GM Tactics:** Handlers surround themselves with dominated thugs (use Syndicate Enforcer stats), using them as disposable weapons and shields.

sections.title: Tier 3: Mindclave Eradicator
sections.content:
*A telekinetic specialist trained to crush armor and throw heavy objects.*
*   **Type:** Bruiser
*   **Difficulty:** 16
*   **Attack Modifier:** +7
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Minor 8 / Major 16 / Severe 30
*   **Hit Points:** 10
*   **Stress:** 6
*   **Experience:** Telekinetic Mastery (+4)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Kinetic Crush* (Close range, 3d8+4 psychic damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Kinetic Shield.* The Eradicator reduces all incoming physical damage by 3.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Telekinetic Throw.* The Eradicator spends 2 Stress to hurl a heavy object (or a PC) up to Far range. The target takes 2d10 physical damage and is knocked Prone.
*   **GM Tactics:** Eradicators are the Mindclave's heavy hitters. They use *Telekinetic Throw* to disrupt the party's positioning and isolate targets for their Hounds to finish off.

sections.title: Tier 4: Mindclave High Councilor (Boss)
sections.content:
*The supreme leader of the telepathic faction, capable of dominating entire crowds.*
*   **Type:** Solo
*   **Difficulty:** 19
*   **Attack Modifier:** +9
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Minor 9 / Major 18 / Severe 32
*   **Hit Points:** 12
*   **Stress:** 12
*   **Experience:** Absolute Control (+5)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Psychic Crush* (Far range, 3d12+6 psychic damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Mental Fortress.* The High Councilor is immune to all psychic damage and psychic effects.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Dominate.* The High Councilor spends 4 Stress to force a PC to make a Difficulty 19 Presence roll. On a failure, the GM controls that PC's next turn.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Psychic Scream.* The GM spends 3 Fear. All PCs within Very Far range take 4d8 psychic damage and become Disoriented until the end of their next turn.
*   **GM Tactics:** The High Councilor is a terrifying opponent who turns the party against itself. They use *Dominate* on the party's heaviest hitter, then unleash *Psychic Scream* when surrounded.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The Valari fight with a combination of religious fervor, biological augmentation, and ancient relic-tech. Their warriors are terrifyingly fast, their priests wield devastating energy weapons, and their bio-constructs are nightmarish.

sections.title: Tier 1: Valari Blade-Initiate
sections.content:
*Young warriors undergoing their first pilgrimage, armed with traditional energy blades.*
*   **Type:** Standard
*   **Difficulty:** 12
*   **Attack Modifier:** +3
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Minor 4 / Major 7 / Severe 14
*   **Hit Points:** 4
*   **Stress:** 3
*   **Experience:** Ritual Combat (+2)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Resonance Blade* (Melee range, 1d8+2 energy damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Zealous Charge.* The Blade-Initiate can move up to 2 range bands as part of a Standard Attack action, instead of the normal 1.
*   **GM Tactics:** Blade-Initiates close distance immediately, bypassing the party's ranged characters to engage in melee. They fight in pairs, flanking a single target.

sections.title: Tier 2: Valari Blademaster
sections.content:
*Veteran warriors who have completed their pilgrimage and earned the right to wield a master-crafted blade.*
*   **Type:** Solo
*   **Difficulty:** 15
*   **Attack Modifier:** +6
*   **Damage Thresholds:** Minor 6 / Major 12 / Severe 22
*   **Hit Points:** 7
*   **Stress:** 5
*   **Experience:** Evasion Mastery (+3)
*   **Standard Attack:** *Twin Resonance Blades* (Melee range, 2d8+4 energy damage)
*   **Feature (Passive):** *Evasion Mastery.* The Blademaster's Difficulty increases by +2 against ranged attacks.
*   **Feature (Action):** *Whirlwind Strike.* The Blademaster spends 2 Stress to attack all PCs within Melee range with a single attack roll.
*   **GM Tactics:** Blademasters are dueling specialists. They use their enhanced ranged defense to close distance safely, then use *Whirlwind Strike* when surrounded.

sections.title: Tier 2: Valari Bio-Construct
sections.content:
