The combined fleets of the Terran Coalition, Aurelian Empire, Kaelen Syndicate, and Valari Collective launch a desperate, all-out assault on the gate. The crew must lead the charge, using the awakened Nexus 9 station itself as a weapon to destroy the gate and end the Eclipse threat once and for all.

> *"This is it. The sky is a chaotic tapestry of plasma fire, exploding ships, and the terrifying, silent bulk of the Eclipse world-killers. The comms channel is a cacophony of desperate orders and dying screams. But beneath it all, you feel the hum of Nexus 9. The station is ready. The galaxy is watching. It's time to close the door."*

### GM Summary

This is the epic conclusion to the campaign. The crew must coordinate the massive fleet battle, pilot Nexus 9 into position, and unleash the station's full Progenitor arsenal to destroy the Eclipse gate. This chapter requires high-level tactical thinking, massive sacrifices, and the culmination of all the choices the crew has made throughout the campaign.

### Core Conflict

The Eclipse world-killers are too powerful for the conventional fleets to destroy. The crew must use Nexus 9 to ram or fire upon the gate, but doing so will likely destroy the station and everyone on it. They must find a way to evacuate the civilian population while holding the line.

### Encounter Sequence

**Scene 1: The Fleet Engagement**
The crew must coordinate the allied fleets to punch a hole through the Eclipse defensive line.
- **Starship Combat:** The crew pilots their ship (or commands a capital ship) against 5x **Eclipse Dreadnoughts** (Tier 4). The crew can use the *Command* action to direct allied ships to focus fire or provide cover.

**Scene 2: The Evacuation**
The crew must ensure the civilian population of Nexus 9 is evacuated before the station is used as a weapon.
- **Social Challenge:** The crew must accumulate 6 successful Presence or Instinct rolls to coordinate the chaotic evacuation. The GM gains 1 Fear for every failed roll, representing civilian transports being destroyed by Eclipse fighters.

**Scene 3: The Final Strike**
The crew takes control of Nexus 9's Progenitor systems and targets the Eclipse gate.
- **Combat:** The crew must defend the station's control room from the **Eclipse Prime** (Tier 4 Legendary Boss), the physical manifestation of the Eclipse hive-mind. The Prime uses *Reality Tear* to alter the battlefield and inflict massive damage.
- **Objective:** The crew must accumulate 8 successful Knowledge or Finesse rolls to align the station's weapons and fire on the gate.

### Key Choices

- **The Ultimate Sacrifice:** Firing the station's weapons will destroy the gate, but the feedback will shatter Nexus 9. The crew must decide who stays behind to manually trigger the firing sequence. This is a guaranteed lethal action. A crew member can volunteer, or they can convince a major NPC (like Commander Vance or the Prime Architect) to do it.

### Chapter-End Consequences

The Eclipse gate is destroyed, and the world-killers are severed from their power source, rendering them inert. The Eclipse armada is broken, and the galaxy is saved. However, Nexus 9 is destroyed, and the political landscape of the galaxy is forever changed.

### Epilogue

The surviving factions gather in the debris field of Nexus 9. The fragile peace has been shattered, but a new, stronger alliance has been forged in the crucible of stars. The crew are hailed as heroes, their names etched into the history of the known galaxy. The Fraying Dark has been held back, for now.

---
*(End of Campaign)*

chapters.title: Conditions Quick Reference
chapters.content:
## Active Conditions

Conditions are specific debuffs applied by adversary abilities, environmental hazards, Domain cards, or failed rolls. A character can suffer multiple conditions simultaneously. Unless otherwise stated, conditions do not stack — you are either affected or you are not.

---

### Combat Conditions

| Condition | Effect | How to Clear |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Pinned Down** | You cannot move from your current position. You have Disadvantage on attack rolls. | Spend 1 Hope; or an ally provides covering fire (uses their action); or the source of suppression is eliminated. |
| **Exposed** | Your Evasion is reduced by 2. Enemies have Advantage on attacks against you. | Move to cover (requires movement); or an ally uses a shielding ability. |
| **Restrained** | You cannot move. You have Disadvantage on Agility and Finesse rolls. Attacks against you have Advantage. | Succeed on a Strength roll (Difficulty 13); or an ally frees you (uses their action). |
| **Blinded** | You have Disadvantage on all rolls that require sight. You cannot make attacks beyond Very Close range. | Clears at the end of the scene; or when the source is removed (e.g., flash grenade effect fades). |
| **Disoriented** | You have Disadvantage on Knowledge and Instinct rolls. You cannot use telepathic abilities. | Clears at the end of your next action; or when an ally spends 1 Hope to steady you. |
| **Prone** | You have Disadvantage on attack rolls. Melee attacks against you have Advantage; ranged attacks beyond Close range have Disadvantage. | Stand up as part of your movement (costs half your movement for the turn). |

### Lasting Conditions

| Condition | Effect | How to Clear |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Wounded** | Your damage thresholds are reduced by 2 at all levels (Minor, Major, and Severe). | Long Rest; or a Medic uses Combat Triage or equivalent healing ability. |
| **Compromised** | Your communications are being monitored. Any plans you discuss openly are known to the enemy. | Succeed on a Knowledge roll (Difficulty 15) to sweep for bugs; or change your comm frequency (requires an Engineer). |
| **Irradiated** | You mark 1 Stress at the start of each scene until the condition is cleared. Healing effects restore 1 less HP. | Medical treatment during a Long Rest (Medic, Difficulty 15); or a specialized antidote. |
| **Corrupted (Eclipse)** | You hear whispers at the edge of your perception. Once per scene, the GM may force you to reroll a successful action roll and take the lower result. | Requires a Mystic's intervention (Difficulty 17) or a Valari purification ritual. Cannot be cleared by rest alone. |

### Environmental Conditions

These conditions affect everyone in a specific area, not individual characters.

| Condition | Effect | How to Clear |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Zero Gravity** | All movement costs double. Melee attacks have Disadvantage unless you have magnetic boots or zero-G training. Ranged attacks are unaffected. | Restore gravity (Engineering action); or equip magnetic boots. |
| **Vacuum Exposure** | Characters without sealed suits mark 1 HP and 1 Stress at the start of each turn. After 3 turns, they fall unconscious. | Seal the breach (Engineering, Difficulty 15); or reach a pressurized area. |
| **Low Visibility** | All attacks beyond Close range have Disadvantage. Sensor-based detection replaces visual detection. | Restore lighting; deploy flares; or use thermal/night vision equipment. |
| **Hazardous Atmosphere** | Characters without respirators mark 1 Stress at the start of each turn. After 5 turns of exposure, they gain the Disoriented condition. | Equip respirators; vent the area (Engineering action); or evacuate. |

---

## Condition Interactions

Some conditions interact with each other in important ways:

- A character who is both **Pinned Down** and **Exposed** is in extreme danger — they cannot move, their Evasion is reduced, and enemies have Advantage. Allies should prioritize freeing them.
- A character who is **Restrained** in **Zero Gravity** cannot attempt the Strength roll to break free unless they have something solid to brace against (GM discretion).
- **Corrupted** characters who also become **Disoriented** hear the Eclipse whispers more clearly. The GM may offer them a dark bargain: clear the Disoriented condition immediately, but mark 1 additional Stress.

---

## Advantage and Disadvantage

Several conditions reference Advantage and Disadvantage. As a reminder:

**Advantage:** Roll 3d12 instead of 2d12. Keep the two highest dice. Then determine Hope/Fear as normal.

**Disadvantage:** Roll 3d12 instead of 2d12. Keep the two lowest dice. Then determine Hope/Fear as normal.

Advantage and Disadvantage cancel each other out. If you have both, roll normally (2d12).

chapters.title: How to Play: Quickstart Rules
chapters.content:
> *Everything you need to sit down and start playing Nexus 9: The Fraying Dark in fifteen minutes.*

---

## What Is This Game?

Nexus 9: The Fraying Dark is a tabletop roleplaying game for 3–6 players and one Game Master (GM). One player runs the world — its factions, threats, and mysteries — while the others play a crew of operatives stationed aboard a massive space station at the crossroads of four rival civilizations. You will negotiate, fight, investigate, fly ships, and uncover ancient secrets. Every choice has consequences. Every victory comes at a cost.

The game uses the Daggerheart-compatible Hope and Fear system: a dual-die mechanic where every roll generates both narrative momentum and escalating tension.

---

## What You Need

| Item | Details |
|:---|:---|
| **Players** | 3–5 players plus 1 Game Master |
| **Dice** | Two twelve-sided dice (2d12) per player, ideally one light (Hope die) and one dark (Fear die). A set of standard polyhedral dice (d4, d6, d8, d10, d20) for damage and other rolls. |
| **Character Sheets** | One per player (see Appendix) |
| **Ship Sheet** | One per crew (see Appendix) |
| **Domain Cards** | Printed or digital cards for each class's domain abilities |
| **Tokens** | Tokens or coins to track Hope, Fear, Stress, and Armor Slots |
| **Session Length** | 3–4 hours recommended |

---

## The Core Mechanic: Action Rolls

When your character attempts something risky, the GM calls for an **Action Roll**.

**Step 1: Roll 2d12.** One die is your Hope die (light), the other is your Fear die (dark).

**Step 2: Add your Trait modifier.** The GM tells you which of the six Traits applies: Agility, Strength, Finesse, Instinct, Presence, or Knowledge. Add the relevant modifier to the total of both dice.

**Step 3: Compare to the Difficulty.** If your total meets or exceeds the Difficulty number (or the target's Evasion, for attacks), you succeed. If it falls short, you fail — but the story always moves forward.

**Step 4: Check which die rolled higher.**

- **Hope die is higher (or tied):** You generate 1 Hope token. The player pool of Hope grows — spend Hope later to activate powerful abilities, assist allies, or turn the tide. You choose which player acts next.
- **Fear die is higher:** The GM generates 1 Fear token. The GM's Fear pool grows — the GM spends Fear to activate enemy abilities, introduce complications, and escalate threats. The GM decides who acts next.

This means every single roll advances the tension economy. Even success can feed the GM's resources, and failure can still give the players tools to fight back.

### Criticals

- **Doubles with Hope (tied dice, attack hits):** Critical Success — increase the severity of your damage by one step, or gain an exceptional narrative advantage.
- **Doubles with Fear (tied dice, attack misses or non-combat):** Critical Failure — the GM gains 2 Fear instead of 1, and the situation worsens dramatically.

---

## The Six Traits

| Trait | Governs |
|:---|:---|
| **Agility** | Dodging, piloting, acrobatics, reflexes |
| **Strength** | Melee attacks, lifting, endurance, physical force |
| **Finesse** | Ranged attacks, lockpicking, sleight of hand, precision |
| **Instinct** | Perception, intuition, survival, reading people |
| **Presence** | Diplomacy, deception, leadership, telepathic force |
| **Knowledge** | Engineering, medicine, history, hacking, science |

Traits are expressed as modifiers ranging from -1 to +3 at character creation (higher at advanced levels). The standard starting array is **+2, +1, +1, 0, 0, -1**, assigned to traits in any order.

---

## Hit Points, Stress, and Damage Thresholds

**Hit Points (HP)** represent your physical resilience. At Level 1, most characters have between 5 and 7 HP depending on class. HP is not reduced by subtracting damage directly. Instead, Nexus 9 uses a **Damage Threshold** system.

### How Damage Works

When you are hit by an attack, the attacker rolls their weapon's damage dice. The total is compared against your **Damage Thresholds** — three numbers (Minor, Major, Severe) that determine the severity of the wound:

| Damage Total | Severity | HP Lost | Stress Marked |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---:|
| Below your Minor threshold | **Glancing Blow** — no effect | 0 | 0 |
| Meets or exceeds Minor | **Minor Hit** | 1 | 0 |
| Meets or exceeds Major | **Major Hit** | 2 | 1 |
| Meets or exceeds Severe | **Severe Hit** | 3 | 2 |

> **Example:** Your Marine has thresholds of Minor 4 / Major 8 / Severe 15. An enemy deals 10 damage. That exceeds your Major threshold (8) but not your Severe (15), so it is a Major Hit — you lose 2 HP and mark 1 Stress.

When your HP reaches 0, you are **Downed** — unconscious and dying unless an ally stabilizes you or you make your class's Death Move.

### Stress and Scars

**Stress** represents mental and emotional strain. You mark Stress when you take Major or Severe hits, push your abilities, resist psychic attacks, or face traumatic events. When your Stress track fills (typically 6 slots), you gain a **Scar** — a permanent narrative consequence (a phobia, a grudge, a broken relationship, a physical mark). Scars are not punishments; they are character development. After gaining a Scar, your Stress track resets.

---

## Armor: Two Layers of Protection

Armor provides two distinct benefits:

**1. Replacement Thresholds.** When you wear armor, its damage thresholds replace your base class thresholds (armor thresholds are always higher). This makes you harder to wound — attacks that would be a Major Hit against you unarmored might only be a Minor Hit in armor.

**2. Armor Slots.** Each suit of armor has an **Armor Score** — this is the number of Armor Slots it provides. When you take a hit, you may **mark one Armor Slot** to reduce the severity by one step (e.g., a Major Hit becomes a Minor Hit). Armor Slots are restored during a Short or Long Rest.

> **Example:** You are wearing a Light Combat Rig (thresholds Minor 6 / Major 13 / Severe 23, Armor Score 3). An enemy deals 14 damage — that exceeds your Major threshold, so it is a Major Hit (2 HP, 1 Stress). You mark one of your 3 Armor Slots to reduce it to a Minor Hit (1 HP, 0 Stress). You now have 2 Armor Slots remaining.

---

## Combat: The Basics

Combat in Nexus 9 is fast, dramatic, and uses the same Hope/Fear spotlight system as all other rolls. There is no initiative order.

**Spotlight System:** The first player to act is whoever makes narrative sense. After resolving their action, the spotlight passes — on a Hope result, the player chooses who goes next; on a Fear result, the GM decides.

**On Your Turn, you may:**

- **Move** one range band (Melee → Very Close → Close → Far → Very Far) as part of your action
- **Take an Action** — attack, use a domain card, interact with the environment, or attempt a skill roll
- **Take a Free Action** — speak a short sentence, draw a weapon, press a button

**Attacking:**

1. Roll 2d12 + the Trait listed on your weapon
2. Compare to the target's Evasion — meet or exceed it to hit
3. Check Hope/Fear and pass the spotlight
4. If you hit, roll your weapon's damage dice
5. Compare the damage total to the target's thresholds to determine severity (0/1/2/3 HP lost)

**GM Actions:** The GM spends Fear tokens to activate adversary attacks and abilities. Each standard attack costs 1 Fear. Special abilities cost 1–3 Fear. Movement is free with an attack.

---

## Range Bands

| Range Band | Description | Examples |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Melee** | Within arm's reach | Grappling in a corridor; shock-pike combat |
| **Very Close** | Same room, a few steps away | Across a small cargo bay |
| **Close** | Across a large room or corridor | Down a station spoke |
| **Far** | Across a plaza or open area | Across a docking bay |
| **Very Far** | Edge of visibility | Sniper positions on a different ring |

You may move one range band as part of your action. Moving two bands requires spending your entire action to Dash.

---

## Hope and Fear Economy

**Hope** is the players' shared resource. Spend it to:

- Activate powerful domain card abilities
- Assist an ally's roll (+1d6 bonus)
- Disengage safely from Melee range (costs 1 Hope)
- Trigger a class feature that requires Hope

**Fear** is the GM's resource. Spend it to:

- Activate enemy attacks and special abilities
- Introduce environmental hazards or complications
- Escalate a threat clock
- Trigger a dramatic twist or ambush

The economy is self-balancing: the more the players succeed with Fear results, the more Fear the GM accumulates. The more the players roll Hope, the more tools they have. This creates a natural dramatic rhythm of rising and falling tension.

---

## Starship Combat: Quick Version

When your crew's ship enters combat, each player takes a **Crew Station**:

| Station | Primary Trait | Key Actions |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Helm** | Agility | Evasive maneuvers, pursue, flee, ram |
| **Tactical** | Instinct | Fire weapons, target lock, suppressive fire |
| **Engineering** | Knowledge | Reroute power, emergency repair, shield boost |
| **Sensors/Comms** | Knowledge or Presence | Sensor sweep, electronic warfare, hail/negotiate |
| **Quartermaster** | Presence | Rally crew, distribute stims, coordinate |

Ship combat uses the same Hope/Fear spotlight system. The ship has its own HP (Hull Points), damage thresholds, and System Stress track. When System Stress fills, the GM rolls on the Critical System Failure table.

---

## Social Conflict and Diplomacy

Not every conflict is solved with weapons. Nexus 9 features a structured social conflict system:

**Persuasion Rolls** use Presence or Knowledge against a target's **Difficulty** (based on their rank, stubbornness, or faction loyalty).

**Leverage:** If you have information, favors, or threats that give you an advantage, you gain Advantage on the roll (roll 3d12, keep the two highest).

**Faction Reputation:** Your crew's standing with each major faction affects Difficulty numbers for social interactions. Helping the Aurelians might make the Kaelen Syndicate hostile.

---

## Domain Cards

Each class has access to two **Domains** — thematic ability pools represented by cards. At character creation, you choose **two Domain cards** (one from each Domain) to form your starting **Loadout**. During play, you can activate these cards by spending actions, Hope, or Stress.

Cards come in three types:

- **Action Cards:** Require your action to use (attacks, buffs, special maneuvers)
- **Reaction Cards:** Trigger in response to specific events (dodging, countering, shielding)
- **Passive Cards:** Always active once equipped (stat bonuses, auras, resistances)

You can swap cards between sessions during downtime, allowing you to adapt your loadout to the mission ahead. Your maximum Loadout at Level 1 is 5 cards.

---

## Your First Session

1. **Create Characters** using Book Two (or use the Pregen Crew in this book)
2. **Choose a Ship** (or use the Starter Ship, the *Dawnrunner*)
3. **Run Session Zero** to establish tone, safety tools, and crew bonds
4. **Start with Chapter 1 of The Gathering Storm** — or create your own opening scenario
5. **Play to find out what happens.** The dice, the choices, and the consequences will surprise everyone — including the GM.

---

> *"Roll the dice. Hope for the best. Prepare for the worst. That is life on Nexus 9."*

sections.title: What Is This Game?
sections.content:
Nexus 9: The Fraying Dark is a tabletop roleplaying game for 3–6 players and one Game Master (GM). One player runs the world — its factions, threats, and mysteries — while the others play a crew of operatives stationed aboard a massive space station at the crossroads of four rival civilizations. You will negotiate, fight, investigate, fly ships, and uncover ancient secrets. Every choice has consequences. Every victory comes at a cost.

The game uses the Daggerheart-compatible Hope and Fear system: a dual-die mechanic where every roll generates both narrative momentum and escalating tension.

---

sections.title: What You Need
sections.content:
| Item | Details |
|:---|:---|
| **Players** | 3–5 players plus 1 Game Master |
| **Dice** | Two twelve-sided dice (2d12) per player, ideally one light (Hope die) and one dark (Fear die). A set of standard polyhedral dice (d4, d6, d8, d10, d20) for damage and other rolls. |
| **Character Sheets** | One per player (see Appendix) |
| **Ship Sheet** | One per crew (see Appendix) |
| **Domain Cards** | Printed or digital cards for each class's domain abilities |
| **Tokens** | Tokens or coins to track Hope, Fear, Stress, and Armor Slots |
| **Session Length** | 3–4 hours recommended |

---

sections.title: The Core Mechanic: Action Rolls
sections.content:
When your character attempts something risky, the GM calls for an **Action Roll**.

**Step 1: Roll 2d12.** One die is your Hope die (light), the other is your Fear die (dark).

**Step 2: Add your Trait modifier.** The GM tells you which of the six Traits applies: Agility, Strength, Finesse, Instinct, Presence, or Knowledge. Add the relevant modifier to the total of both dice.

**Step 3: Compare to the Difficulty.** If your total meets or exceeds the Difficulty number (or the target's Evasion, for attacks), you succeed. If it falls short, you fail — but the story always moves forward.

**Step 4: Check which die rolled higher.**

- **Hope die is higher (or tied):** You generate 1 Hope token. The player pool of Hope grows — spend Hope later to activate powerful abilities, assist allies, or turn the tide. You choose which player acts next.
- **Fear die is higher:** The GM generates 1 Fear token. The GM's Fear pool grows — the GM spends Fear to activate enemy abilities, introduce complications, and escalate threats. The GM decides who acts next.

This means every single roll advances the tension economy. Even success can feed the GM's resources, and failure can still give the players tools to fight back.

### Criticals

- **Doubles with Hope (tied dice, attack hits):** Critical Success — increase the severity of your damage by one step, or gain an exceptional narrative advantage.
- **Doubles with Fear (tied dice, attack misses or non-combat):** Critical Failure — the GM gains 2 Fear instead of 1, and the situation worsens dramatically.

---

sections.title: The Six Traits
sections.content:
| Trait | Governs |
|:---|:---|
| **Agility** | Dodging, piloting, acrobatics, reflexes |
| **Strength** | Melee attacks, lifting, endurance, physical force |
| **Finesse** | Ranged attacks, lockpicking, sleight of hand, precision |
| **Instinct** | Perception, intuition, survival, reading people |
| **Presence** | Diplomacy, deception, leadership, telepathic force |
| **Knowledge** | Engineering, medicine, history, hacking, science |

Traits are expressed as modifiers ranging from -1 to +3 at character creation (higher at advanced levels). The standard starting array is **+2, +1, +1, 0, 0, -1**, assigned to traits in any order.

---

sections.title: Hit Points, Stress, and Damage Thresholds
sections.content:
**Hit Points (HP)** represent your physical resilience. At Level 1, most characters have between 5 and 7 HP depending on class. HP is not reduced by subtracting damage directly. Instead, Nexus 9 uses a **Damage Threshold** system.

### How Damage Works

When you are hit by an attack, the attacker rolls their weapon's damage dice. The total is compared against your **Damage Thresholds** — three numbers (Minor, Major, Severe) that determine the severity of the wound:

| Damage Total | Severity | HP Lost | Stress Marked |
|:---|:---|:---:|:---:|
| Below your Minor threshold | **Glancing Blow** — no effect | 0 | 0 |
| Meets or exceeds Minor | **Minor Hit** | 1 | 0 |
| Meets or exceeds Major | **Major Hit** | 2 | 1 |
| Meets or exceeds Severe | **Severe Hit** | 3 | 2 |

> **Example:** Your Marine has thresholds of Minor 4 / Major 8 / Severe 15. An enemy deals 10 damage. That exceeds your Major threshold (8) but not your Severe (15), so it is a Major Hit — you lose 2 HP and mark 1 Stress.

When your HP reaches 0, you are **Downed** — unconscious and dying unless an ally stabilizes you or you make your class's Death Move.

### Stress and Scars

**Stress** represents mental and emotional strain. You mark Stress when you take Major or Severe hits, push your abilities, resist psychic attacks, or face traumatic events. When your Stress track fills (typically 6 slots), you gain a **Scar** — a permanent narrative consequence (a phobia, a grudge, a broken relationship, a physical mark). Scars are not punishments; they are character development. After gaining a Scar, your Stress track resets.

---

sections.title: Armor: Two Layers of Protection
sections.content:
Armor provides two distinct benefits:

**1. Replacement Thresholds.** When you wear armor, its damage thresholds replace your base class thresholds (armor thresholds are always higher). This makes you harder to wound — attacks that would be a Major Hit against you unarmored might only be a Minor Hit in armor.

**2. Armor Slots.** Each suit of armor has an **Armor Score** — this is the number of Armor Slots it provides. When you take a hit, you may **mark one Armor Slot** to reduce the severity by one step (e.g., a Major Hit becomes a Minor Hit). Armor Slots are restored during a Short or Long Rest.

> **Example:** You are wearing a Light Combat Rig (thresholds Minor 6 / Major 13 / Severe 23, Armor Score 3). An enemy deals 14 damage — that exceeds your Major threshold, so it is a Major Hit (2 HP, 1 Stress). You mark one of your 3 Armor Slots to reduce it to a Minor Hit (1 HP, 0 Stress). You now have 2 Armor Slots remaining.

---

| **Very Close** | Same room, a few steps away | Across a small cargo bay |

sections.title: Your First Session
sections.content:
1. **Create Characters** using Book Two (or use the Pregen Crew in this book)
2. **Choose a Ship** (or use the Starter Ship, the *Dawnrunner*)
3. **Run Session Zero** to establish tone, safety tools, and crew bonds
4. **Start with Chapter 1 of The Gathering Storm** — or create your own opening scenario
5. **Play to find out what happens.** The dice, the choices, and the consequences will surprise everyone — including the GM.

---

> *"Roll the dice. Hope for the best. Prepare for the worst. That is life on Nexus 9."*

chapters.title: Session Zero: Building Your Campaign
chapters.content:
> *Before the first die is rolled, the crew must be forged. Session Zero is where you build the foundation that every session will stand on.*

---

sections.title: Purpose of Session Zero
sections.content:
Session Zero is a dedicated pre-campaign session where the GM and players collaboratively establish the tone, boundaries, characters, crew, and expectations for the campaign ahead. In Nexus 9, this session is not optional — it is essential. The game's themes of political tension, moral compromise, telepathic violation, and wartime trauma require explicit consent and calibration before play begins.

A well-run Session Zero accomplishes five things: it establishes safety boundaries, calibrates the campaign's tone, creates interconnected characters, builds the crew's shared identity, and generates the first threads of dramatic tension.

---

sections.title: Step 1: Safety and Consent
sections.content:
Before anything else, the table must agree on safety tools. Nexus 9 deals with themes that can be intense — authoritarian surveillance, psychic violation, war crimes, betrayal, and sacrifice. Every player must feel empowered to set boundaries.

**Recommended Safety Tools:**

The **Lines and Veils** system is the baseline. A Line is a hard boundary — content that will never appear in the game. A Veil is content that may exist in the fiction but will be handled off-screen or abstractly, never described in graphic detail. The GM should provide index cards and ask each player to write down any Lines and Veils anonymously.

The **X-Card** should be available at every session. Any player can tap or raise the X-Card at any time to indicate that the current content should stop immediately, no questions asked.

The **Open Door Policy** means any player can step away from the table at any time without needing to explain why.

| Tool | Function |
|:---|:---|
| **Lines** | Hard limits — this content never appears |
| **Veils** | Exists off-screen only — never described in detail |
| **X-Card** | Immediate stop on current content, no questions asked |
| **Open Door** | Any player may leave at any time without explanation |

---

sections.title: Step 2: Tone Calibration
sections.content:
Nexus 9 supports a range of tones, from political thriller to military drama to cosmic horror. The table should discuss and agree on where the campaign falls on several spectrums:

**Tone Spectrums (discuss and mark a position):**

| Spectrum | Left End | Right End |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Hope vs. Despair** | Optimistic — heroes can make a difference | Bleak — survival is the best you can hope for |
| **Realism vs. Pulp** | Grounded — consequences are harsh and realistic | Cinematic — heroes are larger than life |
| **Politics vs. Action** | Intrigue-heavy — most conflicts are social | Combat-heavy — most conflicts are physical |
| **Mystery vs. Clarity** | Players discover the truth slowly | Players know the stakes from the start |
| **Personal vs. Epic** | Focus on individual character arcs | Focus on galaxy-shaking events |

The default Nexus 9 tone sits at: **Hopeful but pressured, grounded with cinematic moments, balanced between politics and action, mystery-forward, and escalating from personal to epic.**

---

sections.title: Step 3: Campaign Scope
sections.content:
The GM should present the campaign premise and discuss its expected scope:

**Duration:** How many sessions is the campaign expected to run? The Gathering Storm campaign is designed for 24–36 sessions across three arcs.

**Level Range:** Characters begin at Level 1 and can advance to Level 10 over the course of the full campaign.

**Starting Location:** All campaigns begin on Nexus 9 station. The GM should describe the station's five rings and ask players where their characters spend most of their time.

**Faction Focus:** Which factions will be most prominent? The GM does not need to reveal secrets, but should indicate whether the campaign will focus more on Terran politics, Valari mysticism, Aurelian intrigue, Kaelen underworld, or the Eclipse threat.

---

sections.title: Step 4: Character Creation
sections.content:
Each player creates a character using the rules in Book Two. During Session Zero, character creation should be collaborative — players should build their characters together, not in isolation.

**The Character Creation Checklist:**

1. Choose an **Ancestry** (Human, Valari, Aurelian, Kaelen, or Mixed Heritage)
2. Choose a **Community** (where you grew up — Station-Born, Colony-Raised, Fleet-Bred, Frontier, or Exile)
3. Choose a **Class** (Envoy, Operative, Pilot, Marine, Engineer, Mystic, Broker, or Medic)
4. Assign **Trait Scores** using the standard array or point-buy method
5. Select your starting **Domain Card Loadout** from your class's two domains
6. Choose an **Experience** — a defining life event that shaped your character (e.g., "Survived the Siege of Sol," "Escaped the Mindclave," "Witnessed a Luminary")
7. Write a **Secret** — something your character is hiding from the crew (see below)
8. Establish **Bonds** with at least two other player characters

---

sections.title: Step 5: Bonds and Secrets
sections.content:
**Bonds** are the connective tissue of the crew. Each player should establish at least two bonds with other player characters:

- **One Positive Bond:** "I trust [character] because..." or "I owe [character] a debt because..."
- **One Complicated Bond:** "I am suspicious of [character] because..." or "[Character] and I disagree about..."

Bonds are not static. They will evolve, strengthen, or break over the course of the campaign. The GM should revisit bonds at the end of each arc.

**Secrets** are private truths that each character carries. The player writes their secret on a card and gives it to the GM. The GM will weave these secrets into the campaign narrative. Examples:

- "I am an unregistered telepath hiding from the Mindclave."
- "I was ordered to spy on the crew by Aurelian intelligence."
- "I saw something in hyperspace that I cannot explain, and it is getting closer."
- "I killed someone during the war, and their family is looking for me."

---

sections.title: Step 6: Crew Creation
sections.content:
The crew is more than a collection of individuals — it is a shared identity with its own history, reputation, and resources.

**Step 6a: Choose a Ship.** The crew selects or is assigned a starship. For new campaigns, the Starter Ship (*Dawnrunner*, a modified Terran corvette) is recommended. Record the ship's stats on the Ship Sheet.

**Step 6b: Name the Crew.** Give the crew a collective identity. Are you a registered security contractor? A freelance troubleshooting team? An off-the-books intelligence cell? The crew's public identity affects how NPCs react to them.

**Step 6c: Establish the Debt.** Every crew starts with a Debt — an obligation that binds them together and to the station. The Debt creates immediate dramatic pressure. Examples:

| Debt | Description |
|:---|:---|
| **Financial** | The crew owes a significant sum to a Kaelen Syndicate lender. Payments are due monthly. |
| **Political** | The crew is indebted to a Terran senator who arranged their docking permits. She will call in favors. |
| **Criminal** | The crew is being blackmailed by someone who knows one of their secrets. |
| **Military** | The crew includes a deserter. If discovered, the entire crew faces charges. |
| **Personal** | A crew member's family member is being held as leverage by a faction. |

**Step 6d: Assign Crew Roles.** Each player chooses a primary role aboard the ship (Pilot, Gunner, Engineer, Comms/Ops, Captain, Marine). These roles determine who does what during starship encounters.

---

sections.title: Step 7: The First Thread
sections.content:
Before Session Zero ends, the GM should plant the first narrative thread. This is not a full adventure — it is a hook, a question, or an event that gives the players something to think about before the first proper session.

Examples:

- "As you settle into your berths on Nexus 9, a priority message arrives on your ship's comm: CARGO PICKUP — DOCKING BAY 14 — 0200 HOURS — NO QUESTIONS. The sender is anonymous."
- "A Valari priest approaches one of you in the Green Ring market and says, without preamble: 'The stars are fraying. You have been chosen to witness.' Then she walks away."
- "Station security informs you that your docking permit has been flagged for review. Someone with authority does not want you here."

---

sections.title: Session Zero Checklist
sections.content:
| Step | Complete? |
|:---|:---|
| Safety tools established (Lines, Veils, X-Card, Open Door) | ☐ |
| Tone calibrated across all five spectrums | ☐ |
| Campaign scope discussed (duration, level range, faction focus) | ☐ |
| All characters created with Ancestry, Community, Class, Traits, and Loadout | ☐ |
| Each character has at least two Bonds with other PCs | ☐ |
| Each character has a Secret (given to GM) | ☐ |
| Ship selected and Ship Sheet filled out | ☐ |
| Crew named and public identity established | ☐ |
| Crew Debt established | ☐ |
| Crew Roles assigned | ☐ |
| First narrative thread planted | ☐ |

---

> *"A crew that knows each other's strengths will survive. A crew that knows each other's secrets will either become unbreakable — or tear itself apart."*

chapters.title: Pregen Crew: The Dawnrunner Company
chapters.content:
> *Six ready-to-play characters for immediate use. Each pregen includes a full stat block, starting equipment, domain card loadout, bonds, and a secret. These characters are designed to work together as a crew aboard the starter ship Dawnrunner.*

> **Design Note:** These pregens use the Standard Array (+2, +1, +1, 0, 0, -1) and follow all character creation rules from Book Two. Their damage thresholds assume they are wearing their listed armor. Unarmored thresholds can be found in the Class Damage Thresholds table in Book Four.

---

## 1. COMMANDER KIRA VASQUEZ — The Envoy

*"Every word is a weapon. Every silence is a shield. And every handshake is a gamble."*

**Ancestry:** Terran
**Community:** Station Rat (Nexus 4 — before it vanished)
**Class:** Envoy (Codex and Grace Domains)
**Subclass:** Consul
**Level:** 1

### Traits

| Trait | Modifier |
|:---|:---:|
| Agility | 0 |
| Strength | -1 |
| Finesse | 0 |
| Instinct | +1 |
| Presence | +2 |
| Knowledge | +1 |

### Statistics

| Stat | Value | Notes |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Hit Points** | 5 | Envoy base |
| **Stress Slots** | 7 | Envoy base |
| **Evasion** | 11 | Base 10 + Flight Suit bonus (+1) |
| **Armor** | Flight Suit (Light, Tier 1) | Thresholds below; Armor Score 3 |
| **Proficiency** | 1 | Roll 1 damage die |
| **Hope** | 2 | Starting session Hope |

### Damage Thresholds (Armored — Flight Suit)

| Minor | Major | Severe |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 5 | 11 | 20 |

### Starting Experiences

| Experience | Bonus | Source |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| Street Smarts | +2 | Station Rat community |
| Negotiation | +1 | Chosen |
| Investigation | +1 | Chosen |

### Domain Card Loadout (2 cards)

| Card | Domain | Type | Effect |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Bureaucratic Loophole** | Codex | Action | Spend 1 Hope. Identify a legal or procedural exploit that forces an NPC to comply, delay, or reconsider. |
| **Silver Tongue** | Grace | Action | Roll Presence vs. Difficulty. On success, charm or persuade a target, shifting their disposition one step in your favor. |

### Starting Equipment

- Diplomatic credentials (genuine, Terran Coalition — flagged as "inactive")
- Formal attire (Aurelian-cut)
- Encrypted datapad
- Mag-Pistol (d6, Close range, Finesse)
- Commlink, standard datapad, 2 Emergency Med-Patches, 3 Ration Packs, utility knife
- 200 Credits

### Ancestry Feature

*Tenacity:* Once per Long Rest, when you fail an action roll, you may immediately reroll it and take the new result.

### Community Feature

*Station Knowledge:* You always know the fastest route between any two points on a station, and you can identify safe houses and bolt-holes without a roll.

### Character Details

**Motivation:** Find out what happened to Nexus 4 — and whether the people aboard are still alive somewhere.

**Fear:** That the visions she has of Nexus 4 are not memories but warnings of what is coming for Nexus 9.

**Crew Role:** Captain

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "I trust Reth. He saved my life during the Frontier Incident, and I have never forgotten."
- *Complicated:* "Zara is hiding something. I can feel it every time she avoids my questions about her past."

### Secret

*Kira has fragmented memories of Nexus 4 — and in those memories, she sees the Eclipse. She believes the station did not vanish. It was taken.*

---

## 2. SERGEANT RETH TAL'VORI — The Marine

*"I do not fight because I enjoy it. I fight because someone has to stand between the darkness and everything I love."*

**Ancestry:** Valari (Vael'Kari — Path of the Blade)
**Community:** Military Brat (Valari Warfleet)
**Class:** Marine (Blade and Bone Domains)
**Subclass:** Vanguard
**Level:** 1

### Traits

| Trait | Modifier |
|:---|:---:|
| Agility | +1 |
| Strength | +2 |
| Finesse | 0 |
| Instinct | +1 |
| Presence | -1 |
| Knowledge | 0 |

### Statistics

| Stat | Value | Notes |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Hit Points** | 6 | Marine base |
| **Stress Slots** | 6 | Marine base |
| **Evasion** | 10 | Base 11 + Marine Hardsuit penalty (-1) |
| **Armor** | Marine Hardsuit (Heavy, Tier 1) | Thresholds below; Armor Score 4 |
| **Proficiency** | 1 | Roll 1 damage die |
| **Hope** | 2 | Starting session Hope |

### Damage Thresholds (Armored — Marine Hardsuit)

| Minor | Major | Severe |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 7 | 15 | 26 |

### Starting Experiences

| Experience | Bonus | Source |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| Tactics | +2 | Military Brat community |
| Intimidation | +1 | Chosen |
| Survival | +1 | Chosen |

### Domain Card Loadout (2 cards)

| Card | Domain | Type | Effect |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Cleaving Strike** | Blade | Action | Make a melee attack. On a hit, deal weapon damage to the primary target and half damage to one adjacent enemy. |
| **Tactical Withdrawal** | Bone | Reaction | When an ally within Close range is hit, spend 1 Hope. Move to their position and take the hit instead, using your own thresholds. |

### Starting Equipment

- Vibro-Sword (d10+3, Melee, Strength, Two-Handed, kinetic)
- Mag-Pistol (d6, Close range, Finesse)
- Marine Hardsuit (Heavy, Tier 1)
- Combat stim (x1, clears 2 Stress), extra ammo pack, breaching charge
- Harmonic shard pendant (religious significance)
- Commlink, standard datapad, 2 Emergency Med-Patches, 3 Ration Packs, utility knife
- 200 Credits

### Ancestry Feature

*Crystal Resonance:* You can sense the presence of Progenitor technology or Eclipse corruption within Close range without a roll.

### Community Feature

*Drilled:* In the first round of any combat, you act before any adversary, regardless of the spotlight order.

### Character Details

**Motivation:** Protect his crewmates. After the dishonor of the Border Skirmishes, he will not fail the people who depend on him again.

**Fear:** That the Valari surrender at the Siege of Sol was not a mistake — and that his people are hiding something far worse.

**Crew Role:** Marine / Security

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "Commander Vasquez earned my respect. She speaks truth even when it is dangerous."
- *Complicated:* "Mira's telepathy unsettles me. The Valari do not trust those who can read minds without permission."

### Secret

*Reth knows the true reason for the Valari surrender at the Siege of Sol. He learned it from a dying priest during the Border Skirmishes. He has told no one.*

---

## 3. ZARA "GHOST" OKAFOR — The Operative

*"I was never here. This conversation never happened. And you definitely did not see me take that datapad."*

**Ancestry:** Mindclave-Born (unregistered)
**Community:** Exile (Grew up in the Warrens of a frontier station)
**Class:** Operative (Midnight and Grace Domains)
**Subclass:** Phantom
**Level:** 1

### Traits

| Trait | Modifier |
|:---|:---:|
| Agility | +2 |
| Strength | -1 |
| Finesse | +1 |
| Instinct | +1 |
| Presence | 0 |
| Knowledge | 0 |

### Statistics

| Stat | Value | Notes |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Hit Points** | 6 | Operative base |
| **Stress Slots** | 6 | Operative base |
| **Evasion** | 13 | Base 12 + Flight Suit bonus (+1) |
| **Armor** | Flight Suit (Light, Tier 1) | Thresholds below; Armor Score 3 |
| **Proficiency** | 1 | Roll 1 damage die |
| **Hope** | 2 | Starting session Hope |

### Damage Thresholds (Armored — Flight Suit)

| Minor | Major | Severe |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 5 | 11 | 20 |

### Starting Experiences

| Experience | Bonus | Source |
|:---|:---:|:---|

| Street Smarts | +1 | Chosen |
| Technology | +1 | Chosen |

### Domain Card Loadout (2 cards)

| Card | Domain | Type | Effect |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Pick and Pull** | Midnight | Action | Make a Finesse roll vs. Difficulty. On success, steal a small item from a target without them noticing, or plant an item on them. |
| **Silver Tongue** | Grace | Action | Roll Presence vs. Difficulty. On success, charm or persuade a target, shifting their disposition one step in your favor. |

### Starting Equipment

- Suppressed Pistol (d8, Close range, Finesse, Silent tag)
- Vibro-Knife (d6+1, Melee, Finesse)
- Flight Suit (Light, Tier 1)
- Lockpick set, holo-disguise emitter (1 use), smoke grenade (x2)
- Forged identity papers (3 sets)
- Commlink, standard datapad, 2 Emergency Med-Patches, 3 Ration Packs, utility knife
- 200 Credits

### Ancestry Feature

*Psychic Sensitivity:* You can read the surface emotions (not thoughts) of any creature within Very Close range as a Free Action.

### Community Feature

*Nothing Left to Lose:* When you are at 3 or fewer HP, you gain +1 to all action rolls.

### Character Details

**Motivation:** Stay free. Stay hidden. Stay alive. And maybe, someday, find a place where she does not have to run.

**Fear:** That the Mindclave will find her — and that when they do, she will discover she is more dangerous than she ever imagined.

**Crew Role:** Comms / Operations

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "Doc Patel patched me up when no one else would. I owe her my life — literally."
- *Complicated:* "Kira asks too many questions. If she finds out what I am, everything falls apart."

### Secret

*Zara is a rogue telepath (RI-4). She takes black-market inhibitors to suppress her abilities, but they are becoming less effective. The Mindclave has an active warrant for her capture.*

---

### Starting Equipment

- Terran Service Pistol (d8, Close range, Finesse)
- Flight Suit (Light, Tier 1)

### Ancestry Feature

*Tenacity:* Once per Long Rest, when you fail an action roll, you may immediately reroll it and take the new result.

### Community Feature

*Self-Reliant:* During a Short Rest, you can repair one piece of damaged equipment without tools or an Engineer.

### Character Details

**Motivation:** Prove he made the right call when he disobeyed orders. Earn back his honor — on his own terms.

**Fear:** That the Kaelen Syndicate will call in the debt he owes them, and he will have to choose between his crew and his freedom.

**Crew Role:** Pilot

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "Kel keeps the ship alive. Without them, we would be floating debris. I respect that more than anything."
- *Complicated:* "Reth is too rigid. Rules and honor are fine until they get people killed."

### Secret

*The civilian transport Jace saved was carrying Kaelen Syndicate contraband. The Syndicate considers him an asset — and they have been calling in that debt.*

---

### Starting Equipment

- Flight Suit (Light, Tier 1)

### Ancestry Feature

*Tenacity:* Once per Long Rest, when you fail an action roll, you may immediately reroll it and take the new result.

### Community Feature

*Station Knowledge:* You always know the fastest route between any two points on a station, and you can identify safe houses and bolt-holes without a roll.

### Character Details

**Motivation:** Understand the visions she has been having. Protect her patients. Find out if the Luminary was real.

**Fear:** That the visions are a sign of Eclipse corruption — and that she is already compromised.

**Crew Role:** Medic / Science Officer

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "Zara came to me bleeding and desperate. I helped her without asking questions. That is what doctors do."
- *Complicated:* "Jace is reckless. One day his luck will run out, and I will be the one picking up the pieces."

### Secret

*Mira was contacted by a Luminary. She does not know what it means, but she has been having visions of the Eclipse's return — visions that are becoming more frequent and more terrifying.*

---

## 6. KEL-7 "KEL" TORVIK — The Engineer

*"Give me a wrench, a power cell, and thirty minutes. I will fix anything. Give me an hour, and I will make it better than new."*

**Ancestry:** Frontier-Born (Kaelen-raised)
**Community:** Frontier Settler (Grew up on a Kaelen salvage vessel)
**Class:** Engineer (Valor and Blade Domains)
**Subclass:** Artificer
**Level:** 1

### Traits

| Trait | Modifier |
|:---|:---:|
| Agility | 0 |
| Strength | +1 |
| Finesse | +1 |
| Instinct | -1 |
| Presence | 0 |
| Knowledge | +2 |

### Statistics

| Stat | Value | Notes |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Hit Points** | 7 | Engineer base |
| **Stress Slots** | 5 | Engineer base |
| **Evasion** | 8 | Base 9 + Marine Hardsuit penalty (-1) |
| **Armor** | Marine Hardsuit (Heavy, Tier 1) | Thresholds below; Armor Score 4 |
| **Proficiency** | 1 | Roll 1 damage die |
| **Hope** | 2 | Starting session Hope |

### Damage Thresholds (Armored — Marine Hardsuit)

| Minor | Major | Severe |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 7 | 15 | 26 |

### Starting Experiences

| Experience | Bonus | Source |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| Survival | +2 | Frontier Settler community |
| Engineering | +1 | Chosen |
| Technology | +1 | Chosen |

### Domain Card Loadout (2 cards)

| Card | Domain | Type | Effect |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Fortify Position** | Valor | Action | Spend 1 Hope. Create a barricade or reinforce a position. All allies within Very Close range gain +2 to their damage thresholds until the end of the scene or until the barricade is destroyed. |
| **Heavy Swing** | Blade | Action | Make a melee attack with Advantage. On a hit, deal weapon damage +1d6 additional damage. |

### Starting Equipment

- Plasma Cutter (d8+1, Melee, Strength, can cut through bulkheads)
- Mag-Pistol (d6, Close range, Finesse)
- Marine Hardsuit (Heavy, Tier 1) — modified work suit
- Portable toolkit, welding torch, diagnostic scanner
- Salvaged Progenitor component (unknown function)
- Commlink, standard datapad, 2 Emergency Med-Patches, 3 Ration Packs, utility knife
- 200 Credits

### Ancestry Feature

*Resourceful:* Once per Short Rest, you may produce a common, non-weapon item worth 50 Credits or less that you "happened to have."

### Community Feature

*Self-Reliant:* During a Short Rest, you can repair one piece of damaged equipment without tools or an Engineer.

### Character Details

**Motivation:** Understand the Progenitor component they carry. Build something that matters. Maybe even build a home.

**Fear:** That the Progenitor artifact is not a tool — it is a trap. And that by carrying it, they have already sprung it.

**Crew Role:** Engineer / Mechanic

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "Jace trusts me with the ship. That is the highest compliment a pilot can give an engineer."
- *Complicated:* "Reth does not understand machines. He treats the ship like a beast of burden instead of a living system."

### Secret

*The Progenitor component Kel carries is not inert. It has been slowly interfacing with the Dawnrunner's systems, and Kel has noticed the ship responding to their thoughts. They do not know if this is a gift or a trap.*

---

## Crew Summary

| Character | Class | Role | Ancestry | Key Trait |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Kira Vasquez | Envoy | Captain | Terran | Presence +2 |
| Reth Tal'Vori | Marine | Security | Valari | Strength +2 |
| Zara "Ghost" Okafor | Operative | Comms/Ops | Mindclave-Born | Agility +2 |
| Jace Morrow | Pilot | Pilot | Terran | Agility +2 |
| Dr. Mira Patel | Medic | Science/Medical | Terran | Knowledge +2 |
| Kel-7 Torvik | Engineer | Engineer | Frontier-Born | Knowledge +2 |

---

> **Using These Pregens:** Each character is built using the Standard Array (+2, +1, +1, 0, 0, -1) and follows all character creation rules from Book Two. Their damage thresholds are listed with armor equipped. To find their unarmored thresholds, refer to the Class Damage Thresholds table in Book Four. Each pregen has 2 Domain Cards — one from each of their class's Domains. As they advance to Level 2 and beyond, they will gain additional cards from the Domain lists in Book Three.

> *"Six strangers. One ship. A station on the edge of the abyss. This is how legends begin — or how epitaphs are written."*

sections.title: 1. COMMANDER KIRA VASQUEZ — The Envoy
sections.content:
*"Every word is a weapon. Every silence is a shield. And every handshake is a gamble."*

**Ancestry:** Terran
**Community:** Station Rat (Nexus 4 — before it vanished)
**Class:** Envoy (Codex and Grace Domains)
**Subclass:** Consul
**Level:** 1

### Traits

| Trait | Modifier |
|:---|:---:|
| Agility | 0 |
| Strength | -1 |
| Finesse | 0 |
| Instinct | +1 |
| Presence | +2 |
| Knowledge | +1 |

### Statistics

| Stat | Value | Notes |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Hit Points** | 5 | Envoy base |
| **Stress Slots** | 7 | Envoy base |
| **Evasion** | 11 | Base 10 + Flight Suit bonus (+1) |
| **Armor** | Flight Suit (Light, Tier 1) | Thresholds below; Armor Score 3 |
| **Proficiency** | 1 | Roll 1 damage die |
| **Hope** | 2 | Starting session Hope |

### Damage Thresholds (Armored — Flight Suit)

| Minor | Major | Severe |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 5 | 11 | 20 |

### Starting Experiences

| Experience | Bonus | Source |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| Street Smarts | +2 | Station Rat community |
| Negotiation | +1 | Chosen |
| Investigation | +1 | Chosen |

### Domain Card Loadout (2 cards)

| Card | Domain | Type | Effect |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Bureaucratic Loophole** | Codex | Action | Spend 1 Hope. Identify a legal or procedural exploit that forces an NPC to comply, delay, or reconsider. |
| **Silver Tongue** | Grace | Action | Roll Presence vs. Difficulty. On success, charm or persuade a target, shifting their disposition one step in your favor. |

### Starting Equipment

- Diplomatic credentials (genuine, Terran Coalition — flagged as "inactive")
- Formal attire (Aurelian-cut)
- Encrypted datapad
- Mag-Pistol (d6, Close range, Finesse)
- Commlink, standard datapad, 2 Emergency Med-Patches, 3 Ration Packs, utility knife
- 200 Credits

### Ancestry Feature

*Tenacity:* Once per Long Rest, when you fail an action roll, you may immediately reroll it and take the new result.

### Community Feature

*Station Knowledge:* You always know the fastest route between any two points on a station, and you can identify safe houses and bolt-holes without a roll.

### Character Details

**Motivation:** Find out what happened to Nexus 4 — and whether the people aboard are still alive somewhere.

**Fear:** That the visions she has of Nexus 4 are not memories but warnings of what is coming for Nexus 9.

**Crew Role:** Captain

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "I trust Reth. He saved my life during the Frontier Incident, and I have never forgotten."
- *Complicated:* "Zara is hiding something. I can feel it every time she avoids my questions about her past."

### Secret

*Kira has fragmented memories of Nexus 4 — and in those memories, she sees the Eclipse. She believes the station did not vanish. It was taken.*

---

- Lucky dice (no mechanical effect — sentimental)
- Commlink, standard datapad, 2 Emergency Med-Patches, 3 Ration Packs, utility knife
- 200 Credits

### Ancestry Feature

*Tenacity:* Once per Long Rest, when you fail an action roll, you may immediately reroll it and take the new result.

### Community Feature

*Self-Reliant:* During a Short Rest, you can repair one piece of damaged equipment without tools or an Engineer.

### Character Details

**Motivation:** Prove he made the right call when he disobeyed orders. Earn back his honor — on his own terms.

**Fear:** That the Kaelen Syndicate will call in the debt he owes them, and he will have to choose between his crew and his freedom.

**Crew Role:** Pilot

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "Kel keeps the ship alive. Without them, we would be floating debris. I respect that more than anything."
- *Complicated:* "Reth is too rigid. Rules and honor are fine until they get people killed."

### Secret

*The civilian transport Jace saved was carrying Kaelen Syndicate contraband. The Syndicate considers him an asset — and they have been calling in that debt.*

---

sections.title: 5. DR. MIRA PATEL — The Medic
sections.content:
*"I took an oath to do no harm. I did not take an oath to stand by while others do harm."*

**Ancestry:** Terran
**Community:** Station Rat (Nexus 9)
**Class:** Medic (Splendor and Valor Domains)
**Subclass:** Field Surgeon
**Level:** 1

### Traits

| Trait | Modifier |
|:---|:---:|
| Agility | 0 |
| Strength | -1 |
| Finesse | +1 |
| Instinct | +1 |
| Presence | 0 |
| Knowledge | +2 |

### Statistics

| Stat | Value | Notes |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Hit Points** | 7 | Medic base |
| **Stress Slots** | 5 | Medic base |
| **Evasion** | 10 | Base 9 + Flight Suit bonus (+1) |
| **Armor** | Flight Suit (Light, Tier 1) | Thresholds below; Armor Score 3 |
| **Proficiency** | 1 | Roll 1 damage die |
| **Hope** | 2 | Starting session Hope |

### Damage Thresholds (Armored — Flight Suit)

| Minor | Major | Severe |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 5 | 11 | 20 |

### Starting Experiences

| Experience | Bonus | Source |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| Street Smarts | +2 | Station Rat community |
| Medicine | +1 | Chosen |
| Investigation | +1 | Chosen |

### Domain Card Loadout (2 cards)

| Card | Domain | Type | Effect |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Emergency Triage** | Splendor | Action | Spend 1 Hope. Touch an ally within Melee range. They recover 2 HP. |
| **Shield of Faith** | Valor | Reaction | When an ally within Close range takes a hit, spend 1 Hope. Reduce the severity of the hit by one step. |

### Starting Equipment

- Light Pistol (d6, Close range, Finesse)
- Flight Suit (Light, Tier 1)
- Advanced med-kit (heals 1d6+2 HP, 3 uses), surgical tools, bioscanner
- Station medical credentials, research datapad
- Commlink, standard datapad, 2 Emergency Med-Patches, 3 Ration Packs, utility knife
- 200 Credits

### Ancestry Feature

*Tenacity:* Once per Long Rest, when you fail an action roll, you may immediately reroll it and take the new result.

### Community Feature

*Station Knowledge:* You always know the fastest route between any two points on a station, and you can identify safe houses and bolt-holes without a roll.

### Character Details

**Motivation:** Understand the visions she has been having. Protect her patients. Find out if the Luminary was real.

**Fear:** That the visions are a sign of Eclipse corruption — and that she is already compromised.

**Crew Role:** Medic / Science Officer

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "Zara came to me bleeding and desperate. I helped her without asking questions. That is what doctors do."
- *Complicated:* "Jace is reckless. One day his luck will run out, and I will be the one picking up the pieces."

### Secret

*Mira was contacted by a Luminary. She does not know what it means, but she has been having visions of the Eclipse's return — visions that are becoming more frequent and more terrifying.*

---

sections.title: 6. KEL-7 "KEL" TORVIK — The Engineer
sections.content:
*"Give me a wrench, a power cell, and thirty minutes. I will fix anything. Give me an hour, and I will make it better than new."*

**Ancestry:** Frontier-Born (Kaelen-raised)
**Community:** Frontier Settler (Grew up on a Kaelen salvage vessel)
**Class:** Engineer (Valor and Blade Domains)
**Subclass:** Artificer
**Level:** 1

### Traits

| Trait | Modifier |
|:---|:---:|
| Agility | 0 |
| Strength | +1 |
| Finesse | +1 |
| Instinct | -1 |
| Presence | 0 |
| Knowledge | +2 |

### Statistics

| Stat | Value | Notes |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Hit Points** | 7 | Engineer base |
| **Stress Slots** | 5 | Engineer base |
| **Evasion** | 8 | Base 9 + Marine Hardsuit penalty (-1) |
| **Armor** | Marine Hardsuit (Heavy, Tier 1) | Thresholds below; Armor Score 4 |
| **Proficiency** | 1 | Roll 1 damage die |
| **Hope** | 2 | Starting session Hope |

### Damage Thresholds (Armored — Marine Hardsuit)

| Minor | Major | Severe |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| 7 | 15 | 26 |

### Starting Experiences

| Experience | Bonus | Source |
|:---|:---:|:---|
| Survival | +2 | Frontier Settler community |
| Engineering | +1 | Chosen |
| Technology | +1 | Chosen |

### Domain Card Loadout (2 cards)

| Card | Domain | Type | Effect |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Fortify Position** | Valor | Action | Spend 1 Hope. Create a barricade or reinforce a position. All allies within Very Close range gain +2 to their damage thresholds until the end of the scene or until the barricade is destroyed. |
| **Heavy Swing** | Blade | Action | Make a melee attack with Advantage. On a hit, deal weapon damage +1d6 additional damage. |

### Starting Equipment

- Plasma Cutter (d8+1, Melee, Strength, can cut through bulkheads)
- Mag-Pistol (d6, Close range, Finesse)
- Marine Hardsuit (Heavy, Tier 1) — modified work suit
- Portable toolkit, welding torch, diagnostic scanner
- Salvaged Progenitor component (unknown function)
- Commlink, standard datapad, 2 Emergency Med-Patches, 3 Ration Packs, utility knife
- 200 Credits

### Ancestry Feature

*Resourceful:* Once per Short Rest, you may produce a common, non-weapon item worth 50 Credits or less that you "happened to have."

### Community Feature

*Self-Reliant:* During a Short Rest, you can repair one piece of damaged equipment without tools or an Engineer.

### Character Details

**Motivation:** Understand the Progenitor component they carry. Build something that matters. Maybe even build a home.

**Fear:** That the Progenitor artifact is not a tool — it is a trap. And that by carrying it, they have already sprung it.

**Crew Role:** Engineer / Mechanic

### Bonds

- *Positive:* "Jace trusts me with the ship. That is the highest compliment a pilot can give an engineer."
- *Complicated:* "Reth does not understand machines. He treats the ship like a beast of burden instead of a living system."

### Secret

*The Progenitor component Kel carries is not inert. It has been slowly interfacing with the Dawnrunner's systems, and Kel has noticed the ship responding to their thoughts. They do not know if this is a gift or a trap.*

---

Their damage thresholds are listed with armor equipped. To find their unarmored thresholds, refer to the Class Damage Thresholds table in Book Four. > *"Six strangers. One ship. A station on the edge of the abyss. This is how legends begin — or how epitaphs are written."*

chapters.title: Starter Ship: The Dawnrunner
chapters.content:
> *She is not pretty. She is not fast. But she is yours, and she has never let you down — yet.*

---

sections.title: Ship Profile
sections.content:
The **Dawnrunner** is a modified Terran Coalition corvette, originally designated TC-CV-1187. She was built as a patrol vessel for the Terran Navy's frontier fleet, served for twelve years, was decommissioned after sustaining heavy damage during a border skirmish, and was sold at auction to a Kaelen salvage dealer. After passing through three more owners — each of whom added their own modifications and left their own scars — she ended up in the crew's hands.

The Dawnrunner is a workhorse: reliable, tough, and surprisingly versatile. She is not elegant, and her systems occasionally argue with each other due to the patchwork of Terran, Kaelen, and aftermarket components. But she can take a beating, carry a decent cargo load, and outrun most things her size.

---

sections.title: Ship Statistics
sections.content:
| Attribute | Value |
|:---|:---|
| **Ship Class** | Corvette (Light) |
| **Manufacturer** | Terran Coalition Naval Yards (modified) |
| **Length** | 48 meters |
| **Crew Capacity** | 6–12 (optimal crew: 6) |
| **Passenger Capacity** | 8 (in cramped conditions) |
| **Cargo Capacity** | 40 tons standard; 60 tons with cargo bay modification |
| **Hull Points** | 30 |
| **Armor** | 4 |
| **Evasion** | 12 |
| **Speed** | 3 (Moderate — faster than freighters, slower than fighters) |
| **Hyperspace Rating** | Class 3 (standard — 3 days per sector) |
| **Sensors** | Standard (Near range detection, passive scan) |
| **Signature** | Medium (detectable at Near range by military sensors) |

---

sections.title: Weapons Systems
sections.content:
| Weapon | Damage | Range | Notes |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Twin-Linked Pulse Cannons** | 2d8 | Close–Near | Forward-mounted, requires Gunner action |
| **Point Defense Array** | 1d6 | Close | Automated, grants +2 to resist boarding and missile attacks |
| **Torpedo Launcher** | 3d10 | Near–Far | 4 torpedoes loaded; resupply at station (expensive) |

---

sections.title: Ship Systems
sections.content:
| System | Status | Description |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Engines** | Operational | Terran military-grade sublight drives. Reliable but loud. |
| **Hyperspace Drive** | Operational | Class 3 jump drive. Requires 10 minutes to calculate and spool. |
| **Life Support** | Operational | Supports 12 occupants for 30 days without resupply. |
| **Shields** | Patched | Kaelen aftermarket shield generator. Absorbs first 4 damage per round, then must recharge (1 round). |
| **Sensors** | Operational | Standard sensor suite. Can be boosted by Comms/Ops crew role. |
| **Communications** | Operational | Standard comms array with encrypted channel capability. |
| **Medical Bay** | Basic | A converted storage closet with a surgical table and basic supplies. Grants +1 to medical rolls aboard ship. |
| **Cargo Bay** | Operational | 40-ton capacity. Contains a hidden compartment (DC 16 to detect) installed by a previous owner. |
| **Brig** | Improvised | A reinforced storage room with a magnetic lock. Holds 2 prisoners uncomfortably. |

---

sections.title: Known Quirks
sections.content:
Every ship has personality. The Dawnrunner has more than most.

**The Hum:** The hyperspace drive emits a low, rhythmic hum when the ship is at rest. Long-time crew members find it soothing. New passengers find it unsettling. There is no mechanical explanation.

**Compartment 7:** A small maintenance compartment on the lower deck that is consistently 3 degrees colder than the rest of the ship. Previous owners reported hearing whispers. The current crew has learned to ignore it. (GM Note: This is a narrative hook. It may be connected to the Progenitor component Kel carries, or it may be something else entirely.)

**The Galley Dispenser:** The food synthesizer has a limited menu and an apparent preference. No matter what you order, there is a 1-in-6 chance it produces Kaelen spice porridge instead. The crew has stopped complaining.

**Mismatched Systems:** The Terran nav computer and the Kaelen shield generator occasionally conflict, causing brief system glitches during high-stress maneuvers. When the ship takes a Critical Hit, the GM may rule that one non-essential system temporarily goes offline until the Engineer makes a repair roll.

---

sections.title: Crew Stations
sections.content:
| Station | Location | Crew Role |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Bridge** | Forward upper deck | Captain, Pilot, Comms/Ops |
| **Gunnery Console** | Bridge (port side) | Gunner |
| **Engineering** | Lower deck, aft | Engineer |
| **Medical Bay** | Lower deck, midship | Medic |
| **Cargo Bay** | Lower deck, forward | Accessible to all |
| **Crew Quarters** | Upper deck, midship | 6 bunks, shared common area |
| **Airlock** | Port and starboard, midship | Boarding / EVA access |

---

sections.title: Upgrade Slots
sections.content:
The Dawnrunner has **3 Upgrade Slots** available. Upgrades can be purchased at stations or salvaged from wrecks. Each upgrade occupies one slot.

**Example Upgrades:**

| Upgrade | Cost | Effect |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Enhanced Sensors** | 500 credits | Extend sensor range to Far; +2 to scan rolls |
| **Reinforced Hull Plating** | 800 credits | +4 Hull Points |
| **Stealth Coating** | 1,200 credits | Reduce Signature to Low (detectable only at Close range) |
| **Expanded Cargo Bay** | 400 credits | Increase cargo capacity to 60 tons |
| **Improved Hyperspace Drive** | 1,500 credits | Upgrade to Class 2 (2 days per sector) |
| **Drone Bay** | 600 credits | Launch and control 1 reconnaissance drone |
| **Tractor Beam** | 700 credits | Grapple small objects or disabled ships at Close range |
| **Boarding Ram** | 500 credits | +2 to boarding actions; can force docking with unwilling targets |

---
