chapters.title: Domain Card Quick Reference
chapters.content:
> *A summary of all nine domains, their thematic focus, which classes use them, and how domain cards work in play.*

---

sections.title: How Domain Cards Work
sections.content:
Each class in Nexus 9 has access to two **Domains** — thematic ability pools that define your character's capabilities. Your full collection of available cards is your **Vault**. Before each session, you select a subset of cards from your Vault to form your **Loadout** — the abilities you have ready for immediate use.

---

sections.title: Card Types
sections.content:
| Type | Icon | When Used | Cost |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| **Action** | ⚔ | On your turn, uses your action | Varies (Hope, Stress, or free) |
| **Reaction** | 🛡 | Triggered by a specific event | Varies (Hope, Stress, or free) |
| **Passive** | ✦ | Always active while in your Loadout | None (always on) |

---

sections.title: Card Costs
sections.content:
| Cost | Description |
|:---|:---|
| **Free** | No cost; simply declare use |
| **1 Hope** | Spend 1 Hope from the shared pool |
| **2 Hope** | Spend 2 Hope from the shared pool |
| **1 Stress** | Mark 1 Stress on your character sheet |
| **2 Stress** | Mark 2 Stress on your character sheet |
| **Action** | Uses your action for the turn |

---

sections.title: Loadout Rules
sections.content:
Your maximum Loadout size depends on your level:

| Level | Max Loadout Size |
|:---|:---|
| 1–2 | 4 cards |
| 3–4 | 5 cards |
| 5–7 | 6 cards |
| 8–10 | 7 cards |

You may swap cards in your Loadout during a **Short Rest** (swap 1 card) or a **Long Rest** (rebuild your entire Loadout).

---

sections.title: The Nine Domains
sections.content:
### 1. Arcana (Telepathy and Technomancy)

**Theme:** Psychic power, mind-reading, technomantic manipulation, and forbidden knowledge.

**Used by:** Mystic (Primary), Broker (Secondary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Psychic Probe, Technomantic Surge, Mind Shield |
| 2 | Neural Overload, System Hack, Thought Leech |
| 3+ | Mass Telepathy, Cognitive Override, Technomantic Storm |

---

### 2. Blade (Close Quarters Combat)

**Theme:** Melee mastery, aggressive strikes, and frontline dominance.

**Used by:** Marine (Primary), Engineer (Secondary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Cleaving Strike, Warrior's Stance, Riposte |
| 2 | Whirlwind Attack, Armor Breaker, Relentless Advance |
| 3+ | Executioner's Strike, Unstoppable Charge, Blade Storm |

---

### 3. Bone (Tactics and Evasion)

**Theme:** Battlefield awareness, evasive maneuvers, tactical positioning, and survival instinct.

**Used by:** Pilot (Primary), Marine (Secondary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Evasive Maneuvers, Battle Sense, Tactical Withdrawal |
| 2 | Flanking Fire, Danger Sense, Covering Fire |
| 3+ | Perfect Evasion, Tactical Supremacy, Ghost Run |

---

### 4. Codex (Law, Lore, and Bureaucracy)

**Theme:** Legal expertise, historical knowledge, bureaucratic manipulation, and information warfare.

**Used by:** Envoy (Primary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Bureaucratic Loophole, Historical Precedent, Diplomatic Immunity |
| 2 | Red Tape, Legal Precedent, Intelligence Briefing |
| 3+ | Executive Order, Classified Intel, Political Leverage |

---

### 5. Grace (Diplomacy and Deception)

**Theme:** Persuasion, manipulation, social maneuvering, and reading people.

**Used by:** Envoy (Primary), Operative (Secondary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Silver Tongue, Read the Room, Charming Deflection |
| 2 | Honeyed Words, False Flag, Social Chameleon |
| 3+ | Master Manipulator, Diplomatic Coup, Irresistible Offer |

---

### 6. Midnight (Stealth and Sabotage)

**Theme:** Infiltration, assassination, sabotage, and operating from the shadows.

**Used by:** Operative (Primary), Mystic (Secondary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Pick and Pull, Uncanny Disguise, Midnight Spirit |
| 2 | Darkbind, Saboteur's Touch, Vanishing Act |
| 3+ | Death from Darkness, Ghost Protocol, Perfect Infiltration |

---

### 7. Sage (Survival and Logistics)

**Theme:** Navigation, resource management, wilderness survival, and supply chain expertise.

**Used by:** Broker (Primary), Pilot (Secondary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Wayfinder, Field Repair, Scavenger's Eye |
| 2 | Supply Cache, Astrogation, Emergency Rations |
| 3+ | Master Navigator, Resource Network, Survival Instinct |

---

### 8. Splendor (Medicine and Morale)

**Theme:** Healing, trauma care, psychological support, and crew morale.

**Used by:** Medic (Primary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Emergency Triage, Morale Boost, Steady Hands |
| 2 | Combat Medic, Trauma Surgery, Inspiring Presence |
| 3+ | Miracle Worker, Mass Heal, Unbreakable Spirit |

---

### 9. Valor (Protection and Heavy Weapons)

**Theme:** Defensive tactics, heavy weapons, shielding allies, and holding the line.

**Used by:** Engineer (Primary), Medic (Secondary)

| Level | Sample Cards |
|:---|:---|
| 1 | Shield Wall, Fortify Position, Suppressive Fire |
| 2 | Overwatch, Hardened Defenses, Heavy Barrage |
| 3+ | Impenetrable Defense, Orbital Strike, Last Stand |

---

sections.title: Class Domain Summary
sections.content:
| Class | Primary Domain | Secondary Domain |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **Envoy** | Codex | Grace |
| **Operative** | Midnight | Grace |
| **Pilot** | Bone | Sage |
| **Marine** | Blade | Bone |
| **Engineer** | Valor | Blade |
| **Mystic** | Arcana | Midnight |
| **Broker** | Sage | Arcana |
| **Medic** | Splendor | Valor |

---

sections.title: Domain Overlap Map
sections.content:
Some domains are shared between classes, creating natural synergies and party composition considerations:

| Domain | Classes |
|:---|:---|
| Arcana | Mystic, Broker |
| Blade | Marine, Engineer |
| Bone | Pilot, Marine |
| Codex | Envoy (exclusive) |
| Grace | Envoy, Operative |
| Midnight | Operative, Mystic |
| Sage | Broker, Pilot |
| Splendor | Medic (exclusive) |
| Valor | Engineer, Medic |

**Exclusive Domains:** Codex (Envoy only) and Splendor (Medic only) ensure these classes have unique mechanical identities that cannot be replicated by other classes.

chapters.title: Appendix B: Telepathy Quick Reference

sections.title: The Resonance Index Scale
sections.content:
Telepaths are rated on a 12-point scale (RI-1 to RI-12) by the Mindclave.

| Rating | Capability | Mechanical Equivalent |
|:---|:---|:---|
| **RI-1 - RI-3** | Surface empathy, sensing strong emotions, detecting lies. | Can roll Presence to read a room or detect deception. |
| **RI-4 - RI-6** | Surface thoughts, projecting short messages, mental static. | Can use basic Arcana cards (Level 1-3). |
| **RI-7 - RI-9** | Deep memory extraction, psychic attacks, mind-wipes. | Can use advanced Arcana cards (Level 4-7). |
| **RI-10 - RI-12** | Complete cognitive override, mass illusions, psychic screams. | Can use master Arcana cards (Level 8-10). |

sections.title: Telepathic Resistance
sections.content:
When a telepath attempts to read or manipulate an unwilling target, it triggers a **Psychic Contest**.

1. The telepath makes an Action Roll (usually Presence or Knowledge).
2. The target's Difficulty is their **Evasion + 2** (representing mental fortitude).
3. If the target is also a telepath, they can spend 1 Stress to increase their Difficulty by +2 for that roll.

sections.title: The Cost of Power
sections.content: Pushing telepathic abilities beyond safe limits causes physical trauma. A GM can offer a telepath a **Devil's Bargain**: "You can automatically succeed on this deep scan, but you must mark 2 Stress and take a Minor Scar (Nosebleed/Migraine)."

chapters.content: Telepathy in *Nexus 9* is not magic; it is a forced evolutionary trait that allows a user to perceive and manipulate the quantum states of other minds. While Mystics and Brokers use the Arcana domain to represent their trained abilities, any character with the "Telepath" Experience or a Mindclave background can attempt basic telepathic feats.

chapters.title: Glossary and Lexicon
chapters.content:
> *A comprehensive reference of terms, slang, and nomenclature used throughout Nexus 9: The Fraying Dark.*

---

sections.title: A
sections.content:
**Action Roll** — The core mechanic of the game. Roll 2d12 (one Hope die, one Fear die), add the relevant Trait modifier, and compare to the Difficulty. The higher die determines whether Hope or Fear is generated.

**Adaptive Content** — Under the DPCGL license, original content derived from or based on the Daggerheart System Reference Document that has been translated, altered, or transformed.

**Aethelgard** — A mysterious figure who emerged from a temporal anomaly approximately one thousand years before the campaign present. Aethelgard united the Valari Paths, founded the Vanguard, and led the coalition that drove the Eclipse into hibernation. He vanished after delivering the cryptic warning: *"The cycle will break, or the cycle will break you."*

**Agility** — One of the six Traits. Governs dodging, piloting, acrobatics, and reflexes.

**Arcana Domain** — The domain of telepathy and technomancy. Primary domain of the Mystic, secondary domain of the Broker.

**Armor** — A defensive value that reduces incoming damage before it is applied to Hit Points.

**Aurelian Empire** — A declining hereditary aristocratic monarchy. Once the center of galactic commerce, now characterized by decaying infrastructure, opulent core worlds, and rebellious outer colonies. Known for ornate aesthetics in rich purples, golds, and crimson.

**Aurelia Prime** — Capital world of the Aurelian Empire, located in the Gilded Reach.

---

sections.title: B
sections.content:
**Blade Domain** — The domain of close quarters combat. Primary domain of the Marine, secondary domain of the Engineer.

**Blue Ring** — The diplomatic quarter of Nexus 9 station. Houses embassies, council chambers, and formal reception halls. Heavily secured and politically charged.

**Bone Domain** — The domain of tactics and evasion. Primary domain of the Pilot, secondary domain of the Marine.

**Broker** — One of the eight playable classes. A black-market dealer, information trader, and backroom negotiator. Operates through the Sage and Arcana domains.

**Burnwire** — Station slang for an encrypted, single-use communication channel used by smugglers and intelligence operatives.

---

sections.title: C
sections.content:
**Codex Domain** — The domain of law, lore, and bureaucracy. Primary domain of the Envoy.

**Communities** — A character creation element representing the cultural environment in which a character was raised. Provides mechanical bonuses and narrative hooks.

**Crew** — The collective term for the player characters and their shared ship. Crew creation is a collaborative process during Session Zero.

**Crew Roles** — Specific positions aboard a starship during ship combat: Pilot, Gunner, Engineer, Comms/Ops, Captain, and Marine.

**Cyclical Wars** — The recurring pattern of conflict between the Luminaries and the Eclipse, fought through proxy wars among the Younger Races over the course of a million years.

---

sections.title: D
sections.content:
**Damage Thresholds** — Tiered breakpoints that determine the severity of a successful attack. Minor, Major, Severe, and Critical thresholds are defined per class and level.

**Devil's Bargain** — A GM tool offering a player automatic success on a difficult action in exchange for a significant narrative cost, such as marking Stress or gaining a Scar.

**Difficulty** — The target number for an Action Roll, set by the GM based on the challenge's complexity.

**Director** — A high-ranking leader within the Mindclave hierarchy, each controlling a sector of operations.

**Domain Cards** — Ability cards organized by thematic domains. Each class has access to two domains. Cards are categorized as Action, Reaction, or Passive.

**Downed** — The state a character enters when their Hit Points reach zero. A Downed character is unconscious and dying unless stabilized by an ally.

---

sections.title: E
sections.content:
**Eclipse, The** — One of two remaining Ascended species. The Eclipse believe that true strength is forged through conflict, hardship, and competition. They periodically awaken from hibernation on their hidden world, The Breach, to incite galactic war. Their technology is crystalline and organic, favoring deep blacks, violets, and sickly greens.

**Ascended Species** — Species that were uplifted by the Progenitors billions of years ago and built empires of staggering scope. Most departed the galaxy; the Luminaries and the Eclipse remained.

**Engineer** — One of the eight playable classes. A mechanic, inventor, and systems specialist who keeps the crew alive and the ship running. Operates through the Valor and Blade domains.

**Envoy** — One of the eight playable classes. A diplomat, spy, and manipulator who navigates the treacherous political waters of Nexus 9. Operates through the Codex and Grace domains.

**Evasion** — A defensive value representing a character's ability to avoid attacks entirely. Attackers must meet or exceed a target's Evasion to land a hit.

---

sections.title: F
sections.content:
**Faction Turn** — A GM-facing subsystem that tracks the actions and ambitions of major factions between sessions. Each faction has ratings for Military, Political, and Economic power.

**Fear** — The GM's resource currency, generated when the Fear die rolls higher on an Action Roll. Spent to activate enemy abilities, introduce complications, and escalate threats.

**Finesse** — One of the six Traits. Governs ranged attacks, lockpicking, sleight of hand, and precision work.

**Fraying, The** — The slow unraveling of galactic stability as ancient forces stir, alliances fracture, and the Eclipse's influence spreads. The central dramatic tension of the campaign.

---

sections.title: G
sections.content:
**Gold Ring** — The scientific and medical quarter of Nexus 9 station. Houses research laboratories, xenobiology facilities, and the station hospital.

**Grace Domain** — The domain of diplomacy and deception. Primary domain of the Envoy, secondary domain of the Operative.

**Green Ring** — The commercial and residential quarter of Nexus 9 station. The most densely populated ring, featuring markets, habitation blocks, entertainment venues, and the station's economic heart.

**Triune Assembly** — The tricameral governing council of the Valari, with equal representation from the Warrior, Religious, and Worker Paths.

**Grey Ring** — The industrial and maintenance quarter of Nexus 9 station. Houses engineering bays, recycling plants, power systems, and the station's critical infrastructure. Also contains the entrance to the Warrens.

---

sections.title: H
sections.content:
**Handler** — A mid-ranking Mindclave operative who manages teams of Hounds and oversees telepathic operations in a given sector.

**Hit Points (HP)** — A numerical value representing a character's physical resilience. When HP reaches zero, the character is Downed.

**Hollow, The** — The hidden stronghold world of the Eclipse, where they enter deep hibernation between their cyclical awakenings. Contains buried caches of crystalline warships.

**Hope** — The players' shared resource currency, generated when the Hope die rolls higher on an Action Roll. Spent to activate domain cards, assist allies, resist consequences, and trigger class features.

**Hope Die** — The light-colored d12 rolled as part of every Action Roll. When it rolls higher than the Fear die, 1 Hope is generated.

**Hound** — A specialized Mindclave enforcement agent tasked with hunting unregistered telepaths (rogues).

**Hyperspace** — The method of faster-than-light travel in the Known Galaxy. Ships enter hyperspace through calculated jump points. Travel through hyperspace is not instantaneous and carries risks.

---

sections.title: I
sections.content:
**Imperial Conclave** — The Noble Court that governs the Aurelian Empire alongside the hereditary monarch.

**Instinct** — One of the six Traits. Governs perception, intuition, survival, and reading people.

---

sections.title: K
sections.content:
**Kaelen Syndicate** — A decentralized criminal empire operating through a network of merchant guilds, pirate fleets, and black-market cartels. Their aesthetic is sleek, angular, and predatory, favoring dark metallics, neon accents, and exposed machinery.

**Knowledge** — One of the six Traits. Governs engineering, medicine, history, hacking, and science.

---

sections.title: L
sections.content:
**League of Unaligned Worlds** — A loose coalition of independent systems, minor species, and frontier colonies that refuse to align with any major power. They seek representation and protection but lack military strength.

**Loadout** — The set of domain cards a character has equipped for a given session. Loadouts can be adjusted during downtime between sessions.

**Luminaries, The** — One of two remaining Ascended species. The Luminaries believe in order, structure, and guided evolution. They secretly engineered human telepathy as a weapon against the Eclipse.

---

sections.title: M
sections.content:
**Marine** — One of the eight playable classes. A frontline soldier, boarding specialist, and heavy weapons expert. Operates through the Blade and Bone domains.

**Medic** — One of the eight playable classes. A combat physician, trauma surgeon, and morale anchor. Operates through the Splendor and Valor domains.

**Midnight Domain** — The domain of stealth and sabotage. Primary domain of the Operative, secondary domain of the Mystic.

**Mindclave** — The authoritarian organization within the Terran Coalition that controls all human telepaths. Led by Directors, staffed by Handlers and Hounds.

**Multiclassing** — The optional rule allowing a character to gain features from a second class at higher levels, subject to restrictions.

**Mystic** — One of the eight playable classes. A telepath, technomancer, and wielder of psychic power. Operates through the Arcana and Midnight domains.

---

sections.title: N
sections.content:
**Nexus 9** — The massive five-ringed torus space station that serves as the campaign's primary setting. Built as a diplomatic hub after the Terran-Valari War, it is the ninth attempt at the Nexus Project. Operational for approximately one year at campaign start.

**Nexus 4** — The fourth Nexus station, which was completed and became operational only to vanish without a trace, swallowed by an unexplained temporal anomaly.

---

sections.title: O
sections.content:
**Operative** — One of the eight playable classes. A specialist in stealth, sabotage, and targeted elimination. Operates through the Midnight and Grace domains.

**Orenthal** — The Firstborn of the Progenitors, the first sapient being in the galaxy. Chose to remain when the other Progenitors departed beyond the Rim.

---

sections.title: P
sections.content:
**Resonance Index** — The Mindclave's twelve-point scale (RI-1 to RI-12) for measuring telepathic ability. RI-1–RI-3 covers surface empathy; RI-10–RI-12 represents complete cognitive override.

**Pilot** — One of the eight playable classes. An ace flyer, navigator, and tactical specialist. Operates through the Bone and Sage domains.

**Presence** — One of the six Traits. Governs diplomacy, deception, leadership, and telepathic force.

**Progenitors** — The first sapient species in the galaxy, naturally immortal. They nurtured the Ascended Species before departing beyond the Rim. Only Orenthal remained.

**Psychic Contest** — The opposed roll triggered when a telepath attempts to read or manipulate an unwilling target.

---

sections.title: R
sections.content:
**Red Ring** — The military and security quarter of Nexus 9 station. Houses barracks, armories, the brig, and docking bays for military vessels.

**Rogue Telepath** — An unregistered telepath operating outside Mindclave control. Hunted by Hounds.

---

sections.title: S
sections.content:
**Sage Domain** — The domain of survival and logistics. Primary domain of the Broker, secondary domain of the Pilot.

**Scar** — A permanent narrative consequence gained when a character's Stress track fills completely. Scars represent lasting physical, psychological, or social changes.

**Session Zero** — The collaborative pre-campaign session where players establish tone, safety tools, crew bonds, ship selection, and campaign expectations.

**Siege of Sol** — The climactic battle of the Terran-Valari War, fought in orbit around Earth. The Valari achieved total military victory but surrendered unconditionally upon discovering that human and Valari souls are intrinsically linked.

**Splendor Domain** — The domain of medicine and morale. Primary domain of the Medic.

**Strength** — One of the six Traits. Governs melee attacks, lifting, endurance, and intimidation.

**Stress** — A resource representing mental and emotional strain. Marked when pushing abilities, resisting psychic attacks, or facing trauma. When the Stress track fills, the character gains a Scar.

---

sections.title: T
sections.content:
**Technoseers** — A secretive order of scientists and engineers who worship technology as a path to transcendence. They seek to recover and reverse-engineer Progenitor artifacts.

**Temporal Echo** — The hereditary Aurelian trait that grants brief, unreliable flashes of precognition.

**Terran Coalition** — The human federal republic. Recovering from the Valari War, it is sliding toward military authoritarianism under Mindclave influence.

**Threat Clock** — A GM tool for tracking the progress of major faction agendas. When a clock fills, a significant event occurs (invasion, coup, awakening, etc.).

**Tier** — A grouping of character levels that determines access to equipment, abilities, and domain cards. Tier 1 covers Levels 1–2; Tier 2 covers Levels 3–4; Tier 3 covers Levels 5–7; Tier 4 covers Levels 8–10.

---

sections.title: U
sections.content:
**Umbral Covenant** — A heretical splinter faction of the Technoseers that has begun experimenting with Eclipse technology, believing it holds the key to ultimate power.

---

sections.title: V
sections.content:
**Vael'Kari** — The Path of the Blade of the Valari.

**Vael'Sari** — The Path of the Flame of the Valari.

**Vael'Tari** — The Path of the Root of the Valari.

**Valari** — A technologically superior, deeply spiritual alien species organized into three Paths and governed by the Triune Assembly. Their biology features cranial ridges of crystalline bone and pale luminescent skin.

**Valor Domain** — The domain of protection and heavy weapons. Primary domain of the Engineer, secondary domain of the Medic.

**Vanguard** — A covert, multi-species order founded by Aethelgard, dedicated to watching for the Eclipse's return. Operates in secret across all factions.

**Vault** — The full collection of domain cards available to a character's class. The Loadout is drawn from the Vault.

---

sections.title: W
sections.content:
**Warrens, The** — The lawless, unregulated sub-levels beneath Nexus 9's Grey Ring. A maze of abandoned maintenance corridors, black-market bazaars, and criminal hideouts.

---

sections.title: Y
sections.content:
**Younger Races** — The species that evolved after the Elders departed, including humans, Valari, Aurelians, and Kaelen. They were caught between the Luminaries and the Eclipse in the Cyclical Wars.

---

sections.title: Station Slang
sections.content:
| Term | Meaning |
|:---|:---|
| **Burnwire** | An encrypted, single-use communication channel |
| **Clave-rat** | Derogatory term for a Mindclave loyalist |
| **Dirtside** | Planetside; anywhere that is not a station or ship |
| **Dustwalker** | A frontier explorer or prospector |
| **Fade** | To disappear, especially to avoid Mindclave detection |
| **Ghosting** | Running dark — operating without transponder or ID |
| **Gilt** | Aurelian currency; also slang for anything ostentatious |
| **Greyback** | A worker or maintenance crew member (from the Grey Ring) |
| **Hard burn** | Emergency acceleration; also slang for a desperate gamble |
| **Inkblot** | An Eclipse-touched individual or artifact |
| **Jumpsick** | Nausea and disorientation from hyperspace travel |
| **Rattler** | A Kaelen Syndicate enforcer |
| **Ringside** | On-station; the opposite of dirtside |
| **Scab** | A patch repair on a ship hull; also slang for a temporary fix |
| **Skinwalker** | A telepath who can alter their appearance |
| **Spinward / Rimward** | Directional terms on the station (toward the spin axis / toward the outer hull) |
| **Voidborn** | Someone born and raised in space, never having lived on a planet |
| **Warrens rat** | A resident of the Warrens sub-levels |

sections.title: Product Information
sections.content:
**Nexus 9: The Fraying Dark**
A cosmic-opera tabletop roleplaying game.

This product is an independent creation and is not affiliated with, sponsored by, or endorsed by Darrington Press or Critical Role.

---

sections.title: Compatibility Statement
sections.content:
This product is compatible with Daggerheart and is designed for use with the Daggerheart system. "Daggerheart" is a trademark of Darrington Press, LLC.

---

sections.title: License Attribution
sections.content:
This work includes Adaptive Content derived from the Daggerheart System Reference Document 1.0, which is Public Game Content published by Darrington Press, LLC.

The Daggerheart System Reference Document 1.0 is copyright Darrington Press, LLC, and is used under the terms of the Darrington Press Community Gaming License (DPCGL).

The original Public Game Content has been modified. The mechanical systems, stat block formats, domain structures, class frameworks, and action economy presented in this work are adaptations of the systems described in the Daggerheart SRD. All setting material, characters, factions, locations, narrative content, artwork, and original game content in this work are the creation of the authors and are not part of the Public Game Content.

The full text of the Daggerheart System Reference Document is available at: https://darringtonpress.com/daggerheart-srd

This content is licensed under the Darrington Press Community Gaming License. The full text of the DPCGL is available at: https://darringtonpress.com/license

---

sections.title: What This Means
sections.content:
- The game mechanics in this book (the 2d12 Hope/Fear system, action economy, domain card structure, damage thresholds, and character advancement framework) are adapted from the publicly available Daggerheart SRD.
- All original content — including the Nexus 9 setting, all factions, characters, starships, weapons, locations, campaign narrative, artwork, and supplementary game material — is the original work of the authors.
- This product is not official Daggerheart content. It is a third-party work created under the terms of the DPCGL.

sections.content:
Nexus 9: The Fraying Dark and all original setting material, characters, factions, locations, starships, artwork, and narrative content contained herein are the intellectual property of the authors. All rights reserved.

sections.title: Art and Illustration Credits
sections.content:
All illustrations in this work were generated using AI image generation tools and are used in accordance with applicable terms of service. The art direction, prompts, curation, and integration of all visual assets were performed by the authors.

---

sections.title: Safety and Content Notice
sections.content:
This game contains themes of political conflict, war, espionage, psychic violation, authoritarian surveillance, personal sacrifice, and moral compromise. While the tone is ultimately hopeful, individual sessions may explore intense subject matter. The Session Zero chapter includes recommended safety tools (Lines and Veils, X-Card, Open Door Policy) that should be established before play begins.

---

sections.title: Acknowledgments
sections.content:
This game was inspired by the tradition of cosmic-opera storytelling — tales of civilizations in collision, ancient mysteries, and ordinary people caught in extraordinary circumstances. We are grateful to the creators of the Daggerheart system for building a flexible, story-forward mechanical foundation and for establishing a community license that enables independent creators to build upon it.

---

sections.title: Version
sections.content:
First Digital Edition — 2025

---

> *"The stars do not care about our treaties, our wars, or our prayers. But we care about each other. That is enough."*
